reference: AMA CPT Modifier Guidelines; CMS-1500 Box 24D; CMS Claims Processing Manual Chapter 12

description: Secondary AMA CPT modifier when multiple modifiers are required for the procedure code. Supports complex claim scenarios such as assistant surgeon (80), teaching physician (GC), or anesthesia qualifiers.
reference: AMA CPT Modifier Guidelines; CMS-1500 Box 24D; CMS Claims Processing Manual Chapter 12

description: Timestamp when this procedure event record was first created in the source EHR system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for data governance, audit trail, and HIPAA audit log requirements.
reference: HIPAA §164.312(b) Audit Controls; HITRUST CSF Control 09.aa; ISO 27001 A.12.4

description: Total elapsed time in minutes from procedure start to procedure end. Derived from start and end timestamps at source and stored for OR utilization analytics, anesthesia billing, and surgical quality benchmarking.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; AHRQ OR Utilization Metrics

description: Estimated blood loss in milliliters recorded by the surgical team during the procedure. Used for post-operative care planning, transfusion trigger assessment, and surgical quality benchmarking (ACS NSQIP).
reference: ACS NSQIP; Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; AABB Transfusion Guidelines

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases 10th Revision Procedure Coding System (ICD-10-PCS) Code
description: ICD-10-PCS code for inpatient procedures, used on UB-04 institutional claims for DRG grouping and inpatient quality reporting. Required for CMS inpatient prospective payment system (IPPS) reimbursement.
reference: WHO ICD-10-PCS; CMS IPPS; UB-04 FL 74; 3M Health Information Systems Grouping

description: Body side on which the procedure was performed. Required for surgical safety verification (Universal Protocol), correct-site surgery compliance, and ICD-10-PCS/CPT modifier application.
reference: The Joint Commission Universal Protocol UP.01.01.01; AMA CPT Modifier Guidelines; Epic OpTime

description: Clinical urgency classification of the procedure at time of scheduling or ordering. Drives OR scheduling queue prioritization, resource allocation, and EMTALA compliance tracking for emergent cases.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; EMTALA (Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act); The Joint Commission EC.02.01.01

description: High-level clinical category classifying the nature of the procedure. Used for service-line analytics, OR scheduling resource allocation, and population health stratification.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; AHRQ Clinical Classification Software (CCS)

description: Calendar date on which the procedure was performed. Used as the primary date dimension for reporting, HEDIS measure denominator logic, and claim date-of-service on CMS-1500 and UB-04 forms.
reference: CMS-1500 Box 24A; UB-04 FL 45; HEDIS Technical Specifications; AMA CPT Guidelines

description: Date and time when the procedure was completed (wound closure for surgical cases, or final intervention for non-surgical). Used with start time to calculate procedure duration for OR scheduling, staffing, and quality metrics.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; HL7 FHIR R4 Procedure.performed[x]

description: Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this procedure event, assigned by the source EHR system (e.g., Epic OpTime case number or Cerner SurgiNet procedure ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and charge capture reference.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; HL7 FHIR R4 Procedure.identifier

description: Date and time when the procedure was initiated (knife-to-skin for surgical cases, or first intervention for non-surgical). Principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this transaction. Used for OR utilization, LOS calculation, and quality measure timing.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; HL7 FHIR R4 Procedure.performed[x]; The Joint Commission PC.01.02.01

description: Current lifecycle state of the procedure event per HL7 FHIR Procedure.status value set. Drives revenue cycle charge capture eligibility and quality measure inclusion/exclusion logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: performed|in-progress|cancelled|not-done|on-hold|entered-in-error — promote to reference product]
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Procedure.status; Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet

description: Operational classification of the procedure within the facility's CDM (Charge Description Master), such as 'OR Case', 'Bedside Procedure', 'Interventional Radiology', 'Endoscopy', 'Cardiac Cath'. Drives charge routing and scheduling resource assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: or_case|bedside|interventional_radiology|endoscopy|cardiac_cath|ambulatory_surgery|labor_delivery — promote to reference product]
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; CDM (Charge Description Master); CMS Outpatient Prospective Payment System (OPPS)

description: CMS physician work Relative Value Unit (RVU) assigned to the CPT code for this procedure. Used for provider productivity measurement, compensation modeling, and MIPS performance reporting.
reference: CMS Physician Fee Schedule (RVU); MACRA/MIPS; AMA RVU Update Committee (RUC); Epic Resolute PB

business_glossary_term: Scheduled Procedure Start Date and Time
description: Originally scheduled date and time for the procedure. Compared against actual start time to measure on-time starts, OR schedule adherence, and first-case delay metrics.
reference: Epic Cadence; Cerner SurgiNet; AHRQ OR Efficiency Metrics

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this procedure event record was sourced. Used for data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and audit trail in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Epic OpTime; Epic ClinDoc; Cerner SurgiNet; Cerner PowerChart; MEDITECH Expanse; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001

description: Native primary key or unique identifier of this procedure record in the originating source system (e.g., Epic OpTime case ID, Cerner SurgiNet procedure ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational EHR.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001 Data Lineage

description: Indicates whether a tissue specimen or pathology sample was collected during the procedure. Triggers downstream laboratory/pathology order workflow and chain-of-custody documentation.
reference: Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; CAP (College of American Pathologists) Specimen Handling Guidelines; The Joint Commission NPSG.01.01.01

description: Indicates whether the pre-procedure surgical time-out (Universal Protocol) was completed and documented. Mandatory for Joint Commission accreditation and CMS Conditions of Participation to prevent wrong-site, wrong-procedure, wrong-patient events.
reference: The Joint Commission Universal Protocol UP.01.03.01; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.51; AHRQ Patient Safety

description: FDA-assigned Unique Device Identifier (UDI) for the primary implant or medical device used during the procedure. Required for FDA device tracking, recall management, and 21st Century Cures Act compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830 UDI Rule; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01; ONC 21st Century Cures Act

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this procedure event record in the source EHR system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change data capture (CDC), data quality monitoring, and HIPAA audit trail compliance.
reference: HIPAA §164.312(b) Audit Controls; HITRUST CSF Control 09.aa; ISO 27001 A.12.4; HL7 FHIR R4 Meta.lastUpdated

description: CDC/ACS wound classification (Clean, Clean-Contaminated, Contaminated, Dirty/Infected) assigned at time of procedure. Used for SSI (Surgical Site Infection) surveillance, HAI reporting, and infection control quality metrics.
reference: CDC SSI Surveillance Criteria; ACS NSQIP; The Joint Commission NPSG.07.05.01; CMS HAI Reporting

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each vital sign measurement record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the vital_sign data product.

Reference to the healthcare facility (hospital, clinic, outpatient center) where the vital sign measurement was taken. Supports multi-facility analytics, population health management, and facility-level quality reporting.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation; The Joint Commission; HL7 FHIR R4 Location

business_glossary_term: Vital Sign Clinical Order Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Vital signs fulfill monitoring orders. Real workflow: order for q4h vitals, nurse documents BP/HR. Essential for order compliance tracking, nursing workload analysis, sepsis protocol adherence, and re
Reference to the clinician (nurse, physician, or allied health professional) who recorded or validated the vital sign measurement. Supports accountability and CDI workflows.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.performer; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01

description: Reference to the medical device (bedside monitor, pulse oximeter, thermometer, sphygmomanometer) used to capture the measurement. Supports device integration pipeline traceability and device-level quality audits.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.device; FDA 21 CFR Part 820 Medical Device Quality

description: Foreign key linking to reference.loinc_code. Business justification: Vital sign observation standardization for clinical surveillance, early warning scores, quality measure calculation, and interoperable data exchange. Clinical systems use LOINC codes to aggregate vita
Reference to the patient for whom the vital sign measurement was recorded. Links to the master patient record in the Patient domain.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.subject; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514

description: Reference to the prior version of this vital sign record when observation_status is 'amended' or 'corrected'. Enables amendment chain traceability and supports audit requirements for clinical documentation integrity.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.derivedFrom; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.526

description: Foreign key linking to reference.snomed_concept. Business justification: SNOMED-CT finding codes for vital signs are required for FHIR Observation resource interoperability, clinical decision support rules, and quality measure reporting. snomed_finding_code is a denormaliz

Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: Unit-level acuity monitoring, nurse-to-patient ratio compliance reporting, and early warning score dashboards require knowing which clinical unit (ICU, step-down, ED) a vital sign was recorded in. Cha
Reference to the clinical encounter (inpatient admission, ED visit, outpatient visit) during which the vital sign was measured. Supports encounter-level trending and early warning score calculations.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.encounter; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Interpretation flag indicating whether the measured value falls within, below, or above the reference range. 'critical_low' and 'critical_high' trigger immediate clinical notification per facility policy. Supports sepsis screening, EWS alerting, and clinical deterioration detection workflows.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.interpretation; The Joint Commission NPSG.02.03.01 Critical Values; AHRQ

description: The clinical or administrative reason provided when a vital sign record is amended, corrected, or marked as entered-in-error (e.g., 'transcription error', 'wrong patient', 'device malfunction', 'duplicate entry'). Populated only when observation_status is 'amended', 'corrected', or 'entered-in-error'. Supports CDI audit trails and data quality governance.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.note; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.526

description: The anatomical body site where the measurement was taken (e.g., 'left arm', 'right arm', 'finger', 'ear', 'forehead', 'toe'). Encoded using SNOMED CT body structure codes. Relevant for blood pressure laterality, temperature site, and SpO2 probe placement. Affects clinical interpretation.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.bodySite; SNOMED CT Body Structure Hierarchy

description: The clinical care unit or department where the patient was located when the vital sign was measured (e.g., 'ICU', 'ED', 'Medical/Surgical', 'OR', 'PACU', 'Step-Down'). Supports unit-level benchmarking, staffing analytics, and HAI surveillance.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; The Joint Commission; Epic ADT Module

description: Free-text clinical annotation or comment entered by the recording clinician to provide context for the vital sign measurement (e.g., 'patient anxious during measurement', 'cuff size large', 'post-exercise reading'). Supports CDI workflows and clinical interpretation.
reference: The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01; Epic ClinDoc Flowsheet Comment; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514

description: The date and time when this vital sign record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Represents the ETL ingestion audit timestamp, distinct from measurement_timestamp (when the vital was taken) and documented_timestamp (when it was entered in the EHR). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HITRUST CSF Control 09.ab; ISO 27001 A.12.4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312

description: The date and time when the vital sign measurement was entered or finalized in the EHR system (Epic ClinDoc flowsheet or Cerner PowerChart). May differ from measurement_timestamp when documentation is retrospective. Used for CDI audit trails and documentation timeliness reporting.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.issued; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01; Epic ClinDoc Flowsheet Audit

description: The numeric score contribution of this individual vital sign measurement to the composite Early Warning Score (EWS), Modified Early Warning Score (MEWS), or National Early Warning Score (NEWS/NEWS2). Scored 0–3 per parameter per NEWS2 methodology. Supports real-time clinical deterioration detection and rapid response team activation.
reference: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2 Score; AHRQ Sepsis Screening; CMS SEP-1 Bundle

description: Identifies the specific early warning scoring system used to derive the ews_score_contribution for this vital sign (e.g., NEWS2 for adult inpatients, PEWS for pediatric patients, MEWS for general ward use). Allows multi-system EWS coexistence in the same dataset.
reference: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2; AHRQ; The Joint Commission Rapid Response Standards

description: The source system flowsheet row identifier from Epic ClinDoc or Cerner PowerChart that uniquely identifies the flowsheet template row from which this vital sign was extracted. Supports ETL lineage, source system reconciliation, and re-ingestion deduplication.
reference: Epic ClinDoc Flowsheet Architecture; Cerner PowerChart FlowSheet; HL7 FHIR Provenance Resource

description: Identifies which component of the Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) is represented when observation_type is 'gcs_total' or a GCS sub-score (Eye Opening E1-E4, Verbal Response V1-V5, Motor Response M1-M6, or Total 3-15). Null for non-GCS vital sign observations. Supports neurological assessment trending and ICU severity scoring.
reference: Glasgow Coma Scale (Teasdale & Jennett 1974); HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.component; AHRQ

description: Indicates whether the vital sign value was self-reported by the patient (True), such as home blood pressure readings, pain scores, or weight from a patient portal or remote monitoring program. Distinguishes patient-generated health data (PGHD) from clinician-measured values for analytics and clinical decision support.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.performer; ONC 21st Century Cures Act; CMS Interoperability Rule

description: Indicates whether the vital sign measurement was automatically captured from a continuous bedside telemetry or patient monitoring device (True) versus manually entered by a clinician (False). Supports differentiation of high-frequency device-generated data from manually documented assessments in time-series analytics.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.method; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; ISO 80001

description: The clinical method used to obtain the vital sign measurement (e.g., 'auscultation' for BP, 'pulse oximetry' for SpO2, 'tympanic' or 'oral' or 'rectal' or 'axillary' for temperature, 'Doppler' for BP). Encoded using SNOMED CT method codes where applicable. Affects clinical interpretation and normal range thresholds.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.method; SNOMED CT; Regenstrief LOINC Method Axis

description: The precise date and time when the vital sign measurement was physically taken from the patient. This is the principal clinical event time, distinct from the documentation timestamp. Critical for time-series trending, EWS/MEWS/NEWS calculations, and sepsis screening time-to-treatment metrics. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.effectiveDateTime; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01; CMS Sepsis Bundle SEP-1

description: The Medical Record Number assigned to the patient by the facility Master Patient Index (MPI). Retained on the vital sign record to support direct clinical lookup without requiring a join to the patient master in operational workflows.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HL7 FHIR R4 Patient.identifier; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.24

description: The principal quantitative measured value of the vital sign observation (e.g., 120 for systolic BP in mmHg, 98.6 for temperature in °F, 98 for SpO2 in %). Stored as a decimal to accommodate fractional values such as temperature and BMI. Null when the observation is non-numeric (e.g., qualitative pain descriptors).
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.valueQuantity; Regenstrief LOINC; AHRQ Clinical Quality Measures

description: Current lifecycle status of the vital sign observation record per HL7 FHIR Observation status value set. 'final' indicates a verified measurement; 'amended' or 'corrected' indicates a post-documentation change; 'entered-in-error' flags records to be excluded from clinical calculations. Supports data quality filtering for EWS and sepsis screening. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: registered|preliminary|final|amended|corrected|cancelled|entered-in-error — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.status; The Joint Commission RC.02.01.01

description: Standardized enumeration of the vital sign observation category captured in this record. Each row represents a single atomic observation. Supports time-series analytics, EWS/MEWS/NEWS scoring, and sepsis screening. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood_pressure_systolic|blood_pressure_diastolic|heart_rate|respiratory_rate|temperature|spo2|height|weight|bmi|pain_score|gcs_total — promote to reference product]
reference: HL7 FHIR US Core Vital Signs Profile; LOINC Panel 85353-1 Vital Signs Panel

description: The supplemental oxygen delivery method in use at the time of SpO2 or respiratory rate measurement (e.g., room air, nasal cannula, simple mask, non-rebreather mask, high-flow nasal cannula, mechanical ventilator). Null for non-respiratory vital signs. Critical context for SpO2 interpretation and NEWS2 scoring (Scale 1 vs Scale 2).
reference: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2 Score; HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.component; CMS SEP-1

description: The specific pain assessment scale used when observation_type is 'pain_score' (e.g., Numeric Rating Scale 0-10, Visual Analog Scale, Wong-Baker FACES, FLACC for pediatric/non-verbal, CPOT for critically ill). Null for non-pain vital sign observations. Required for accurate pain score interpretation per Joint Commission pain management standards.
reference: The Joint Commission Pain Management Standards; AHRQ Pain Assessment Tools; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13

description: The patient's body position at the time of the vital sign measurement (e.g., sitting, standing, supine). Particularly relevant for blood pressure and orthostatic vital sign assessments. Encoded using SNOMED CT observable entity codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sitting|standing|supine|prone|left_lateral|right_lateral|semi-recumbent — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.component; SNOMED CT; AHA Blood Pressure Measurement Guidelines

description: The upper bound of the normal reference range for this vital sign observation type, in the same unit of measure as numeric_value. Used for clinical alerting, EWS/MEWS/NEWS scoring, and out-of-range flagging.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.referenceRange.high; AHRQ Clinical Decision Support; NEWS2 Score Guidelines

description: The lower bound of the normal reference range for this vital sign observation type, in the same unit of measure as numeric_value. Used for clinical alerting, EWS/MEWS/NEWS scoring, and out-of-range flagging. Age- and condition-adjusted ranges may differ from population norms.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.referenceRange.low; AHRQ Clinical Decision Support; NEWS2 Score Guidelines

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this vital sign measurement was ingested (e.g., Epic ClinDoc flowsheet, Cerner PowerChart, MEDITECH Expanse, bedside monitoring device integration, wearable device feed). Supports data lineage, ETL audit, and multi-source reconciliation.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Provenance; HITRUST CSF Control 09.ab; ISO 27001 A.12.4

description: The native primary key or observation identifier from the originating source system (Epic, Cerner, MEDITECH, or device integration platform). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer record and the operational EHR record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.identifier; HITRUST CSF; CMS Interoperability Rule

description: The flow rate of supplemental oxygen in liters per minute (L/min) being administered to the patient at the time of the SpO2 or respiratory vital sign measurement. Null when oxygen_delivery_method is 'room_air' or for non-respiratory observations. Supports NEWS2 Scale 2 scoring and respiratory therapy documentation.
reference: Royal College of Physicians NEWS2 Score; HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.component; CMS SEP-1

description: Free-text or coded string value for vital sign observations that are qualitative or semi-quantitative (e.g., pain descriptor 'mild', 'moderate', 'severe'; GCS verbal response 'oriented'; SpO2 trend 'improving'). Null when numeric_value is populated.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.valueString; Epic ClinDoc Flowsheet

description: The unit of measure for the numeric vital sign value using UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure) notation (e.g., 'mm[Hg]' for blood pressure, '/min' for heart/respiratory rate, 'Cel' or '[degF]' for temperature, '%' for SpO2, 'cm' or '[in_i]' for height, 'kg' or '[lb_av]' for weight, 'kg/m2' for BMI, '{score}' for pain/GCS).
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Observation.valueQuantity.unit; UCUM Standard; Regenstrief LOINC

description: The date and time when this vital sign record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer (e.g., due to an amendment, correction, or status change propagated from the source EHR). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HITRUST CSF Control 09.ab; ISO 27001 A.12.4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the advance directive record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); Epic Advance Care Planning Module

description: Reference to the healthcare facility (hospital, clinic, or outpatient center) where the advance directive was documented or verified. Supports multi-facility enterprise reporting and care transition workflows.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation; The Joint Commission (TJC) LD.04.01.01; Epic Advance Care Planning Module

description: Reference to the clinician or provider who documented or verified the advance directive in the Electronic Health Record (EHR). Supports audit and accountability requirements.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); The Joint Commission (TJC) RC.02.01.01; Epic ClinDoc

description: Reference to the patient for whom this advance directive was documented. Links to the patient master record.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); Epic ClinDoc

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.note. Business justification: Advance directives in the EHR are typically documented through a clinical note authored by the attending physician or care coordinator (e.g., a Goals of Care note, a DNR discussion note, or an ACP not

Foreign key linking to reference.loinc_code. Business justification: Advance directive document types are classified using LOINC document ontology codes (e.g., LOINC 75320-2 Advance directive) for FHIR Consent/DocumentReference resources, HIE document exchange, and reg

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Advance directive management is governed by organizational policies implementing state/federal regulations (Patient Self-Determination Act). Healthcare organizations must link advance directive record

Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Advance directives constrain order placement. Real workflow: DNR status prevents code blue orders. Essential for end-of-life care compliance, CPOE decision support, ethics committee review, and regula

Foreign key linking to reference.snomed_concept. Business justification: Advance directive types and code status preferences (e.g., DNR, POLST) are SNOMED-CT coded for FHIR Consent resource interoperability and regulatory compliance reporting. Healthcare domain experts exp

Reference to the newer advance directive record that supersedes this one. Null if this directive has not been superseded. Enables directive version chain tracking and ensures the most current directive is applied in care decisions.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); HL7 FHIR R4 Consent.status; Epic Advance Care Planning Module

description: Reference to the clinical encounter during which the advance directive was documented or verified. May be null if documented outside an encounter context.
reference: Epic ClinDoc; HL7 FHIR R4 Consent Resource

description: Patient's documented preference regarding artificially administered nutrition and hydration (e.g., feeding tubes, IV fluids) as specified in the advance directive or POLST/MOLST. Critical for end-of-life care planning.
reference: POLST National Paradigm; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13

description: The result of the formal patient decision-making capacity assessment conducted at the time of advance directive documentation. Determines whether the patient could legally execute or modify the directive.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.02.01; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA)

description: Free-text clinical notes or additional instructions documented by the provider regarding the patient's advance directive, including nuances, patient-expressed wishes, or contextual information not captured in structured fields.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) RC.02.01.01; HIPAA 45 CFR §164; Epic ClinDoc

description: The patient's current resuscitation code status as documented in the advance directive. Full Code = all resuscitative measures; DNR = Do Not Resuscitate; DNR/DNI = Do Not Resuscitate / Do Not Intubate; Comfort Care = palliative measures only; Limited Interventions = selective resuscitative measures as specified.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.05.01; EMTALA; Epic ClinDoc

description: The date and time when this advance directive record was first created in the lakehouse data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164; ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; The Joint Commission (TJC) RC.01.02.01

description: Current lifecycle status of the advance directive document. Active = currently in force; Revoked = patient has withdrawn the directive; Superseded = replaced by a newer directive; Expired = past the expiration date; Pending Verification = documented but not yet clinically verified.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); HL7 FHIR R4 Consent.status; Epic Advance Care Planning Module

value_regex: DNR|POLST|MOLST|Living Will|Healthcare Power of Attorney|Comfort Care Order
description: Classification of the advance directive document type. DNR = Do Not Resuscitate; POLST = Physician Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment; MOLST = Medical Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment; Living Will = written legal document; Healthcare Power of Attorney = proxy designation; Comfort Care Order = palliative/comfort-focused care order.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.05.01; EMTALA

value_regex: EHR Scanned|Patient Holds Original|Family Holds Copy|Registry|Attorney on File
description: Indicates where the physical or electronic copy of the advance directive document is stored or held. Supports retrieval during care transitions and emergency situations.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.05.01; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; Epic Advance Care Planning Module

description: The date and time when the advance directive was first entered or documented in the Electronic Health Record (EHR). Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this record's creation in the clinical system.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) RC.01.02.01; HIPAA 45 CFR §164; Epic ClinDoc Audit Trail

description: The date on which the advance directive becomes legally and clinically effective. Used to determine whether the directive is currently binding for care decisions.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; HL7 FHIR R4 Consent.dateTime

description: Indicates whether an ethics committee consultation was requested in connection with this advance directive. Supports quality reporting, compliance tracking, and clinical ethics workflows.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.05.01; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; AHRQ Patient Safety Standards

description: The date on which the advance directive expires and is no longer legally or clinically binding. Null if the directive has no defined expiration (open-ended). Supports automated status transitions to 'expired'.
reference: Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); State Departments of Health — Facility Licensing; HL7 FHIR R4 Consent.provision.period

business_glossary_term: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) Consent Resource ID
description: The FHIR Consent resource identifier corresponding to this advance directive, enabling interoperability with Health Information Exchange (HIE) platforms and FHIR-compliant systems.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Consent Resource; ONC 21st Century Cures Act Interoperability Rule; CMS Interoperability and Patient Access Final Rule

description: Indicates whether the patient is currently enrolled in a hospice program at the time of advance directive documentation. Relevant for care plan alignment and CMS hospice benefit compliance.
reference: CMS Medicare Hospice Benefit §418; The Joint Commission (TJC) PC.02.03.01; NCQA HEDIS

value_regex: Accept Hospitalization|Avoid Hospitalization|Comfort Care Only|No Preference Stated
description: Patient's documented preference regarding hospitalization and transfer to acute care settings as specified in the POLST/MOLST. Guides care transitions and Emergency Department (ED) triage decisions.
reference: POLST National Paradigm; EMTALA; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA)

description: Indicates whether a language interpreter was used during the advance directive discussion and documentation process. Supports compliance with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act and CMS language access requirements.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; Title VI Civil Rights Act; The Joint Commission (TJC) RC.02.01.01; HHS Language Access Standards

description: Patient's documented preference regarding life-sustaining treatment as captured in the POLST/MOLST or living will. Full Treatment = all medically appropriate interventions; Selective Treatment = specific interventions as detailed; Comfort Measures Only = palliative and comfort-focused care only. Core clinical decision support field.
reference: POLST National Paradigm; CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.05.01

description: Patient's documented preference regarding mechanical ventilation and intubation as specified in the advance directive or POLST/MOLST. Directly informs DNR/DNI code status decisions.
reference: POLST National Paradigm; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA); CMS Conditions of Participation §482.13; The Joint Commission (TJC) RI.01.05.01

description: The Medical Record Number (MRN) assigned to the patient in the source Electronic Health Record (EHR) system. Supports cross-system patient matching via the Master Patient Index (MPI).
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164; CMS Conditions of Participation; Epic MPI

description: Indicates whether the advance directive document has been notarized. Some state jurisdictions require notarization for legal validity of living wills and healthcare power of attorney designations.
reference: State Departments of Health — Facility Licensing; Patient Self-Determination Act (PSDA)

description: Indicates whether the OR is equipped and staffed to support pediatric surgical cases. Requires specialized equipment (pediatric anesthesia circuits, warming devices, smaller instruments) and trained perioperative staff.
reference: AORN Pediatric Perioperative Guidelines, Pediatric Surgery Program Standards

description: Indicates whether the OR maintains positive air pressure relative to adjacent corridors and support spaces. Positive pressure prevents contaminated air from entering the sterile field. Required for most ORs; negative pressure is used only for airborne isolation procedures.
reference: CDC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, ASHRAE Standard 170

description: Indicates whether the OR suite is configured and equipped to support robotic-assisted surgical systems (e.g., da Vinci Surgical System). Requires additional space, power, and structural support.
reference: AORN Guidelines for Perioperative Practice, Robotic Surgery Program Standards

description: Ceiling height of the operating room in feet. Critical for surgical light placement, boom configuration, and laminar airflow system design.
reference: Facility Guidelines Institute (FGI) Guidelines for Design and Construction of Hospitals

description: Physical length dimension of the operating room in feet. Used for space planning, equipment placement, and compliance with facility design standards.
reference: Facility Guidelines Institute (FGI) Guidelines for Design and Construction of Hospitals

description: Physical width dimension of the operating room in feet. Minimum dimensions are regulated for different OR types to ensure safe equipment access and staff movement.
reference: Facility Guidelines Institute (FGI) Guidelines for Design and Construction of Hospitals

description: Recurring time block reserved for preventive maintenance, deep cleaning, and equipment calibration (e.g., 'Every Sunday 6:00 AM - 12:00 PM', 'First Monday of month'). OR is unavailable for surgical scheduling during this window.
reference: SAP PM Preventive Maintenance Schedule, Biomedical Engineering Maintenance Standards

description: Date and time when the current operational status became effective. Used to track OR availability history and calculate downtime metrics for capacity management.
reference: Epic OpTime OR Availability, Cerner SurgiNet Room Status

description: Coded reason for the current operational status (e.g., 'EQUIP_FAIL' for equipment failure, 'STAFF_SHORT' for staffing shortage, 'SCHED_MAINT' for scheduled maintenance, 'RENO' for renovation). Supports root cause analysis of OR unavailability.

Model or type of the primary surgical table installed in the OR (e.g., general surgery table, orthopedic fracture table, neurosurgery head frame table, robotic surgery table). Table type determines compatible procedures and patient positioning capabilities.
reference: Biomedical Engineering Equipment Standards, AORN Positioning Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the OR has integrated video capture, recording, and display systems for endoscopic, laparoscopic, or robotic surgery. Supports surgical education, quality review, and telemedicine consultation.
reference: AORN Guidelines for Perioperative Practice, PACS Integration Standards

description: Unique identifier for the room. Primary key for the room entity.
Reference to the parent facility or hospital where this room is located. Links to the facility master data.
Reference to the parent unit or department within the facility where this room is located. Links to the unit master data.
Current accreditation status of the room by relevant accrediting bodies (e.g., The Joint Commission, AAAHC). Impacts operational authorization and reimbursement eligibility.
Indicates whether the room is currently active and available for use in operational systems. Supports soft-delete patterns and historical record retention.

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the room meets ADA accessibility requirements for patients and staff with disabilities. Required for regulatory compliance and patient access.
Indicates whether the room is equipped and structurally reinforced to accommodate bariatric patients (typically over 350 lbs). Includes specialized beds, lifts, and wider doorways.
Number of patient beds or stretchers that can be accommodated in the room. Used for bed management, capacity planning, and CMS reporting.
Description of ceiling-mounted equipment boom configuration in the room (e.g., single boom, dual boom, articulating arm). Relevant for OR and ICU rooms with suspended equipment.
Timestamp when the room record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Date from which the current room configuration, assignment, or status became effective. Supports temporal tracking and historical analysis.
Date until which the current room configuration, assignment, or status is valid. Null indicates current/active record. Supports temporal tracking and historical analysis.
Indicates whether the room is connected to emergency backup power systems. Critical for life-safety equipment in ORs, ICUs, and emergency departments.
Number of hand hygiene stations (sinks or alcohol-based hand rub dispensers) in the room. Critical for infection prevention compliance and Joint Commission accreditation.
reference: WHO Hand Hygiene Guidelines, The Joint Commission Standards

business_glossary_term: Heating Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Air Exchange Rate
description: Number of complete air changes per hour (ACH) provided by the HVAC system. Critical for infection control, OR sterility, and regulatory compliance per ASHRAE 170.
reference: ASHRAE 170 Ventilation of Health Care Facilities

description: Indicates whether the room has integrated imaging equipment (e.g., C-arm, fluoroscopy, intraoperative CT/MRI). Common in hybrid ORs and interventional procedure rooms.
Indicates whether the room is equipped and certified for isolation of infectious patients. Critical for infection prevention and control workflows.
Date of the most recent terminal or deep cleaning of the room. Used for infection prevention tracking, environmental services scheduling, and outbreak investigation.
Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection of the room. Used for compliance tracking and accreditation readiness.
reference: The Joint Commission Standards, State Health Department Regulations

description: Timestamp when the room record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.
Indicates whether the room space is owned by the organization, leased from a third party, shared with another entity, or contracted. Impacts financial reporting and cost allocation.
State or regulatory license number for the room, if applicable (e.g., for surgical suites, radiology rooms). Required for compliance and accreditation tracking.
reference: State Department of Health Licensing Requirements

description: Number of medical air outlets for compressed air delivery. Used for respiratory therapy and surgical equipment.
Allocated monthly cost for the room space, including lease payments, depreciation, utilities, and maintenance. Used for cost center chargebacks and financial analysis.
Indicates whether the room has negative pressure ventilation to prevent airborne pathogen spread. Required for airborne isolation (e.g., tuberculosis, COVID-19).
reference: CDC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, ASHRAE 170

description: Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for the room or its equipment. Supports biomedical engineering and facilities management workflows.
Number of nitrous oxide gas outlets. Typically present in operating rooms and dental procedure rooms for anesthesia delivery.
Indicates whether the room is equipped with a nurse call system for patient-to-staff communication. Standard for patient rooms and required for CMS compliance.
Calculated percentage of time the room is occupied over a reporting period. Key performance indicator for space utilization and capacity management.
ISO cleanroom classification for operating room air quality and particulate control. Defines laminar airflow requirements for surgical sterility. Applicable only to OR rooms.
Number of medical gas outlets for oxygen delivery. Essential for patient care capacity planning and emergency preparedness.
Classification of the room based on occupancy and privacy level. Impacts billing, patient preference, and infection control protocols.
Human-readable name or description of the room, often used for scheduling and communication (e.g., Cardiac Cath Lab 1, Pediatric Exam Room B).
The official room number or identifier used for wayfinding, signage, and operational reference. May include alphanumeric codes (e.g., 3A-201, OR-5, ICU-12).
Current operational status of the room. Drives bed management, ADT workflows, and scheduling availability.

Classification of the room based on its primary clinical or operational function. Determines equipment requirements, staffing, and scheduling rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: patient|operating_room|procedure_room|exam_room|consultation_room|support_room|isolation_room|negative_pressure_room — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Total floor area of the room measured in square feet. Used for space utilization analysis, cost allocation, and compliance with facility design standards.
Indicates whether the room is equipped with telemetry monitoring infrastructure for continuous cardiac and vital sign monitoring.
Number of medical vacuum outlets for suctioning. Required for surgical, procedural, and critical care rooms.
Number of electrical outlets dedicated to ventilator equipment. Critical for ICU and critical care room planning and patient placement.
Unique identifier for the clinical or administrative unit within the healthcare facility. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_status. Business justification: Clinical units (Chest Pain Center, Stroke Unit, NICU) hold unit-level accreditation status (ACC, AHA, state designations). accreditation_status links to care_site but not unit. Unit-level accreditatio

Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: A clinical or administrative unit (ICU, ED, OR suite floor) physically resides within a specific building. While unit already links to care_site, a care site may span multiple buildings, so the unit-t
Reference to the parent facility or building where this unit is located. Links to the facility master data product.

Foreign key linking to provider.clinician. Business justification: Unit management accountability requires linking the manager to their clinician record for credentialing verification, privileging status, and regulatory reporting. Hospitals must track which credentia

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Clinical units (ICU, ED, NICU) are governed by specific compliance programs (CMS Conditions of Participation, infection control programs). Compliance officers track unit-level program enrollment for d

Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Hospital units are organized by specialty service line (cardiology, oncology, neurology). Linking unit to specialty supports specialty-based staffing models, nurse-to-patient ratio planning, and servi
Boolean flag indicating whether the unit is currently accepting new patient admissions. False when the unit is at capacity, under diversion, or temporarily closed to new admissions.
reference: Epic EHR ADT Bed Management, HL7 ADT A01 Message

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit is currently accepting patient transfers from other units or facilities. Used in bed management and patient flow coordination.
reference: Epic EHR ADT Bed Management, HL7 ADT A02 Message

description: Typical patient acuity level served by the unit. Critical (ICU, CCU), acute (step-down, telemetry), intermediate (med-surg), general (rehab, skilled nursing), ambulatory (outpatient clinics).
reference: AHRQ Patient Acuity Classification, CMS Case Mix Index (CMI)

description: Age restriction policy for the unit. Pediatric-only (NICU, PICU, pediatric floors), adult-only (most general units), geriatric-only (specialized elder care), or all ages (ED, some outpatient clinics).
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation, Joint Commission Patient Rights Standards

description: Number of complete air changes per hour in the unit. Minimum 6 ACH for general patient rooms, 12+ ACH for isolation rooms, 15+ ACH for operating rooms per ASHRAE and CDC guidelines.
reference: ASHRAE Standard 170, CDC Environmental Infection Control Guidelines

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit (typically ED or cardiac unit) holds accreditation as a Chest Pain Center from the American College of Cardiology or Society of Cardiovascular Patient Care.
reference: ACC Chest Pain Center Accreditation, SCPC Accreditation Standards

description: Timestamp when the unit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Format, HITRUST Audit Logging Requirements

description: Short alphanumeric department code used in operational systems for grouping units into clinical or administrative departments. Used for reporting, scheduling, and organizational hierarchy.
reference: Epic EHR Department Master, Workday HCM Organizational Structure

description: Date when the unit became operational or when the current configuration became effective. Used for historical tracking and regulatory reporting.

Name and version of the Electronic Health Record (EHR) system used in the unit, such as 'Epic Hyperspace 2023', 'Cerner Millennium PowerChart', or 'MEDITECH Expanse'. Used for interoperability and training planning.
reference: ONC Certified EHR Technology, HL7 FHIR Interoperability Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit has emergency generator backup power. Required for critical care units (ICU, OR, ED, NICU) per NFPA 99 and Joint Commission Life Safety Standards.
reference: NFPA 99 Health Care Facilities Code, Joint Commission Life Safety Standards

description: Date when the unit was closed, decommissioned, or when the current configuration expired. Null for active units. Used for historical tracking and capacity planning.
Physical floor or level within the building where the unit is located. May be numeric (1, 2, 3) or alphanumeric (G for ground, B1 for basement 1, M for mezzanine).
Gender restriction policy for the unit. Male-only or female-only for certain inpatient units (e.g., obstetrics, urology). Mixed for most units. Not applicable for procedural or diagnostic units.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation, Joint Commission Patient Rights Standards

business_glossary_term: Heating Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) System Type
description: Type of HVAC system serving the unit, such as 'Central Air', 'Variable Air Volume (VAV)', 'Dedicated Outdoor Air System (DOAS)', or 'Negative Pressure Isolation System'. Critical for infection control and environmental safety.
reference: ASHRAE Standard 170, CDC Environmental Infection Control Guidelines

description: Infection control risk classification for the unit. Standard (general units), enhanced (oncology, transplant), high-risk (ICU, burn unit), isolation (dedicated infectious disease units). Drives environmental services protocols.
reference: CDC Healthcare Infection Control Practices, Joint Commission Infection Prevention Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. True for ICU, ED, inpatient floors. False for outpatient clinics, procedural units with scheduled hours.

Number of isolation rooms within the unit designed for patients requiring infection control precautions (contact, droplet, or airborne isolation). Critical for infection prevention and Healthcare-Associated Infection (HAI) management.
reference: CDC Isolation Precautions Guidelines, Joint Commission Infection Control Standards

description: Timestamp when the unit record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.
reference: ISO 8601 Timestamp Format, HITRUST Audit Logging Requirements

description: Total number of beds the unit is licensed to operate by state or federal regulatory authorities. This is the maximum legal capacity and is reported to CMS and state health departments.
reference: CMS Provider of Services File, State Health Department Licensing

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit is part of a Magnet-recognized nursing program by the American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC). Magnet recognition signifies excellence in nursing care and patient outcomes.

Type of medical gas system installed in the unit, such as 'Oxygen, Medical Air, Vacuum', 'Full Surgical Suite Gases (O2, N2O, Medical Air, Vacuum)', or 'None'. Critical for clinical operations and safety inspections.
reference: NFPA 99 Medical Gas Systems, Joint Commission Environment of Care Standards

description: Number of negative pressure (airborne infection isolation) rooms within the unit, used for patients with airborne infectious diseases such as tuberculosis, measles, or COVID-19. Subset of isolation rooms.
reference: CDC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control, ASHRAE Standard 170

description: Type of nurse call system installed in the unit, such as 'Wired Pillow Speaker', 'Wireless Badge System', 'Integrated EMR Alert System', or 'Mobile App-Based'. Used for patient safety and response time monitoring.
reference: Joint Commission Patient Safety Standards, AHRQ Patient Safety Indicators

description: Target nurse-to-patient staffing ratio for the unit, expressed as 1:X (e.g., 1:2 for ICU, 1:4 for step-down, 1:6 for med-surg). Used for staffing planning and quality benchmarking.
reference: State Nurse Staffing Laws, Joint Commission Staffing Standards

description: Daily end time for unit operations in HH:MM 24-hour format. For 24/7 units, this is typically 23:59. For outpatient or procedural units, this reflects scheduled closing time.
Daily start time for unit operations in HH:MM 24-hour format. For 24/7 units (ICU, ED), this is typically 00:00. For outpatient or procedural units, this reflects scheduled opening time.
Four-digit revenue center code used on UB-04 claims for billing and reimbursement. Maps to CMS revenue code standards (e.g., 0100-0219 for room and board, 0300-0329 for laboratory).
reference: CMS UB-04 Revenue Code List, NUBC Revenue Code Standards

description: Total square footage of the unit's physical space. Used for facility planning, space utilization analysis, and environmental services scheduling.
reference: Facility Management Standards, SAP PM Asset Master

description: Number of beds currently staffed and available for patient occupancy. Staffed bed count is typically less than or equal to licensed bed count and reflects actual operational capacity based on workforce availability.
reference: CMS Hospital Compare, AHRQ Hospital Survey

description: Stroke center certification level for units providing acute stroke care. Comprehensive (highest), primary, acute stroke ready, or not designated. Impacts patient routing and quality reporting.
reference: Joint Commission Stroke Certification, CMS Stroke Quality Measures

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit is designated as a teaching unit where medical residents, fellows, nursing students, or other trainees receive clinical education. Impacts staffing models and documentation requirements.
reference: ACGME Accreditation Standards, CMS Graduate Medical Education (GME) Reporting

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the unit has continuous cardiac telemetry monitoring capability. True for ICU, CCU, step-down, and telemetry units. False for general med-surg and outpatient units.
reference: American Heart Association Telemetry Standards, Joint Commission Critical Care Standards

description: Trauma center level designation for Emergency Department (ED) units. Level 1 is highest capability (comprehensive trauma care), Level 4 is basic. Not applicable for non-ED units.
reference: American College of Surgeons Trauma Center Verification, State EMS Trauma System

description: Short alphanumeric code used to identify the unit in operational systems, scheduling, and Admit Discharge Transfer (ADT) workflows. Typically 2-10 characters.
reference: Epic EHR Cadence Unit Code, HL7 ADT Message PV1-3

description: Full descriptive name of the unit, such as 'Medical Surgical Floor 3 West', 'Intensive Care Unit (ICU) North', 'Emergency Department (ED) Main', or 'Operating Room (OR) Suite B'.

Current operational status of the unit. Active units are open and accepting patients. Inactive or temporarily closed units are not operational. Under construction units are being built or renovated. Decommissioned units are permanently closed.

High-level classification of the unit's operational purpose: inpatient (patient stays overnight), outpatient (same-day care), procedural (OR, cath lab), diagnostic (radiology, lab), administrative (offices), or support (pharmacy, supply).
reference: HL7 FHIR Location.type, CMS Facility Type Codes

description: Wing, section, or zone designation within the floor, such as 'North', 'South', 'East', 'West', 'Tower A', 'Pavilion B'. Used for wayfinding and spatial organization.
Unique identifier for the facility license or accreditation credential record.

Foreign key linking to compliance.accreditation_status. Business justification: Facility-level licenses and accreditations (building permits, OR certifications, unit-specific credentials) must link to enterprise accreditation tracking for deemed status verification, CMS certifica

Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Many licenses and permits are building-specific (occupancy permits, building operating licenses, fire safety certificates, elevator permits). Adding building_id enables building-level credential track
Reference to the care site or facility location to which this license or accreditation applies.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: License and accreditation records are issued under specific compliance programs (TJC, DNV, state licensure programs). Linking license_accreditation to compliance_program enables traceability from a fa

The level or tier of accreditation granted, if applicable. Examples include full accreditation, provisional accreditation, conditional accreditation, or specific program-level designations such as Level I Trauma Center or Primary Stroke Center.

The number of licensed beds authorized under this credential, if applicable. Critical for hospital and inpatient facility licenses.

The full name or title of the license or accreditation credential as issued by the authority.

Current lifecycle status of the license or accreditation credential. Indicates whether the credential is active, pending renewal, expired, suspended, revoked, or inactive.

Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context related to the license or accreditation credential. May include special conditions, restrictions, or historical context.

The date on which the issuing authority approved the facilitys plan of correction.

The date on which the current status became effective.
Explanation or reason for the current status, particularly for suspended, revoked, or inactive credentials. May include regulatory findings, compliance failures, or voluntary surrender.

description: The result or outcome of the most recent survey or inspection. May include findings such as full compliance, deficiencies identified, conditional status, or immediate jeopardy.

Indicates whether this result has been flagged for antibiotic stewardship program review due to resistance pattern, restricted antimicrobial use, or other stewardship criteria. True indicates flagged for review.

business_glossary_term: Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI) Breakpoint Version
description: Version of the CLSI M100 Performance Standards document used to interpret the susceptibility result. Breakpoints are updated annually and version is critical for accurate interpretation (e.g., M100-Ed31, M100-Ed32).
Date and time when this susceptibility result record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Zone of inhibition diameter measured in millimeters for disk diffusion (Kirby-Bauer) susceptibility testing method. Larger zones indicate greater susceptibility.
reference: CLSI M02 Disk Diffusion Performance Standards

description: Indicates whether inducible resistance was detected (e.g., inducible clindamycin resistance in Staphylococcus, inducible AmpC in Enterobacteriaceae). True indicates inducible resistance detected; False indicates not detected.

Indicates whether this result triggered an infection control alert due to detection of multidrug-resistant organism (MDRO), reportable organism, or outbreak-associated pathogen. True indicates alert generated.
Comparison operator for the MIC value when the exact value is at or beyond the test range limits (e.g., <=0.5 indicates at or below the lowest tested concentration).

business_glossary_term: Minimum Inhibitory Concentration (MIC) Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure for the MIC value, typically micrograms per milliliter (mcg/mL) or milligrams per liter (mg/L).
Minimum inhibitory concentration value representing the lowest concentration of antimicrobial agent that inhibits visible growth of the organism. Expressed as a numeric value with unit of measure.
Code identifying the standardized panel or battery of antimicrobial agents tested together (e.g., Gram Positive Panel, Gram Negative Panel, Anaerobe Panel). Panels are organism-specific.
Human-readable name of the antimicrobial susceptibility panel tested (e.g., Gram Positive Cocci Panel, Enterobacteriaceae Panel, Pseudomonas Panel).

Status of quality control testing performed concurrently with patient susceptibility testing. Passed indicates QC organisms yielded expected results; Failed indicates out-of-range QC results requiring investigation.
reference: CLIA Quality Control Requirements, CLSI M100

description: Indicates whether this susceptibility result is reportable to public health authorities (e.g., state health department, CDC) due to detection of notifiable disease or antimicrobial resistance pattern. True indicates reportable.
reference: CDC National Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System

description: Specific resistance gene detected through molecular testing (e.g., mecA, vanA, blaNDM, blaKPC). Used for epidemiological tracking and infection control.
Known or suspected mechanism of antimicrobial resistance detected (e.g., ESBL, Carbapenemase, MRSA, VRE, AmpC). Critical for infection control and antibiotic stewardship.
MIC breakpoint value at or above which the organism is classified as resistant according to CLSI guidelines. Used for automated interpretation.
Free-text comment or interpretive note from the laboratory regarding the susceptibility result. May include recommendations for therapy, warnings about resistance patterns, or technical notes.

Current status of the susceptibility result in its lifecycle. Preliminary results may be released before final verification; Final results have been verified and released; Corrected indicates an amendment to a previously final result.
Date and time when the susceptibility test result was generated or finalized by the laboratory information system. Represents the business event time of result availability.

Code identifying the source laboratory information system (LIS) that generated this susceptibility result (e.g., Epic Beaker, Cerner PathNet, Sunquest). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.

Clinical interpretation of the susceptibility test result based on CLSI breakpoints. Susceptible indicates the organism is inhibited by achievable drug concentrations; Intermediate indicates uncertain efficacy; Resistant indicates the organism is not inhibited by normally achievable concentrations; Susceptible-dose dependent indicates efficacy depends on dosing regimen.
MIC breakpoint value at or below which the organism is classified as susceptible according to CLSI guidelines. Used for automated interpretation.
Result of synergy testing for combination antimicrobial therapy (e.g., beta-lactam/beta-lactamase inhibitor combinations). Positive indicates synergistic effect detected.

Date and time when the susceptibility result was verified and approved for clinical use by authorized laboratory personnel (medical technologist or pathologist).

Precise date and time when the laboratory order was electronically placed in the system. The principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Critical for STAT order tracking and turnaround time analysis.

Boolean flag indicating whether this is a point-of-care test performed at or near the patient location (bedside, clinic exam room, emergency department) rather than in the central laboratory. Examples: glucose meter, rapid strep test, blood gas analyzer.
Unique accession or tracking number assigned by the external reference laboratory to this specimen. Used for result reconciliation and inquiry. Populated only for send-out orders.

Status of electronic result integration from the reference laboratory into the internal LIS and EHR (Electronic Health Record). Tracks whether results were successfully auto-imported or require manual intervention. Populated only for send-out orders.

Date and time when the laboratory result was received back from the reference laboratory and integrated into the LIS (Laboratory Information System). Populated only for send-out orders. Marks completion of the send-out order lifecycle.
Name of the courier or shipping service used to transport the specimen to the reference laboratory. Examples: FedEx, UPS, DHL, courier service. Populated only for send-out orders.
Carrier-provided tracking number for the specimen shipment. Enables real-time tracking of specimen in transit and confirmation of delivery to reference laboratory. Populated only for send-out orders.
Name of the source operational system from which this laboratory order record originated. Examples: Epic Beaker, Cerner PathNet, MEDITECH Laboratory. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.
Unique identifier for this laboratory order in the source operational system (Epic Beaker, Cerner PathNet). Used for cross-system reconciliation and drill-back to source records.

Date and time when the specimen was shipped or dispatched to the external reference laboratory. Used to track send-out order logistics and calculate total turnaround time. Populated only for send-out orders.
Anatomical site or body location from which the specimen was collected. Examples: left arm venipuncture, throat swab, wound site, right knee joint. Important for pathology and microbiology orders.
Type of biological specimen collected for the laboratory test. Examples: blood, serum, plasma, urine, tissue, swab, cerebrospinal fluid. Determines handling and processing requirements.
reference: SNOMED CT (Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms) Specimen

description: Date and time when this laboratory order record was last modified in the data lakehouse. Audit field for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.
Unique identifier for the laboratory reference range record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to laboratory.instrument. Business justification: Reference ranges in clinical laboratories are often instrument-specific — the same analyte may have different normal ranges depending on the analyzer platform used (e.g., Beckman Coulter vs. Roche Cob

Foreign key linking to reference.loinc_code. Business justification: Reference ranges are LOINC-specific and vary by test methodology. Structured linkage enables proper result interpretation across systems, supports automated abnormal flag generation, and ensures clini

Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Specialty-specific reference ranges (e.g., cardiology troponin thresholds vs. general medicine) are standard lab practice. Linking reference_range to provider.specialty enables specialty-driven result

Foreign key linking to laboratory.test_catalog. Business justification: Reference ranges are defined FOR specific tests in the catalog. Currently reference_range has test_code/test_name but no FK to test_catalog. Business reality: reference ranges are test-specific and sh

Age group or age range for which this reference range applies (e.g., Neonate, Infant, Child, Adolescent, Adult, Geriatric, or specific age ranges like 0-1 years, 1-12 years, 18-65 years). Reference ranges are age-dependent for many tests.

Priority level for clinical alerts triggered when results fall outside this reference range. Critical priority requires immediate notification within minutes; urgent within hours; routine for next business day review.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) NPSG.02.03.01 Critical Test Results

description: Indicates whether results outside this reference range should trigger automated clinical alerts or notifications. True for critical value ranges requiring immediate provider notification per Joint Commission requirements.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) NPSG.02.03.01 Critical Test Results

description: Clinical interpretation guidance describing the significance of values outside this reference range. Provides context for clinicians interpreting abnormal results (e.g., elevated values may indicate infection, dehydration, or malignancy).
Timestamp when this reference range record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The critical high value threshold (panic value) above which immediate clinical notification is required. Represents life-threatening high values requiring urgent intervention.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA), The Joint Commission (TJC) NPSG.02.03.01

description: The critical low value threshold (panic value) below which immediate clinical notification is required. Represents life-threatening low values requiring urgent intervention.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA), The Joint Commission (TJC) NPSG.02.03.01

description: The date through which this reference range remains valid. Null indicates the range is currently active with no planned end date. Supports historical tracking and periodic review requirements.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) Quality Control Requirements

description: The date from which this reference range becomes valid and should be used for result interpretation. Supports versioning of reference ranges over time.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) Quality Control Requirements

description: Standardized code used by result interpretation logic to assign abnormal flags when test results fall outside this reference range. Maps to HL7 observation interpretation codes.

Date when this reference range was last reviewed and validated by the laboratory medical director or quality team. CLIA and CAP require periodic review of reference ranges at least annually or when methodology changes.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) 42 CFR 493.1253, College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accreditation Requirements

description: Timestamp when this reference range record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
Internal system code or identifier used by the Laboratory Information System (Epic Beaker, Cerner PathNet) to reference this range in result interpretation logic and reporting.
The lower boundary of the normal reference range. Values below this threshold are typically flagged as low or abnormal.

Indicates whether this reference range represents an institutional medical director override of standard published ranges. True if the laboratory medical director has approved a deviation from manufacturer or published ranges based on local population characteristics.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) 42 CFR 493.1253, College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accreditation Requirements

description: The analytical method or instrument platform used to establish this reference range (e.g., Spectrophotometry, Immunoassay, Mass Spectrometry, PCR). Reference ranges may vary by methodology for the same test.
Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this reference range. Supports compliance with CLIA and CAP requirements for annual review and documentation.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) 42 CFR 493.1253, College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accreditation Requirements

description: Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this reference range. May include information about interfering substances, pre-analytical requirements, or special patient populations.

Clinical justification and documentation for medical director override of standard reference ranges. Required when medical_director_override_flag is true. Includes rationale, supporting data, and approval documentation.
reference: Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) 42 CFR 493.1253, College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accreditation Requirements

description: Description of the reference population used to establish this range (e.g., healthy adult volunteers, local patient population, manufacturer validation study). Documents the basis for the reference interval per CLSI guidelines.
Pregnancy status for which this reference range applies. Certain laboratory values have distinct reference ranges during pregnancy (e.g., thyroid function tests, hemoglobin).

Race or ethnicity group for which this reference range applies, when clinically validated differences exist (e.g., creatinine clearance adjustments for African American patients). Only populated when evidence-based clinical guidelines support race-specific ranges.
reference: CDC Race and Ethnicity Code Set, National Institutes of Health (NIH) Guidelines

description: Current review and approval status of this reference range. Tracks lifecycle state for quality management and compliance purposes.
reference: College of American Pathologists (CAP) Accreditation Requirements

description: Number of individuals in the reference population used to establish this range. CLSI recommends minimum 120 samples for robust reference intervals. Documents statistical validity of the range.
Biological sex for which this reference range applies. Many laboratory tests have sex-specific reference ranges (e.g., hemoglobin, creatinine).

Detailed citation or reference to the authoritative source document (e.g., CAP guideline version, manufacturer package insert identifier, peer-reviewed journal article, institutional policy document). Required for CLIA compliance and periodic review.
The type of authoritative source from which this reference range was derived. CLIA requires documentation of reference range sources.

Statistical method used to calculate the reference interval (e.g., parametric 95% confidence interval, non-parametric percentile method, robust method). Documents the analytical approach per CLSI standards.
The standardized unit of measure for the reference range values (e.g., mg/dL, mmol/L, g/dL, cells/mcL, IU/L). Must match the unit used in test results for proper interpretation.
reference: Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM)

description: The upper boundary of the normal reference range. Values above this threshold are typically flagged as high or abnormal.
Unique identifier for the specimen record. Primary key.

Identifier of the clinical order that requested the laboratory tests for which this specimen was collected. Links specimen to ordering workflow.

Unique identifier of the patient from whom the specimen was collected. Links specimen to patient master record.
Identifier of the parent specimen if this specimen is an aliquot or derivative. Supports specimen lineage tracking.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Specimen chain of custody, biohazard handling, and retention policies are governed by CLIA and CAP compliance programs. Linking specimen to its governing compliance program enables chain-of-custody au

Identifier of the clinical encounter or visit during which the specimen was collected. Provides clinical context and billing linkage.
Date and time when the specimen was received and accessioned into the Laboratory Information System (LIS). Marks the beginning of laboratory custody and processing.

Laboratory Information System (LIS) assigned unique work-unit identifier for the specimen. This is the operational identity in Epic Beaker and Cerner PathNet, used to track the specimen through the laboratory workflow from receipt through testing and disposal.
Current lifecycle status of the specimen in the laboratory workflow. Tracks progression from receipt through testing to final disposition.
Biohazard risk classification of the specimen based on known or suspected infectious agents. Determines safety precautions and handling protocols.
reference: OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard, CDC Biosafety Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the chain of custody has been maintained throughout specimen handling. Critical for forensic, toxicology, and legal specimens.
Date and time when the specimen was collected from the patient. Critical for time-sensitive tests and stability assessments.
Duration of specimen collection in minutes. Relevant for timed collections such as 24-hour urine or glucose tolerance tests.
Technique or procedure used to collect the specimen (e.g., venipuncture, clean catch, biopsy, swab). Critical for quality assessment and result interpretation.
reference: SNOMED CT Procedure Codes, HL7 Specimen Collection Method

description: Professional role or title of the person who collected the specimen (e.g., phlebotomist, registered nurse, physician). Provides context for collection quality and training requirements.
Free-text comments or notes about the specimen, collection circumstances, or quality observations. Provides additional context for laboratory staff and clinicians.
Assessment of specimen quality and integrity upon receipt at the laboratory. Documents any pre-analytical issues that may affect test results.
Type of collection container or tube used (e.g., red top, lavender top EDTA, sterile cup). Determines appropriate tests and handling requirements.
reference: HL7 Specimen Container Type Value Set

description: Date and time when this specimen record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Date and time when the specimen was disposed of or destroyed. Supports retention policy compliance and inventory management.
Method used to dispose of the specimen (e.g., biohazard waste, incineration, autoclave). Ensures compliance with safety and environmental regulations.
reference: OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard, EPA Waste Regulations

description: Indicates whether the patient was fasting at the time of specimen collection. Critical for interpretation of glucose, lipid, and metabolic tests.
Count of aliquots or sub-specimens created from the original specimen for distribution to different testing sections or for storage.
Processing priority level assigned to the specimen. Determines turnaround time expectations and workflow sequencing.
Reason for specimen rejection if not acceptable for testing (e.g., hemolyzed, insufficient quantity, unlabeled, expired). Supports quality improvement and recollection requests.
Date when the specimen retention period expires and the specimen may be disposed of per laboratory policy.
Current retention status of the specimen relative to laboratory retention policies. Indicates whether specimen is available for additional testing or has been disposed.
Any special handling requirements or precautions for the specimen (e.g., keep frozen, protect from light, handle as infectious). Ensures proper specimen management.
Anatomical site or body location from which the specimen was collected (e.g., left antecubital vein, throat, wound site). Provides clinical context for interpretation of test results.
Type of biological specimen collected (e.g., blood, urine, tissue, cerebrospinal fluid, swab, stool). Defines the nature of the material submitted for laboratory analysis.
reference: SNOMED CT Specimen Type Codes, HL7 Specimen Type Value Set

description: Temperature at which the specimen is stored, measured in degrees Celsius. Critical for specimen stability and quality assurance.
Time elapsed between specimen collection and laboratory receipt, measured in minutes. Critical for time-sensitive analytes and quality assessment.
Temperature at which the specimen was transported from collection site to laboratory, measured in degrees Celsius. Affects specimen stability and quality.
Date and time when this specimen record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.
Volume of specimen collected, measured in milliliters. Used to determine test feasibility and aliquot planning.
Unique identifier for the laboratory test catalog entry. Primary key for the test catalog product.
