business_glossary_term: Dose Area Product (DAP) in Gray-Centimeter Squared (Gy·cm²)
description: Dose Area Product (DAP) in Gy·cm² for fluoroscopy, X-ray angiography, and interventional radiology procedures. Measures total radiation energy delivered to the patient. Primary dose metric for fluoroscopic procedures per IEC 60601-2-43.
reference: IEC 60601-2-43, ACR DIR, NCRP Report 168, Joint Commission

description: Dose Length Product (DLP) in mGy·cm for CT studies. Represents the total radiation energy imparted along the scan length. Used with conversion coefficients to estimate effective dose and is the primary metric for ACR DIR CT dose reporting.
reference: IEC 60601-2-44, ACR DIR, NCRP Report 172, ICRP Publication 102

description: Indicates whether this study triggered a radiation dose alert based on institutional or regulatory thresholds (e.g., CTDIvol > alert level, fluoroscopy time > 30 min, RAK > 3 Gy). True = alert triggered; False = within normal limits. Supports Joint Commission radiation safety event reporting.
reference: Joint Commission Sentinel Event Alert, FDA Dose Check (NEMA XR-25), ACR DIR

description: The specific dose metric that triggered the dose alert, if applicable. Identifies which threshold was exceeded to support targeted clinical review and root cause analysis. 'not_triggered' when dose_alert_flag is False. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ctdivol|dlp|dap|fluoroscopy_time|rak|effective_dose|not_triggered — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Dose Check (NEMA XR-25), Joint Commission, ACR DIR

description: The numeric threshold value that was exceeded to trigger the dose alert, expressed in the unit of the triggering metric (mGy, mGy·cm, Gy·cm², seconds, or mSv). Supports audit trail for dose safety events.
reference: FDA Dose Check (NEMA XR-25), Joint Commission Sentinel Event Alert

description: Current lifecycle status of the radiation dose record. 'preliminary' = dose data received but not yet reviewed; 'final' = reviewed and confirmed; 'amended' = corrected after finalization; 'corrected' = technical correction applied; 'cancelled' = study voided.
reference: IEC 61910, ACR DIR, HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport

business_glossary_term: ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR) Submission Status
description: Status of submission of this dose record to the ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR). 'pending'=queued for submission; 'submitted'=transmitted to ACR DIR; 'accepted'=confirmed by ACR DIR; 'rejected'=rejected by ACR DIR (requires correction); 'not_required'=procedure not in DIR scope.
reference: ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR), CMS Quality Programs, MIPS

description: Date and time when this dose record was submitted to the ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR) or other regulatory dose registry. Supports audit trail for regulatory compliance and CMS quality program reporting.
reference: ACR DIR, CMS MIPS, Joint Commission

description: Result of comparing the study dose against the applicable national or institutional Diagnostic Reference Level (DRL). 'below_drl'=dose is below the reference level (optimal); 'at_drl'=dose equals reference level; 'above_drl'=dose exceeds reference level (requires review); 'not_applicable'=no DRL defined for this procedure.
reference: ICRP Publication 135, ACR DIR, NCRP Report 172, CMS Quality Programs

description: Estimated effective dose in millisieverts (mSv) representing the stochastic risk-weighted whole-body equivalent dose. Derived from DLP × conversion coefficient (k-factor) for CT, or from DAP for fluoroscopy. Used for patient communication, cumulative dose tracking, and ALARA compliance.
reference: ICRP Publication 103, NCRP Report 160, ACR DIR, Joint Commission

description: Entrance skin dose (ESD) in milligray (mGy) for radiographic and fluoroscopic procedures. Represents the absorbed dose at the skin entry point. Critical for radiation injury risk assessment and Joint Commission threshold monitoring.
reference: Joint Commission Sentinel Event Alert, NCRP Report 168, IEC 60601-2-43

description: Total fluoroscopy beam-on time in seconds for fluoroscopic and interventional procedures. Regulatory threshold monitoring metric; Joint Commission requires tracking and alerting when fluoroscopy time exceeds defined thresholds (e.g., 30 minutes cumulative).
reference: Joint Commission Sentinel Event Alert, IEC 60601-2-43, FDA 21 CFR 1020.32

description: Peak kilovoltage (kVp) applied to the X-ray tube during the study. Represents the energy of the X-ray beam. Used for dose optimization, protocol review, and physics quality assurance.
reference: DICOM PS3.3, ACR Technical Standards, IEC 60601-2-44

description: DICOM modality code indicating the type of imaging equipment used. CT=Computed Tomography, FL=Fluoroscopy, NM=Nuclear Medicine, DX=Digital Radiography (X-ray), MG=Mammography, XA=X-Ray Angiography, RF=Radiofluoroscopy. Determines applicable dose metrics (CTDIvol/DLP for CT, DAP/fluoroscopy time for fluoroscopy). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CT|FL|NM|DX|MG|XA|RF|PT|SPECT — promote to reference product]
reference: DICOM PS3.3 C.7.3.1.1.1, ACR DIR, IEC 61910

description: Total count of individual X-ray exposures (radiographic frames or pulses) delivered during the study. Used for dose optimization analysis and comparison against diagnostic reference levels (DRLs).
reference: DICOM PS3.3 TID 10011, ACR DIR, NCRP Report 172

description: Patient age in whole years at the time of the imaging study. Used for pediatric dose benchmarking (pediatric patients require lower dose protocols), age-stratified DRL comparisons, and ACR DIR age-group reporting. Derived at time of study to avoid recalculation.
reference: ACR DIR, NCRP Report 172, ICRP Publication 135, Pediatric Imaging Standards

description: Patient effective diameter or water-equivalent diameter in centimeters, used for Size-Specific Dose Estimate (SSDE) calculation in CT. Derived from scout/localizer images or patient measurements. Required for AAPM TG-204 SSDE methodology.
reference: AAPM Report 204 (SSDE), AAPM Report 220, ACR DIR

description: Patient body weight in kilograms at the time of the imaging study. Used for size-specific dose estimate (SSDE) calculations for CT, weight-based protocol selection, and dose normalization for benchmarking.

Indicates whether this dose record has been flagged for or completed a review by a qualified medical physicist. True = physicist review required or completed; False = no physicist review needed. Triggered by dose alerts, unusual protocol deviations, or regulatory requirements.
reference: Joint Commission Radiation Safety Standards, ACR Practice Parameters, NCRP

description: Date and time when the medical physicist completed the review of this dose record. Null if physicist_review_flag is False or review is pending. Supports compliance audit trail for Joint Commission radiation safety requirements.
reference: Joint Commission Radiation Safety Standards, ACR Practice Parameters

description: Human-readable description of the imaging procedure performed (e.g., 'CT Chest with Contrast', 'Fluoroscopic Guidance for Biopsy'). Sourced from the Charge Description Master (CDM) or RIS procedure catalog.
reference: ACR DIR, CDM, Epic Radiant, Cerner RadNet

business_glossary_term: Radiation Dose Structured Report (RDSR) Unique Identifier (UID)
description: DICOM Radiation Dose Structured Report (RDSR) SOP Instance UID per IEC 61910 standard. Globally unique identifier for the structured dose report generated by the imaging modality. Used for PACS integration and ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR) submission.
reference: IEC 61910, DICOM PS3.3 TID 10011, ACR DIR

description: Reference Air Kerma (RAK) in mGy for interventional fluoroscopy procedures. Measured at the interventional reference point (IRP) per IEC 60601-2-43. Used for radiation injury risk assessment and regulatory threshold compliance.

Operational system of record from which this dose record was sourced (e.g., Epic Radiant, Cerner RadNet, third-party dose management system). Used for data lineage, ETL audit, and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: Epic Radiant, Cerner RadNet, MEDITECH Expanse, IEC 61910

description: Size-Specific Dose Estimate (SSDE) in mGy for CT studies, calculated per AAPM TG-204 methodology by applying a size-based conversion factor to CTDIvol. Provides a more accurate patient-specific dose estimate than CTDIvol alone. Used for pediatric dose optimization and ACR DIR advanced reporting.
reference: AAPM Report 204, AAPM Report 220, ACR DIR

description: Calendar date on which the imaging study was performed. Principal business event date for dose record. Used for trending, regulatory reporting, and cumulative dose period calculations.
reference: DICOM PS3.3, ACR DIR, Joint Commission

description: DICOM Study Instance UID uniquely identifying the imaging study in the PACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System). Enables direct linkage between dose records and DICOM image metadata for audit and regulatory reporting.
Precise date and time the imaging study was performed, including timezone offset. Used for intraday workflow analysis, dose alert timing, and RDSR correlation.
reference: DICOM PS3.3, IEC 61910, HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy

description: X-ray tube current-time product in milliampere-seconds (mAs) for the study. Directly influences radiation dose output. Used for protocol optimization and dose benchmarking.
reference: DICOM PS3.3, ACR Technical Standards, IEC 60601-2-44

description: Date and time when this dose record was last modified in the source system or Silver layer. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.
Primary key for report
Reference to the facility (hospital, imaging center, outpatient clinic) where the imaging study was performed. Used for operational reporting, capacity management, and multi-site analytics.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation; Joint Commission Accreditation; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Radiology reports document findings from ordered studies. Providers review results in context of original orders. Quality metrics track order-to-report turnaround times. Regulatory requirements for re

Foreign key linking to clinical.diagnosis. Business justification: Radiology reports confirm or establish clinical diagnoses (e.g., confirmed malignancy, fracture). CDI programs, DRG coding, and quality reporting require linking the report's findings to the resulting

Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Reports reference CPT codes for billing reconciliation between RIS and revenue cycle systems, support professional fee coding, and enable radiologist productivity tracking by procedure complexity and

business_glossary_term: Icd10 Primary Icd Code Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Reports document ICD-10 codes for clinical documentation integrity, support diagnosis-based quality measures, and enable population health analytics linking imaging findings to disease prevalence.
Reference to the parent imaging order that triggered this radiology report. Links the report back to the order management workflow in the radiology domain.

Foreign key linking to reference.loinc_code. Business justification: FHIR DiagnosticReport.code requires a LOINC code for the report type, mandatory for HIE and EHR interoperability. HL7 ORU R01 messages use LOINC to identify the observation/report type. Radiology info
Reference to the patient who is the subject of the imaging study and this report. Protected Health Information (PHI) under HIPAA. Links to the Master Patient Index (MPI).
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HL7 FHIR Patient; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Foreign key linking to claim.prior_authorization. Business justification: Radiology reports serve as primary clinical documentation in PA appeal workflows. A direct FK enables automated retrieval of the authorizing PA when a report is submitted for appeal review — standard
Reference to the provider who authored the addendum or amendment. May differ from the original signing radiologist. Null for the original report.
reference: IHE RAD-28; AHIMA Health Information Management Standards; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Reference to the radiologist (provider) who performed the final attestation and signed the report. Corresponds to the interpreting physician of record for legal and billing purposes.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation; AMA CPT; HIPAA 45 CFR §164; IHE RAD-28

description: Reference to the radiologist who performed the primary interpretation of the imaging study. May differ from the signing radiologist in teaching or supervisory contexts (e.g., resident reads, attending signs). Used for productivity tracking and RVU attribution.
reference: CMS Conditions of Participation; ACGME Residency Program Requirements; AMA CPT; Epic Radiant

description: Foreign key linking to reference.snomed_concept. Business justification: Structured radiology reporting (ACR/RSNA, FHIR DiagnosticReport) requires SNOMED CT coding of primary findings for quality measures, cancer registry reporting, and clinical decision support. A domain
Reference to the clinical encounter (visit) during which the imaging study was ordered and performed. Enables linkage to the patient visit context for clinical and revenue cycle workflows.
reference: HL7 FHIR Encounter; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the Radiology Information System (RIS) to the imaging order and its associated report. Used as the primary cross-system business key linking the report to the imaging order, PACS, and billing systems. Sourced from Epic Radiant and Cerner RadNet.
reference: IHE RAD-28; DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0008,0050); HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport.identifier

description: Sequential number identifying the addendum within the report's amendment history. Null for the original report. Increments with each addendum or amendment added post-finalization. Used to order and display the full report amendment history.
reference: IHE RAD-28; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport; HIM Documentation Standards; AHIMA Health Information Management Standards

description: Full text content of the addendum or amendment added to the report post-finalization. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI). Null for the original report. Preserves the complete amendment history as versioned child records.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; IHE RAD-28; AHIMA Health Information Management Standards; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport

description: Date and time when the addendum or amendment was authored and signed. Null for the original report. Used to track the timeline of post-final modifications.
reference: IHE RAD-28; AHIMA Health Information Management Standards; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport

description: Classifies the type of post-final modification to the report. 'Addendum' adds new information without changing the original; 'amendment' modifies existing content; 'correction' fixes a factual error; 'retraction' withdraws the report. Null for the original report.
reference: IHE RAD-28; AHIMA Health Information Management Standards; HIM Documentation Standards; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport

description: Date and time when the signing radiologist attested and finalized the report, transitioning it to 'final' status. This is the principal business event timestamp for the report lifecycle. Required for billing, legal, and compliance purposes.
reference: IHE RAD-28; CMS Conditions of Participation; HIPAA 45 CFR §164; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Anatomical region or body part that was the subject of the imaging study (e.g., CHEST, ABDOMEN, BRAIN, KNEE). Sourced from DICOM tag (0018,0015) and RIS order. Used for clinical classification and CPT code validation.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0018,0015); SNOMED CT; ACR Index

description: Indicates whether contrast agent was administered during the imaging study. Relevant for CPT code selection, billing accuracy, radiation safety, and clinical documentation of contrast reactions.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; AMA CPT; DICOM PS3.3; Epic Radiant

description: Name of the contrast agent administered during the imaging study (e.g., Gadolinium, Iohexol, Gadobutrol). Null if no contrast was administered. Used for adverse event tracking, pharmacy reconciliation, and clinical documentation.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; FDA Drug Labeling; NDC; Epic Willow Pharmacy

description: Date and time when the radiology report record was first created in the source system. Represents the audit creation timestamp for the report entity. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and compliance reporting.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) — Audit Controls; ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF

description: Indicates whether the critical finding has been communicated to the ordering or responsible provider. Required for Joint Commission compliance. Only applicable when critical_finding_flag is True.
reference: The Joint Commission NPSG.02.03.01; ACR Practice Parameter for Communication of Diagnostic Imaging Findings

description: Date and time when the critical finding was communicated to the ordering or responsible provider. Used to measure turnaround time compliance with Joint Commission and ACR critical results communication standards.
reference: The Joint Commission NPSG.02.03.01; ACR Practice Parameter for Communication of Diagnostic Imaging Findings

description: Indicates whether the report contains a critical or urgent finding that requires immediate communication to the ordering provider per ACR and Joint Commission guidelines. When True, triggers a critical results notification workflow. Supports patient safety and regulatory compliance.
reference: The Joint Commission NPSG.02.03.01; ACR Practice Parameter for Communication of Diagnostic Imaging Findings; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Globally unique DICOM Study Instance UID linking this report to the corresponding image set in the Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS). Enables direct navigation from the report to the source images. Sourced from DICOM tag (0020,000D).
reference: DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0020,000D); IHE RAD-28; PACS Integration Standards

description: Date and time when the radiologist began dictating the report. Used to measure report turnaround time (TAT) from study completion to dictation. Sourced from Epic Radiant or Cerner RadNet workflow timestamps.
reference: ACR Radiology Reporting Turnaround Time Guidelines; IHE RAD-28; Epic Radiant

description: Full narrative text of the radiologist's findings section of the report, describing the imaging observations in detail. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI). This is the primary clinical content of the report and the source of truth for diagnostic observations. Sourced from Epic ClinDoc or Cerner PowerChart dictation/transcription.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport.text; IHE RAD-28; ACR Reporting Guidelines

description: Structured follow-up recommendation from the radiologist's impression, indicating the recommended next action (e.g., 'Follow-up CT in 3 months', 'Biopsy recommended', 'No follow-up needed'). Used for care coordination, population health management, and closing the loop on incidental findings.
reference: ACR Reporting Guidelines; The Joint Commission; CMS Quality Reporting; NCQA HEDIS

description: Radiologist's summary impression or conclusion section of the report, providing the diagnostic interpretation and clinical recommendations. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI). This is the most clinically actionable section of the report and is used for clinical decision support, CDI, and quality measurement.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport.conclusion; IHE RAD-28; ACR Reporting Guidelines

description: Indicates the side of the body examined when applicable (left, right, bilateral, or unspecified). Critical for surgical safety, billing accuracy, and clinical documentation. Sourced from DICOM tag (0020,0060) and RIS.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0020,0060); The Joint Commission National Patient Safety Goals; ICD-10-CM

description: DICOM-standard code identifying the imaging modality used for the study (e.g., CT = Computed Tomography, MR = Magnetic Resonance Imaging, XR/DX/CR = X-Ray, US = Ultrasound, PT = Positron Emission Tomography, NM = Nuclear Medicine, MG = Mammography, FL = Fluoroscopy). Sourced from DICOM tag (0008,0060). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CT|MR|XR|US|PT|NM|MG|FL|DX|CR — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0008,0060); ACR Practice Parameters; IHE RAD-28

description: Date and time when the report was first released in preliminary status, making it available to ordering providers before final attestation. Key lifecycle event for TAT measurement and clinical workflow.
reference: IHE RAD-28; ACR Radiology Reporting Turnaround Time Guidelines; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport

business_glossary_term: Radiation Dose CT Dose Index Volume (CTDIvol) mGy
description: CT Dose Index Volume (CTDIvol) in mGy representing the average radiation dose per unit length for CT studies. Null for non-CT modalities. Used alongside DLP for comprehensive radiation dose monitoring and ACR Dose Index Registry reporting.
reference: ACR Dose Index Registry; NCRP Report No. 172; FDA Radiation-Emitting Products; IHE REM Profile; DICOM PS3.3

business_glossary_term: Radiation Dose Dose Length Product (DLP) mGy·cm
description: Dose Length Product (DLP) in mGy·cm representing the total radiation dose delivered during the CT or fluoroscopy study. Null for non-ionizing modalities (MRI, Ultrasound). Used for radiation dose monitoring, ACR Dose Index Registry reporting, and patient safety compliance.
reference: ACR Dose Index Registry; NCRP Report No. 172; FDA Radiation-Emitting Products; IHE REM Profile; DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0040,A124)

description: Standardized ACR Reporting and Data System (RADS) assessment category assigned in the impression (e.g., BI-RADS 0-6 for mammography, LI-RADS 1-5 for liver, TI-RADS 1-5 for thyroid, Lung-RADS 1-4). Drives follow-up recommendations and quality measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BI-RADS 0|BI-RADS 1|BI-RADS 2|BI-RADS 3|BI-RADS 4|BI-RADS 5|BI-RADS 6|LI-RADS 1|LI-RADS 2|LI-RADS 3|LI-RADS 4|LI-RADS 5|TI-RADS 1|TI-RADS 2|TI-RADS 3|TI-RADS 4|TI-RADS 5|Lung-RADS 1|Lung-RADS 2|Lung-RADS 3|Lung-RADS 4 — promote to reference product]
reference: ACR BI-RADS Atlas; ACR LI-RADS; ACR Lung-RADS; ACR TI-RADS; NCQA HEDIS

description: Current workflow state of the radiology report. Drives clinical decision-making and downstream notification workflows. 'Preliminary' indicates a draft interpretation; 'final' indicates the signed, attested report; 'addendum' and 'amended' indicate post-final modifications. Aligns with HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport.status.
reference: HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport.status; IHE RAD-28; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Native report identifier from the source Radiology Information System (RIS), such as Epic Radiant or Cerner RadNet. Used for cross-system reconciliation, ETL lineage tracking, and source system audit.
reference: Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet; IHE RAD-28

description: 10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the radiologist who signed and attested the final report. Required for CMS billing, claims submission, and provider credentialing validation. Sourced from the NPPES registry.
reference: CMS NPI Final Rule 45 CFR Part 162; HIPAA 45 CFR §162.406; CMS-1500; UB-04

description: Indicates whether the imaging order and report were designated as STAT (immediate/urgent priority), requiring expedited turnaround time. Drives workflow prioritization in the RIS and radiologist worklist. Used for TAT compliance monitoring.
reference: ACR Radiology Reporting Turnaround Time Guidelines; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Date and time when the imaging study was performed (image acquisition). This is the principal real-world event time for the report. Sourced from DICOM tag (0008,0020)/(0008,0030) and RIS. Used as the primary temporal anchor for TAT calculations and clinical timelines.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 Tags (0008,0020) and (0008,0030); IHE RAD-28; HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy.started

description: Human-readable description of the imaging study protocol or procedure performed (e.g., 'CT Chest with Contrast', 'MRI Brain without Contrast'). Sourced from the RIS order and DICOM tag (0008,1030). Used for report titling and clinical context.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 Tag (0008,1030); Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Reference to the structured reporting template used to generate the report (e.g., ACR-standardized templates, site-specific templates). Supports structured reporting initiatives, quality measurement, and AI-assisted reporting workflows.
reference: ACR Reporting and Data Systems (RADS); IHE RAD-28; RadReport Template Library

description: Date and time when the report transcription was completed (if applicable). Relevant for facilities using human transcription services rather than voice recognition. Used in TAT measurement.
reference: IHE RAD-28; ACR Radiology Reporting Turnaround Time Guidelines; Epic Radiant

description: Date and time when the radiology report record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312(b) — Audit Controls; ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; ETL Best Practices

description: Sequential version number of the report, starting at 1 for the initial report and incrementing with each amendment or addendum. Enables tracking of the full amendment history and identification of the current authoritative version.
reference: IHE RAD-28; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport; HIM Documentation Standards

business_glossary_term: Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM) Series Identifier
description: Unique identifier for the DICOM series record within the lakehouse. Primary key for this table.

Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: RIS/PACS charge capture and billing reconciliation require series-level CPT code as a proper FK for charge integrity. The existing 'cpt_code' plain string column is a denormalized code value; replacin

Foreign key linking to radiology.protocol. Business justification: dicom_series currently has protocol_name: STRING (free-text field). Each DICOM series is acquired using a specific imaging protocol. Adding imaging_protocol_id FK normalizes this relationship to the p

Foreign key linking to radiology.modality. Business justification: A DICOM series is acquired on a specific physical imaging modality unit. While the parent radiology_study already has modality_id, the dicom_series level FK to radiology.modality.modality_id is valuab

Foreign key linking to provider.clinician. Business justification: The performing clinician on each DICOM series drives RVU attribution, radiation safety tracking, quality review, and credentialing compliance. DICOM standard captures performing physician; normalizing
Foreign key reference to the parent imaging study that contains this series. Links to the imaging_study product.
Unique identifier assigned by the RIS to the imaging order or examination. Links DICOM images to radiology orders and reports.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Study Module, HL7 v2.x ORM Message

description: Anatomical region or body part that was imaged in this series (e.g., 'CHEST', 'HEAD', 'ABDOMEN'). May use SNOMED CT codes or free text.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Series Module, SNOMED CT Body Structure Hierarchy

description: Name or description of the contrast agent administered for this series (e.g., 'Iohexol 300mg/mL', 'Gadolinium'). Null if no contrast was used.
Route by which contrast agent was administered (e.g., intravenous, oral). Null if no contrast was used.
Total volume of contrast agent administered in milliliters. Null if no contrast was used or volume not recorded.
Timestamp when this series record was first created in the lakehouse. Audit trail for data lineage.

business_glossary_term: Computed Tomography (CT) Dose Index Volume in Milligray (mGy)
description: CT Dose Index Volume, a standardized measure of radiation dose for CT examinations. Null for non-CT modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - CT Radiation Dose Module, FDA CT Dose Reporting

description: Dose Length Product, representing the integrated radiation dose over the scan length for CT examinations. Null for non-CT modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - CT Radiation Dose Module, FDA CT Dose Reporting

description: X-ray tube current in milliamperes during image acquisition. Used for radiation dose calculation. Null for non-radiation modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - CT/XR Image Module

description: Duration of X-ray exposure in milliseconds. Used for radiation dose calculation. Null for non-radiation modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - CT/XR Image Module

description: Direction cosines of the first row and first column with respect to the patient coordinate system. Six-value array stored as string (e.g., '1\0\0\0\1\0'). Essential for 3D spatial registration.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - Image Plane Module

description: Peak kilovoltage output of the X-ray generator used for CT or radiography acquisition. Null for non-radiation modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - CT/XR Image Module

description: Laterality of the body part examined: L (Left), R (Right), B (Bilateral), U (Unpaired/midline). Null if not applicable.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Series Module

description: Manufacturer of the imaging equipment used to acquire this series (e.g., 'GE Healthcare', 'Siemens Healthineers', 'Philips Medical Systems').
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Equipment Module

description: Model name of the imaging equipment (e.g., 'Discovery CT750 HD', 'MAGNETOM Skyra', 'Ingenuity CT').
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Equipment Module

description: Type of imaging equipment or acquisition method used to create this series. Standard DICOM modality codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CT|MR|XA|RF|US|MG|DX|CR|NM|PT|OT — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - Modality Defined Terms

description: Total count of DICOM image instances (individual images or frames) contained within this series. Used for completeness validation and PACS retrieval.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Series Module

business_glossary_term: Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Archive Status
description: Current storage tier or availability status of the series within the PACS archive hierarchy (online cache, nearline storage, offline tape, etc.).
reference: IHE Radiology Technical Framework - Image Manager/Archive Actor

business_glossary_term: Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Storage Path
description: File system path or object storage location where the DICOM series images are stored in the PACS archive. Used for image retrieval and system integration.
reference: IHE Radiology Technical Framework - Image Manager/Archive Actor

description: Patient position during image acquisition using DICOM standard codes: HFS (Head First Supine), HFP (Head First Prone), FFS (Feet First Supine), etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HFS|HFP|FFS|FFP|HFDR|HFDL|FFDR|FFDL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - Patient Orientation Module

description: Physical distance between pixel centers in millimeters, formatted as 'row_spacing\column_spacing' (e.g., '0.625\0.625'). Used for accurate measurement and calibration.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - Image Plane Module

description: Two-character CPT or HCPCS modifier providing additional information about the procedure (e.g., laterality, professional/technical component). Null if no modifier applies.
reference: AMA CPT Code Set, CMS HCPCS Level II Modifiers

description: Free-text comments from quality control review, documenting any issues, artifacts, or technical concerns identified during QC assessment.
reference: ACR Technical Standard for Diagnostic Medical Physics Performance Monitoring

description: Result of quality control review for this series. Indicates whether the series meets institutional quality standards for diagnostic interpretation.
reference: ACR Technical Standard for Diagnostic Medical Physics Performance Monitoring

description: Estimated or measured radiation dose delivered to the patient during this series in milligray. Critical for dose tracking and regulatory reporting. Null for non-radiation modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - Radiation Dose Module, IHE Radiation Exposure Monitoring (REM) Profile

description: Ten-digit NPI of the referring physician. Used for provider attribution and claims processing.
reference: CMS National Provider Identifier Standard, HIPAA Administrative Simplification

description: Name of the physician who ordered the imaging study. Used for result routing and clinical communication.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Study Module

description: Indicates whether all expected instances for this series have been received and stored in PACS. True if complete, False if incomplete or pending.
reference: IHE Radiology Technical Framework - Image Manager/Archive Actor

description: Date when the series was acquired or started. Represents the calendar day of image capture.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 - General Series Module

description: Human-readable description of the series content, typically including anatomical region, imaging protocol, and acquisition parameters (e.g., 'Chest CT with Contrast Axial 2.5mm').
Globally unique DICOM Series Instance UID conforming to ISO 8824 UID format. This is the business identifier for the series across all systems and institutions.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.5 - UID Encoding Rules

description: Sequential number assigned to this series within the parent study. Used for ordering and display purposes in PACS and radiology workstations.

description: Anatomical region or body part targeted by this imaging protocol (e.g., 'Brain', 'Chest', 'Abdomen and Pelvis', 'Lumbar Spine'). Corresponds to the DICOM Body Part Examined attribute and SNOMED CT anatomical site codes. Used for protocol selection and PACS routing.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,0015); SNOMED CT; RSNA RadLex Anatomy

description: The clinical reason or diagnostic question driving the imaging order for which this protocol is designed (e.g., 'Pulmonary Embolism Rule-Out', 'Abdominal Pain', 'Stroke Workup'). Maps to ICD-10 diagnosis categories and supports appropriateness criteria evaluation.
reference: ICD-10-CM; ACR Appropriateness Criteria; CMS LCD Policies

description: Standard contrast agent dose in milliliters specified for this protocol. May be weight-based (documented separately in patient preparation instructions) or fixed volume. Used for pharmacy preparation and contrast utilization tracking.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; Epic Willow Pharmacy; FDA Drug Labeling

description: Injection flow rate for intravenous contrast in milliliters per second as specified in the protocol. Critical for CT angiography and dynamic phase imaging to achieve optimal vascular enhancement. Null for non-IV contrast protocols.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; RSNA Contrast Administration Guidelines

description: Indicates whether intravenous or oral contrast administration is required for this protocol. True = contrast is required; False = non-contrast study. Drives pre-procedure screening for contrast allergies, renal function checks, and pharmacy preparation.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; Epic Radiant Protocol Library; FDA Drug Safety Communications

description: Route of administration for the contrast agent specified in this protocol (e.g., intravenous for CT angiography, oral for GI studies, intrathecal for myelography). Drives nursing preparation and patient consent requirements.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; Epic Willow Pharmacy Integration; Joint Commission Medication Safety

description: Timestamp when this protocol record was first created in the radiology data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA record retention requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; Joint Commission Record Retention Standards; ISO 27001

description: Radiation dose optimization program under which this protocol was designed or benchmarked. 'image_gently' for pediatric dose reduction, 'image_wisely' for adult dose reduction, 'acr_dir' for ACR Dose Index Registry benchmarking. Supports regulatory reporting and quality improvement programs.
reference: Image Gently Campaign; Image Wisely Program; ACR Dose Index Registry; NCRP Report 172

description: Date from which this protocol version becomes effective and available for clinical use in the RIS. May differ from approval_date if a future activation date is set. Used for version control and ensuring technologists use the current protocol.
reference: Epic Radiant Protocol Library; ACR Practice Parameters; Joint Commission Standards

description: Required fasting duration in hours prior to the examination when fasting_required is True (e.g., 4 hours for contrast CT, 6 hours for PET-CT). Null when fasting is not required.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; ACR Practice Parameters; ASA Fasting Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the patient must fast (NPO) prior to this imaging examination. True = fasting required; False = no fasting required. Drives pre-procedure patient instructions and scheduling communication.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; ACR Practice Parameters; Joint Commission Patient Safety Goals

description: Field of view in millimeters defining the anatomical coverage area for image reconstruction. Determines spatial resolution and the extent of anatomy captured in the image matrix. Applicable to CT and MRI protocols.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,1164); ACR Practice Parameters; RSNA Imaging Guidelines

description: Indicates whether MRI implant safety screening is required before performing this protocol. True = screening required (applicable to all MRI protocols); False = not required. Drives pre-procedure safety questionnaire workflows to identify contraindicated implants, pacemakers, or metallic foreign bodies.
reference: ACR MRI Safety Guidelines; FDA MRI Device Guidance; Joint Commission Patient Safety Goals

description: Peak kilovoltage (kVp) setting for the X-ray tube as specified in this protocol. Determines X-ray beam energy and affects image contrast and patient radiation dose. Applicable to CT, X-ray, and fluoroscopy modalities. Null for MRI and ultrasound.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,0060); ACR-AAPM Technical Standards; NCRP Report 172

description: Timestamp when this protocol record was most recently modified in the radiology data platform. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; Joint Commission Record Retention Standards; ISO 27001

description: Magnetic field strength in Tesla for MRI protocols (e.g., 1.5T, 3.0T, 7.0T). Determines scanner compatibility, image quality characteristics, and specific absorption rate (SAR) limits. Null for non-MRI modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,0087); ACR MRI Safety Guidelines; FDA MRI Device Guidance

description: Milliampere-seconds (mAs) setting specifying the X-ray tube current-time product for this protocol. Directly influences image noise and patient radiation dose. Used in CT and radiography protocols. Null for MRI and ultrasound.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,1152); ACR-AAPM Technical Standards; NCRP Report 172

description: DICOM-standard imaging modality code for which this protocol is defined (e.g., CT = Computed Tomography, MRI = Magnetic Resonance Imaging, XR = X-Ray, US = Ultrasound, PET = Positron Emission Tomography, NM = Nuclear Medicine, MG = Mammography). Drives scheduling to the correct equipment and technologist skill set. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CT|MRI|XR|US|PET|NM|MG|FL|DX|PT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3; ACR Practice Parameters; Epic Radiant Modality Configuration

description: Code used to route completed images to the correct reading worklist, hanging protocol, and storage location within the Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS). Ensures images are delivered to the appropriate radiologist subspecialty queue for interpretation.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.4 Storage SOP Classes; Epic Radiant PACS Integration; ACR Informatics Standards

description: Target patient population for which this protocol is designed. Drives protocol selection logic in the RIS based on patient age and clinical context. Pediatric and neonatal protocols use weight-based dosing and modified acquisition parameters.
reference: ACR Practice Parameters; Image Gently Campaign; Image Wisely Program; CMS Pediatric Quality Measures

description: Standardized patient preparation instructions for this protocol, including fasting requirements (e.g., NPO 4 hours), hydration instructions, medication holds (e.g., hold Metformin for contrast studies), bowel preparation, and breath-hold coaching. Displayed to scheduling staff and communicated to patients prior to the exam.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; Epic Radiant Patient Instructions; Joint Commission Patient Safety Goals

description: Helical pitch factor for CT protocols, defined as table feed per rotation divided by total collimation. Values less than 1.0 indicate overlapping acquisitions (higher dose, better quality); values greater than 1.0 indicate faster coverage with lower dose. Null for non-helical CT and other modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,9311); ACR-AAPM Technical Standards; NCRP Report 172

description: Clinical category classifying the purpose of this imaging protocol. 'diagnostic' for standard clinical workup, 'screening' for population health programs (e.g., lung cancer screening), 'interventional' for image-guided procedures, 'research' for IRB-approved studies, 'emergency' for ED/trauma protocols, 'pediatric' for age-specific protocols.
reference: ACR Practice Parameters; CMS Coverage Determinations; IRB Research Protocols

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the protocol within the Radiology Information System (RIS), such as Epic Radiant or Cerner RadNet. Used for order mapping, PACS integration, and cross-system reference.
reference: Epic Radiant Protocol Library; Cerner RadNet; ACR Practice Parameters

description: Human-readable name of the imaging acquisition protocol as displayed in the RIS and PACS worklist (e.g., 'CT Abdomen Pelvis with Contrast', 'MRI Brain without Contrast'). Used by technologists and radiologists to identify the correct acquisition procedure.
reference: Epic Radiant Protocol Library; ACR Practice Parameters; RSNA RadLex

description: Current lifecycle status of the imaging protocol. 'active' indicates the protocol is approved and available for use; 'draft' indicates pending approval; 'retired' indicates superseded by a newer version; 'under_review' indicates undergoing clinical review.
reference: Epic Radiant Protocol Library; Joint Commission Standards; ACR Practice Parameters

description: MRI pulse sequence type used in this protocol (e.g., 'T1 SE', 'T2 FSE', 'FLAIR', 'DWI', 'GRE', 'SSFP', 'EPI'). Defines the fundamental MRI acquisition technique and determines tissue contrast characteristics. Null for non-MRI modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,9005); ACR MRI Practice Parameters; RSNA RadLex

description: Reference CT Dose Index Volume (CTDIvol) in milligray representing the average radiation dose within the scan volume for this CT protocol. Standardized metric for comparing dose across CT scanners and protocols. Reported to ACR Dose Index Registry and used for DRL benchmarking.
reference: NCRP Report 172; ACR Dose Index Registry; DICOM RDSR Tag (0018,9345); IEC 60601-2-44

business_glossary_term: Reference Radiation Dose - Dose Length Product (DLP) (mGy·cm)
description: Reference Dose Length Product (DLP) in milligray-centimeters representing the expected radiation dose for this CT protocol under standard conditions. Used for radiation dose optimization programs, comparison against Diagnostic Reference Levels (DRLs), and regulatory reporting to CMS and ACR Dose Index Registry.
reference: NCRP Report 172; ACR Dose Index Registry; CMS Radiation Dose Reporting; IAEA Safety Reports Series No. 87; DICOM RDSR

description: RSNA RadLex ontology code for the imaging procedure or anatomy associated with this protocol (e.g., RID10321 for CT of abdomen). Supports standardized terminology in radiology reporting, PACS integration, and research data aggregation.
reference: RSNA RadLex Ontology; ACR Informatics Standards; HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy

description: Image reconstruction algorithm specified for this protocol (e.g., 'Filtered Back Projection', 'Iterative Reconstruction', 'Deep Learning Reconstruction', 'ASIR-V 40%'). Affects image quality, noise characteristics, and radiation dose efficiency. Vendor-specific algorithm names are stored as-is.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0018,1210); ACR-AAPM Technical Standards; RSNA Imaging Informatics

description: Indicates whether pre-procedure renal function assessment (eGFR/creatinine) is required before administering contrast for this protocol. True = renal check required (standard for iodinated and gadolinium contrast protocols); False = not required. Drives lab order triggers in Epic Radiant.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; CMS Contrast Nephropathy Guidelines; Epic Radiant Pre-Procedure Checks

description: Date on which this protocol version was retired or superseded by a newer version. Null for currently active protocols. Used for version lifecycle management and historical audit trails.
reference: Epic Radiant Protocol Library; ACR Practice Parameters; Joint Commission Standards

description: Internal procedure code assigned to this protocol within the Radiology Information System (RIS), such as Epic Radiant or Cerner RadNet. Used for order entry mapping, scheduling, and charge capture integration with the CDM.
reference: Epic Radiant Procedure Dictionary; Cerner RadNet; CMS Charge Description Master (CDM)

description: Estimated acquisition time in seconds for this protocol under standard conditions. Used for scheduling slot allocation in the RIS, patient throughput planning, and breath-hold instruction timing for chest and abdominal studies.
reference: Epic Radiant Scheduling Configuration; ACR Practice Parameters

description: Indicates whether procedural sedation or anesthesia is required or commonly needed for this protocol (e.g., pediatric MRI, claustrophobic patients). True = sedation required; False = no sedation required. Triggers anesthesia scheduling and pre-sedation assessment workflows.
reference: Joint Commission Sedation Standards; CMS Conditions of Participation; ACR Practice Parameters

description: Nominal slice thickness in millimeters for cross-sectional imaging protocols (CT, MRI). Thinner slices provide higher spatial resolution for small structure evaluation; thicker slices reduce noise and radiation dose. Null for planar modalities.
reference: DICOM Standard PS3.3 Tag (0050,0018); ACR Practice Parameters; RSNA Imaging Guidelines

description: Total estimated examination time in minutes including patient preparation, positioning, scanning, and post-processing. Used for scheduling block allocation in the RIS and patient appointment communication. Distinct from scan_duration_estimate_sec which covers acquisition only.
reference: Epic Radiant Scheduling; ACR Practice Parameters; CMS Facility Scheduling Guidelines

description: Version number of the protocol definition following semantic versioning (e.g., '1.0', '2.3', '3.1.2'). Enables tracking of protocol revisions over time and ensures technologists are using the current approved version.
reference: Epic Radiant Protocol Library; ACR Practice Parameters; Joint Commission Standards

description: Unique surrogate primary key for the imaging study record in the lakehouse. Serves as the anchor for all downstream joins to series, reports, dose records, and contrast events.
reference: IHE RAD TF-1; HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy Resource

description: Foreign key linking to scheduling.scheduling_appointment. Business justification: A radiology study is performed during a scheduled appointment. This link supports appointment-to-study conversion reporting, no-show and cancellation impact analysis, and scheduling utilization metric
Reference to the facility or imaging center where the study was performed. Supports multi-site reporting and capacity analytics.
reference: Epic Radiant; CMS Facility NPI; Joint Commission

description: Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Studies fulfill clinical orders. Direct linkage supports charge capture validation (matching performed study to ordered procedure), results reporting workflows where study context requires original or
Reference to the clinician who placed the imaging order. Used for referral tracking, utilization management, and RVU attribution.
reference: Epic Radiant; CMS NPI Registry; ACR Appropriateness Criteria

description: Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: Studies document actual contrast administered during imaging. FK to drug_master enables adverse event tracking, lot number traceability for recalls, billing reconciliation, and REMS compliance reporti

Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Studies are billed using CPT codes for professional and technical components. Links enable RVU calculation, charge capture validation, and revenue integrity audits across PACS and billing systems.
Reference to the patient who is the subject of this imaging study. Core PARTY_REFERENCE linking the study to the Master Patient Index (MPI).
reference: HL7 FHIR Patient; CMS Conditions of Participation; HIPAA §164.514

description: Identifier of the imaging device (scanner, modality unit) used to acquire this study. Corresponds to DICOM Station Name (0008,1010) or Device Serial Number. Used for equipment utilization, maintenance scheduling, and dose calibration tracking.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0008,1010); FDA Medical Device Regulations; Joint Commission Equipment Management

description: Foreign key linking to insurance.health_plan. Business justification: Revenue cycle and denial management require linking each completed radiology study to the governing health plan for reimbursement validation, benefit coverage checks, and payer-specific quality report

business_glossary_term: Icd10 Primary Icd Code Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Studies document ICD-10 codes for medical necessity validation, claims adjudication, and clinical registry reporting. Essential for linking imaging findings to diagnosis codes in quality measures.
Reference to the originating radiology order (CPOE order) that triggered this imaging study. Links the study back to the clinical order workflow in the RIS/EHR.
reference: Epic Radiant Order; Cerner RadNet Order; HL7 FHIR ServiceRequest

description: Foreign key linking to radiology.protocol. Business justification: study currently has acquisition_protocol: STRING (free-text field). Adding imaging_protocol_id FK normalizes this relationship to the protocol master table. Studies are acquired using standardized pro

Foreign key linking to reference.loinc_code. Business justification: FHIR ImagingStudy and HL7 ORU messages require a LOINC code identifying the performed imaging procedure for health information exchange and EHR integration. Radiology informatics experts universally e

Foreign key linking to radiology.modality. Business justification: study currently has modality_code: STRING (descriptive code like CT, MR, XR) and equipment_asset_id FK to facility.equipment_asset. Adding modality_id FK links to the radiology-specific equipment mast

Foreign key linking to insurance.payer. Business justification: Payer-level denial management, remittance reconciliation, and payer-specific turnaround time reporting require each radiology study to be directly associated with the responsible payer. Radiology reve

Foreign key linking to clinical.diagnosis. Business justification: Radiology studies are performed for a primary clinical diagnosis that drives DRG assignment, coding accuracy, and payer reimbursement. The existing icd10_primary_icd_code_id references a code table, n

Foreign key linking to insurance.prior_auth_rule. Business justification: Prior authorization rules govern which imaging studies require pre-approval. Linking each completed study to the applicable prior_auth_rule supports denial prevention audits, prior auth compliance tra

Foreign key linking to claim.prior_authorization. Business justification: Each radiology study is performed under a specific prior authorization. This FK enables PA utilization tracking (approved vs. consumed units), payer audit reconciliation, and denial prevention reporti

Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Radiology studies are performed in specific imaging rooms. Room-level utilization reporting, equipment downtime impact analysis, and infection control investigations (e.g., tracing exposure events) re

Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: External/imported radiology studies must be traceable to the originating org_provider for care coordination, legal accountability, and continuity-of-care workflows. Healthcare domain experts universal

Foreign key linking to supply.udi_record. Business justification: Joint Commission and FDA require implantable device traceability per procedure. Interventional radiology studies use stents, coils, and filters requiring UDI documentation. Linking radiology_study to
Reference to the clinical encounter (inpatient, outpatient, or ED visit) during which this imaging study was ordered and performed.
reference: Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet; HL7 FHIR Encounter

description: RIS-assigned accession number uniquely identifying this imaging study within the facility's Radiology Information System (RIS). Used for order tracking, billing, and PACS worklist management. Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0008,0050).
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0008,0050); IHE RAD Scheduled Workflow; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Anatomical body part or region imaged during the study. Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0018,0015). Used for clinical classification, protocol selection, and radiation dose benchmarking.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0018,0015); SNOMED CT Anatomy; ACR RADLEX

description: Clinical reason or indication for the imaging study as documented by the ordering provider (e.g., 'Rule out pulmonary embolism', 'Evaluate chest pain'). Supports ACR Appropriateness Criteria compliance and CDI workflows.
reference: ACR Appropriateness Criteria; HIPAA §164.514 PHI; HL7 FHIR ServiceRequest.reasonCode; CMS Medical Necessity

description: Indicates whether a contrast agent (e.g., iodinated contrast for CT, gadolinium for MRI) was administered during this study. Drives contrast reaction monitoring, nephrotoxicity risk tracking, and CPT code selection.
reference: ACR Manual on Contrast Media; DICOM PS3.3 (0018,0010); CMS Contrast Billing Guidelines

description: Date and time this imaging study record was first created in the source RIS/PACS system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

Indicates whether the radiologist identified a critical or actionable finding requiring immediate communication to the ordering provider per ACR communication guidelines. Drives critical results notification workflows.
reference: ACR Practice Parameters for Communication of Diagnostic Imaging Findings; Joint Commission NPSG 02.03.01; TJC

description: Date and time the radiologist or radiology staff notified the ordering provider of a critical imaging finding. Required for Joint Commission and ACR compliance documentation. Populated only when critical_finding_flag = True.
reference: Joint Commission NPSG 02.03.01; ACR Practice Parameters for Communication; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Globally unique DICOM Study Instance UID assigned at acquisition. The primary DICOM identifier used by PACS for image retrieval, routing, and federation across imaging systems. Conforms to DICOM PS3.3 attribute (0020,000D).
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0020,000D); IHE RAD TF-2; HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy.identifier

description: Total number of DICOM image instances across all series in this study. Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0020,1208). Used for PACS storage sizing and study completeness checks.

Method by which an externally acquired study was imported into the local PACS (e.g., CD/DVD media, direct DICOM push, HIE exchange, Vendor Neutral Archive). Applicable only when is_external_import = True.
reference: IHE RAD IRWF; ACR Outside Study Management; HL7 FHIR

description: Indicates whether this study was imported from an external facility rather than acquired internally. True = externally sourced study; False = internally acquired. Drives provenance tracking and comparison read workflows.
reference: IHE RAD Import Reconciliation Workflow (IRWF); HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy

description: Indicates whether a STAT (immediate) read was completed within the required turnaround time SLA for urgent/STAT priority studies. Used for quality reporting and radiologist performance monitoring.
reference: ACR Practice Parameters; CMS Radiology Quality Metrics; Joint Commission

description: Anatomical laterality of the body part examined (left, right, bilateral). Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0020,0060). Critical for patient safety, wrong-site prevention, and surgical correlation.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0020,0060); Joint Commission NPSG 01.01.01 Patient Identification; SNOMED CT

description: Date and time the imaging order was received by the radiology department or RIS. Used to calculate order-to-scan turnaround time and measure scheduling efficiency.
reference: Epic Radiant Order Tracking; IHE RAD Scheduled Workflow; ACR Radiology Metrics

description: Logical or physical storage location identifier within the PACS where this study's images are archived (e.g., tier name, storage node, cloud bucket path). Used for image retrieval routing and storage lifecycle management.
reference: IHE RAD TF Image Archive; ACR PACS Standards; DICOM PS3.4 Storage

business_glossary_term: Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Archive Status
description: Current archival status of the study images within the PACS. Indicates whether images have been received, fully archived to long-term storage, retrieved for reading, or purged per retention policy.
reference: IHE RAD TF-1 Image Archive; DICOM PS3.4 Storage SOP Classes; ACR PACS Standards

description: Age of the patient in years at the time of the imaging study. Derived at acquisition time and stored for analytics, pediatric protocol selection, and dose benchmarking without requiring a join to patient demographics.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0010,1010); ACR Dose Index Registry; Pediatric Imaging Guidelines

description: Biological sex of the patient as recorded at the time of the imaging study. Used for protocol selection (e.g., pelvic MRI), dose benchmarking, and population health analytics. Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0010,0040).
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0010,0040); HIPAA §164.514 PHI; HL7 FHIR Patient.gender

description: Clinical priority level assigned to the imaging study at the time of ordering. Drives RIS worklist ordering, scheduling urgency, and turnaround time (TAT) SLA targets.
reference: Epic Radiant Priority; Cerner RadNet Priority; ACR Appropriateness Criteria; IHE RAD Scheduled Workflow

description: Volume CT Dose Index (CTDIvol) in mGy for CT studies, representing the average radiation dose within the scan volume. Used alongside DLP for dose benchmarking against ACR national dose reference levels.
reference: ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR); NCRP Report 172; DICOM PS3.3 Radiation Dose SR; IHE REM Profile

description: Dose-Length Product (DLP) in mGy·cm representing the total radiation dose delivered during this CT or fluoroscopy study. Key metric for radiation dose tracking, ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR) reporting, and patient safety compliance.
reference: ACR Dose Index Registry (DIR); NCRP Report 172; IHE RAD Radiation Exposure Monitoring (REM); DICOM PS3.3 (0040,A124)

description: Clinical department or service line that originated the imaging order (e.g., Emergency Department, Oncology, Orthopedics). Used for utilization analytics, volume reporting by department, and capacity planning.
reference: Epic Radiant Referring Department; Cerner RadNet; CMS Utilization Reporting

description: Date and time the radiologist finalized (signed) the interpretation report. Used to calculate scan-to-report turnaround time (TAT), a key quality and operational KPI.
reference: ACR Practice Parameters; CMS Radiology Quality Reporting; Epic Radiant Report Sign-off

description: Current status of the radiologist's interpretation report for this study. Preliminary reports are available before final sign-off; addendum and corrected statuses track post-signature changes.
reference: ACR Practice Parameters for Communication; HL7 FHIR DiagnosticReport.status; Epic Radiant Report Status

description: Identifies the source Radiology Information System (RIS) from which this study record was ingested. Supports multi-system data lineage, reconciliation, and provenance tracking in the lakehouse.
reference: HL7 FHIR Provenance; IHE RAD TF; Epic Radiant; Cerner RadNet

description: Total number of DICOM series contained within this imaging study. Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0020,1206). Used for PACS storage capacity planning and study completeness validation.
Total storage size of all DICOM image files for this study, expressed in megabytes. Used for PACS storage capacity planning, archival cost allocation, and data lifecycle management.
reference: ACR PACS Standards; IHE RAD TF Image Archive

description: Date and time when image acquisition began for this study. Corresponds to DICOM Study Time (0008,0030). Used for turnaround time (TAT) calculation and operational throughput analytics.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0008,0030); IHE RAD TF; ACR Radiology Metrics

description: Calendar date on which the imaging study was performed. Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0008,0020). Used for scheduling analytics, turnaround time measurement, and regulatory reporting.
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0008,0020); CMS Radiology Reporting Requirements

description: Human-readable description of the imaging study as defined in the RIS/PACS worklist (e.g., 'CT Chest with Contrast', 'MRI Brain without Contrast'). Corresponds to DICOM attribute (0008,1030).
reference: DICOM PS3.3 (0008,1030); Epic Radiant Procedure Description; Cerner RadNet

description: Current workflow status of the imaging study within the RIS/PACS lifecycle. Drives worklist management, reporting queues, and operational dashboards.
reference: IHE RAD Scheduled Workflow; Epic Radiant Study Status; Cerner RadNet; HL7 FHIR ImagingStudy.status

description: Date and time this imaging study record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture.
Unique identifier for the code set version record. Primary key.
Reference to the code_set_version_id of the newer version that replaces this one. Nullable if this is the current active version.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry Versioning, HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Succession

description: The cryptographic hash algorithm used to generate the file_hash for integrity verification: MD5, SHA256, or SHA512.
reference: NIST FIPS 180-4 Secure Hash Standard, ISO/IEC 27001 Cryptographic Controls

description: The name of the reference code set (e.g., ICD-10-CM, CPT, HCPCS, SNOMED CT, LOINC, NDC, RxNorm, DRG Grouper, HL7 Value Set, FHIR Value Set).
reference: HL7 FHIR Terminology Service, CMS Code Set Standards

description: The functional category of the code set: diagnosis (ICD-10), procedure (CPT/HCPCS), drug (NDC/RxNorm), observation (LOINC), terminology (SNOMED CT), or grouper (DRG/APR-DRG).
reference: CMS Code Set Standards, HL7 FHIR CodeSystem

description: The fiscal or calendar year for which this code set version is required for regulatory compliance and reimbursement (e.g., 2024 for FY2024 ICD-10-CM).
reference: CMS ICD-10 Compliance Timeline, HIPAA Transaction Code Set Standards

description: Legal copyright and licensing information for this code set version, including usage restrictions and attribution requirements.
reference: AMA CPT Copyright Policy, SNOMED International Licensing, WHO ICD Copyright

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the jurisdiction or country-specific variant of this code set (e.g., USA for ICD-10-CM, CAN for ICD-10-CA).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes, WHO ICD-10 National Variants

description: The date and time when this code set version record was first created in the enterprise reference data repository.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, DAMA-DMBOK Audit Trail Standards

description: The date and time when this code set version file was downloaded from the source authority.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, DAMA-DMBOK Data Lineage

description: The date on which this code set version becomes valid and available for use in clinical documentation, billing, and reporting.
reference: CMS ICD-10 Implementation Timeline, AMA CPT Release Schedule

description: Cryptographic hash (SHA-256 or MD5) of the source file, used to verify data integrity and detect unauthorized modifications.
reference: NIST FIPS 180-4 Secure Hash Standard, ISO/IEC 27001 Data Integrity Controls

description: The name of the source file or package containing this code set version (e.g., icd10cm_tabular_2024.xml, cpt_codes_2024.csv).
reference: CMS File Naming Standards, HL7 FHIR Package Specification

description: The file format or data structure of the source code set: csv (comma-separated values), xml (extensible markup language), json (JavaScript object notation), fhir (FHIR CodeSystem resource), hl7 (HL7 v2 table), proprietary (vendor-specific format).
reference: HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Format, CMS File Format Standards

business_glossary_term: Is Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Compliant
description: Indicates whether this code set version meets HIPAA transaction and code set standards for electronic healthcare transactions.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR 162 Transaction and Code Set Standards, CMS HIPAA Compliance

description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language of the code set descriptions and labels (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish).
reference: ISO 639-1 Language Codes, HL7 FHIR Language Support

description: The date and time when this code set version record was last modified in the enterprise reference data repository.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, DAMA-DMBOK Metadata Management

description: The licensing model governing the use of this code set: public_domain (freely available), proprietary (commercial license required), open_source (open license with attribution), restricted (limited use agreement).
reference: SNOMED International Affiliate License, AMA CPT License Agreement, Creative Commons Licensing

description: The current status of the ETL process for loading this code set version into the enterprise data platform: pending (queued), in_progress (loading), completed (successfully loaded), failed (load error), validated (post-load quality checks passed).
reference: ISO/IEC 25012 Data Quality Model, DAMA-DMBOK Data Integration

description: The date and time when this code set version was successfully loaded into the enterprise reference data repository.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, DAMA-DMBOK Metadata Management

description: The official date on which the source authority published or released this code set version.
reference: AMA CPT Publication Schedule, WHO ICD-10 Release Calendar, SNOMED International Release Schedule

description: The total number of individual codes or entries contained in this code set version.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry, HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Count

description: Summary of changes, additions, deletions, and updates included in this code set version compared to the previous version.
reference: AMA CPT Release Notes, WHO ICD-10 Update Documentation, SNOMED CT Release Notes

description: The governing body or organization that publishes and maintains this code set (e.g., WHO, AMA, CMS, SNOMED International, Regenstrief Institute, FDA, NLM).
reference: WHO ICD Maintenance, AMA CPT Editorial Panel, SNOMED International, Regenstrief LOINC Committee

description: The official download or reference URL from which this code set version was obtained.
reference: HL7 FHIR Terminology Service, CMS Code Set Downloads

description: The date on which this code set version is no longer valid for new transactions. Nullable for versions that remain active indefinitely.
reference: CMS Code Set Sunset Policy, HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Expiration

description: The primary business domain(s) for which this code set version is intended: clinical (EHR documentation), billing (claims and revenue cycle), research (clinical trials and studies), quality (HEDIS/quality reporting), or all (enterprise-wide).
reference: HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Use Context, CMS Quality Reporting Standards

description: The outcome of post-load data quality validation: not_validated (validation not yet performed), passed (all quality checks passed), failed (critical validation errors detected), warning (non-critical issues identified).
reference: ISO/IEC 25012 Data Quality Model, AHRQ Data Quality Framework

description: The date and time when data quality validation was performed on this code set version.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format, ISO/IEC 25012 Data Quality Model

description: The official version number or release identifier assigned by the source authority (e.g., 2024, v3.5, FY2024, 20240101).
reference: WHO ICD-10 Versioning, AMA CPT Release Notes, SNOMED International Release Notes

description: Current lifecycle status of this code set version: draft (not yet effective), active (currently in use), superseded (replaced by newer version), retired (no longer valid), deprecated (scheduled for retirement).
reference: HL7 FHIR Publication Status, ISO/IEC 11179 Metadata Registry

description: Unique identifier for the CPT code record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: CPT codes are published annually by the AMA with version-specific RVU values and payment amounts. The cpt_code table tracks effective_date and termination_date but lacks explicit version tracking. Add
Maximum patient age (in years) for which the CPT code is clinically appropriate. Null if no upper age restriction applies. Used for claims editing and clinical decision support.
Minimum patient age (in years) for which the CPT code is clinically appropriate. Null if no lower age restriction applies. Used for claims editing and clinical decision support.
Base unit value assigned to anesthesia CPT codes, representing the complexity and risk of the anesthesia service. Used in anesthesia billing formulas along with time units and modifying factors.
Grouping of clinically related CPT codes, such as all codes for a specific type of surgery or diagnostic test. Used for clinical pathway design, order sets, and utilization management.
Dollar amount multiplied by total RVU to calculate Medicare payment. Updated annually by CMS. Used in revenue cycle forecasting and contract modeling.

Five-digit numeric code published by the American Medical Association (AMA) that identifies medical, surgical, and diagnostic services. This is the authoritative business identifier used on CMS-1500 claims, in CPOE systems, and for professional billing.

Classification of the CPT code. Category I codes describe established procedures and services. Category II codes are supplemental tracking codes for performance measurement. Category III codes are temporary codes for emerging technology, services, and procedures.

Current lifecycle status of the CPT code. Active codes are valid for billing. Inactive codes have been retired but retained for historical claims. Deleted codes are no longer recognized. Pending codes are scheduled for future activation.
Timestamp when this CPT code record was first created in the enterprise reference data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

business_glossary_term: CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) Code Effective Date
description: Date on which the CPT code became valid for use in billing and clinical documentation. Typically January 1 of the release year for annual updates.
Indicates whether the CPT code is typically performed in a facility setting (hospital, ASC) or non-facility setting (physician office). Affects practice expense RVU calculation.
Complete clinical description of the procedure or service, including anatomical site, approach, and any modifiers or conditions. Used for clinical documentation, coding review, and CDI workflows.
Indicates whether the procedure is anatomically or clinically specific to male or female patients. Used for claims editing and clinical decision support.

Number of days of post-operative care included in the procedure payment. 000 = endoscopic or minor procedure with 0-day follow-up. 010 = minor procedure with 10-day follow-up. 090 = major surgery with 90-day follow-up. XXX = global concept does not apply. YYY = carrier determines global period. ZZZ = add-on code. MMM = maternity codes with global period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 000|010|090|XXX|YYY|ZZZ|MMM — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Relative Value Unit representing the professional liability insurance component. Reflects the relative risk and cost of malpractice insurance for the service.
Maximum number of units of service that CMS considers medically reasonable for a single patient on a single date of service. Used for claims editing and fraud detection.

Indicates whether bilateral surgery, assistant surgeon, or co-surgeon modifiers are applicable. 0 = not subject to modifier rules. 1 = bilateral surgery applies. 2 = assistant surgeon may not be paid. 3 = assistant surgeon and co-surgeon rules apply. 9 = concept does not apply.

Indicates payment adjustment rules when multiple procedures are performed in the same session. 0 = no payment adjustment. 1 = standard payment adjustment applies. 2 = bilateral adjustment applies. 3 = assistant surgeon adjustment. 4 = diagnostic imaging adjustment. 5 = therapy adjustment. 9 = concept does not apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 0|1|2|3|4|5|9 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

National average Medicare payment amount for the CPT code, calculated as total RVU multiplied by the conversion factor. Actual payment varies by geographic locality. Used for budgeting and benchmarking.

business_glossary_term: NCCI (National Correct Coding Initiative) Edit Indicator
description: Indicates whether this CPT code is subject to National Correct Coding Initiative edits, which prevent inappropriate code combinations from being billed together. Used for claims scrubbing and compliance.

Level of physician supervision required for the service to be billable. Direct = physician must be present in the room. General = physician must be immediately available. Personal = physician must perform the service. None = no supervision required.
Comma-separated list of valid CMS Place of Service codes where this CPT code may be performed and billed. Used for claims editing and compliance validation.
Relative Value Unit representing the practice expense component, including clinical staff, medical supplies, and equipment costs. Varies by facility vs non-facility setting.

High-level section of the CPT code set to which this code belongs, such as Evaluation and Management, Anesthesia, Surgery, Radiology, Pathology and Laboratory, or Medicine. Used for reporting and analytics grouping.
Abbreviated description of the procedure or service, typically 28 characters or fewer, used in billing systems and charge capture interfaces for space-constrained displays.
Name of the operational system or reference data vendor from which this CPT code record was sourced, such as AMA CPT Database, 3M Coding and Reimbursement, or Epic Resolute Professional Billing.
Unique identifier for this CPT code in the source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting integration issues.
Subsection within the CPT section, providing more granular classification such as Digestive System, Cardiovascular System, or Office Visits. Used for clinical analytics and charge capture workflows.
Indicates whether the service can be delivered via telemedicine or telehealth platforms and remain eligible for reimbursement. Updated based on CMS and payer policies.

business_glossary_term: CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) Code Termination Date
description: Date on which the CPT code was retired or deleted from the active code set. Null for currently active codes. Used to prevent billing of obsolete codes and support historical claims adjudication.
Sum of work RVU, practice expense RVU, and malpractice RVU. Used to calculate Medicare reimbursement and benchmark physician productivity.
Timestamp when this CPT code record was last modified in the enterprise reference data system. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

Relative Value Unit representing the physician work component, including time, technical skill, physical effort, mental effort, and judgment required to perform the service. Used in MIPS reporting and physician compensation models.
Unique identifier for the DRG record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: DRG grouper systems (MS-DRG, APR-DRG, AP-DRG) are versioned annually by CMS with fiscal-year-specific relative weights and payment rules. The drg table has grouper_version (STRING), fiscal_year (INT),
The arithmetic mean (average) length of stay in days for cases in this DRG. Used for benchmarking and operational performance analysis.
Indicates whether this DRG is included in bundled payment initiatives such as the Bundled Payments for Care Improvement (BPCI) program or Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) model.
reference: CMS Bundled Payments for Care Improvement (BPCI), CMS Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR)

description: Grouping of related DRGs into clinical families for service line analysis (e.g., 'Cardiovascular', 'Orthopedic', 'Neurology'). Facilitates portfolio management and strategic planning.

business_glossary_term: Complication or Comorbidity (CC) / Major Complication or Comorbidity (MCC) Level
