description: Indicates the severity level of complications or comorbidities included in this DRG definition: without CC/MCC, with CC (moderate complications), or with MCC (major complications). Drives relative weight differentiation.
The dollar threshold above which a case qualifies for additional outlier payment. Calculated as: (DRG payment × fixed loss ratio) + fixed loss amount.
The timestamp when this DRG record was first created in the reference data system. Used for data lineage and historical analysis.
The official numeric code assigned to this DRG by the grouper system (e.g., 470, 871). Used as the business identifier for reimbursement and case mix reporting.
Extended narrative description of the DRG, including clinical context, typical procedures, and coding guidance. Used for clinical documentation improvement (CDI) and coder education.
Classification of the DRG as medical (non-surgical), surgical, or procedure-based. Used for service line segmentation and resource planning.
The date on which this DRG definition and relative weight became effective (typically October 1 of the fiscal year).
The date on which this DRG definition and relative weight expire (typically September 30 of the fiscal year). Null for currently active DRGs.
The geometric mean length of stay in days for cases in this DRG, calculated from national claims data. Used as the threshold for identifying outlier cases and calculating outlier payments.
The DRG grouper classification system under which this DRG is defined: MS-DRG (Medicare Severity), AP-DRG (All Patient), APR-DRG (All Patient Refined), or IR-DRG (International Refined).
reference: CMS MS-DRG Grouper, 3M Health Information Systems APR-DRG

description: The timestamp when this DRG record was last modified in the reference data system. Used for change tracking and audit trails.
The national average total charges (not payments) for cases in this DRG. Used for charge benchmarking and cost-to-charge ratio analysis.
The national average Medicare payment amount for this DRG, calculated as: national base rate × relative weight. Used for benchmarking and financial forecasting.
The total number of Medicare cases nationally that grouped to this DRG in the most recent fiscal year. Used for prevalence analysis and market sizing.
Indicates whether this DRG is classified as an OR procedure DRG, which affects payment and resource allocation.

Indicates whether this DRG is subject to the post-acute care transfer policy, which adjusts payment when a patient is discharged to a post-acute care setting (SNF, IRF, LTCH, HHA) before the geometric mean LOS.
The ending ICD-10-CM diagnosis code in the range of principal diagnoses that can group to this DRG.
reference: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines, MS-DRG Grouper Logic

description: The starting ICD-10-CM diagnosis code in the range of principal diagnoses that can group to this DRG. Used for grouper logic validation and clinical documentation improvement.
reference: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines, MS-DRG Grouper Logic

description: Indicates whether this DRG requires the presence of a specific ICD-10-PCS procedure code to group (true for surgical DRGs, false for medical DRGs).
Indicates whether this DRG is associated with specific quality measures under the Hospital Value-Based Purchasing (VBP) or Hospital-Acquired Condition (HAC) Reduction Programs.
Indicates whether this DRG is included in the Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program (HRRP) and subject to payment penalties for excess readmissions.

The DRG relative weight representing the average resource intensity for cases in this DRG compared to the national average (1.0000). Used to calculate expected reimbursement: base rate × relative weight.
The system of record from which this DRG definition was sourced (e.g., '3M Grouper', 'Epic Resolute', 'Cerner Revenue Cycle'). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Indicates whether this DRG has special payment rules or adjustments (e.g., new technology add-on payments, device-intensive procedures).
The full descriptive title of the DRG (e.g., 'Major Hip and Knee Joint Replacement or Reattachment of Lower Extremity with MCC').

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Code ID
description: Unique identifier for the HCPCS code record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: HCPCS Level II codes are maintained by CMS with annual updates and quarterly releases. The hcpcs_code table has version_year (INT) and status attributes that should be normalized to code_set_version f
Indicates any age-based coverage restrictions for this HCPCS code (e.g., pediatric only, adult only, age 65+). Defines patient age eligibility criteria.
The base unit value assigned to anesthesia-related HCPCS codes, used in calculating anesthesia reimbursement. Null for non-anesthesia codes.
reference: CMS Physician Fee Schedule, ASA Relative Value Guide

description: Indicates the payment methodology for this HCPCS code when performed in an Ambulatory Surgical Center setting. Defines whether the code is separately payable, bundled, or excluded from ASC payment.
Indicates whether an assistant surgeon is allowed for this HCPCS code and the applicable payment percentage for assistant surgeon services.
Indicates whether this HCPCS code is eligible for bilateral surgery payment adjustment when performed on both sides of the body during the same operative session.
Indicates whether co-surgeons are allowed for this HCPCS code, where two surgeons perform distinct portions of a complex procedure.

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Code Type
description: Classification of the code lifecycle: permanent codes are established in the annual update, temporary codes are used for emerging technology or services, deleted codes are no longer valid.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: Indicates whether the HCPCS code is covered by Medicare: covered codes are reimbursable, not_covered codes are excluded, carrier_discretion allows local Medicare Administrative Contractor (MAC) determination, bundled codes are included in other services, conditional codes require specific clinical criteria.
reference: CMS National Coverage Determinations (NCD), CMS Local Coverage Determinations (LCD)

description: Indicates whether this HCPCS code requires specific ICD-10 diagnosis codes for coverage. True if diagnosis-based medical necessity criteria apply, false otherwise.
reference: CMS National Coverage Determinations (NCD), CMS Local Coverage Determinations (LCD)

description: Indicates whether this HCPCS code represents durable medical equipment as defined by Medicare. True for DME items (typically E-codes), false for other categories.
Indicates whether this HCPCS code represents a pharmaceutical drug or biological product (typically J-codes). True for drug codes, false for equipment, supplies, and services.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Effective Date
description: The date on which this HCPCS code became valid and available for use in billing and claims submission.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: The maximum frequency at which this HCPCS code can be billed (e.g., once per year, once per lifetime, twice per month). Defines time-based coverage restrictions.
reference: CMS National Coverage Determinations (NCD), CMS Local Coverage Determinations (LCD)

description: Indicates any gender-based restrictions for this HCPCS code. Some codes are specific to male or female anatomy; most have no restriction.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: The number of days of post-operative care included in the payment for this HCPCS code (e.g., 000, 010, 090, XXX, YYY, ZZZ). Defines the global surgical package period.
reference: CMS Physician Fee Schedule, Global Surgery Policy

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Category
description: The major category of the HCPCS code based on the first letter: A=Transportation/Medical Supplies, B=Enteral/Parenteral, C=Outpatient PPS, D=Dental, E=DME, G=Temporary Procedures, H=Alcohol/Drug, J=Drugs, K=Temporary Codes, L=Orthotics/Prosthetics, M=Medical Services, P=Pathology/Lab, Q=Temporary Codes, R=Diagnostic Radiology, S=Private Payer, T=State Medicaid, V=Vision/Hearing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|E|G|H|J|K|L|M|P|Q|R|S|T|V — 17 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Code
description: The five-character alphanumeric HCPCS Level II code (e.g., E0601, J1234). First character is always a letter (A-V), followed by four digits. Used to identify durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, supplies, drugs, and non-physician services.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: The percentage of the total payment allocated to intraoperative services for this HCPCS code within the global surgical package.
The date on which this HCPCS code record was last modified in the reference data system, reflecting the most recent change to code attributes, descriptions, or coverage indicators.

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Long Description
description: Full detailed description of the HCPCS code, providing comprehensive information about the item, service, drug, or supply represented by the code.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: Indicates whether a modifier is required when billing this HCPCS code. True if a modifier is mandatory for proper claims adjudication, false otherwise.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: Indicates whether and how payment adjustments apply when this HCPCS code is billed with other procedures on the same date of service. Defines reduction percentages for subsequent procedures.
reference: CMS Physician Fee Schedule, Multiple Procedure Payment Reduction (MPPR) Policy

description: Indicates whether this HCPCS code has an associated NDC crosswalk mapping. True for drug codes that map to specific NDC products, false otherwise.

business_glossary_term: OPPS (Outpatient Prospective Payment System) Payment Indicator
description: Indicates the payment status for this HCPCS code under the Hospital Outpatient Prospective Payment System. Defines whether the code is separately payable, packaged, or excluded from OPPS payment.
Indicates any restrictions on where this HCPCS code may be performed or billed (e.g., inpatient only, outpatient only, office only). Defines valid place of service codes.
reference: CMS Place of Service Codes, 42 CFR 414

description: The percentage of the total payment allocated to postoperative services for this HCPCS code within the global surgical package.
The percentage of the total payment allocated to preoperative services for this HCPCS code within the global surgical package.

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Pricing Indicator
description: Indicates the pricing methodology for the HCPCS code: fee_schedule uses CMS published rates, asc uses Ambulatory Surgical Center payment, reasonable_charge uses historical pricing, not_priced has no established rate, contractor_priced is determined by local MAC.
reference: CMS Physician Fee Schedule, CMS ASC Payment System

description: Indicates whether this HCPCS code requires prior authorization from the payer before services can be rendered. True if pre-approval is required, false otherwise.
Indicates whether this HCPCS code has separate professional and technical components (e.g., for radiology and pathology services). Defines billing options for split or global billing.
The maximum allowable quantity of this HCPCS code that can be billed per claim, per day, or per time period as defined by coverage policy. Null if no limit applies.
reference: CMS National Coverage Determinations (NCD), CMS Local Coverage Determinations (LCD)

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Short Description
description: Abbreviated description of the HCPCS code, typically 28 characters or less, used for display on claims and billing screens.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: The name of the source system or data feed from which this HCPCS code record was obtained (e.g., CMS HCPCS Annual Update, 3M Coding System, internal reference data management system).
Indicates whether team surgery is allowed for this HCPCS code, where multiple surgeons of different specialties are medically necessary.
Indicates whether this HCPCS code includes a technical component representing facility resources, equipment, and supplies.

business_glossary_term: HCPCS (Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System) Termination Date
description: The date on which this HCPCS code was discontinued or replaced. Null for currently active codes.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

description: The standard unit of measure for billing this HCPCS code (e.g., each, milligram, milliliter, hour, day, unit). Defines the quantity increment for claims submission.
reference: CMS HCPCS Level II Coding Manual

business_glossary_term: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) Code ID
description: Unique identifier for the ICD code record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: ICD-10-CM and ICD-10-PCS codes are published annually by CMS with version-specific effective/expiration dates. The icd_code table has version (STRING) and status attributes that should be normalized t
The maximum patient age (in years) for which this ICD code is clinically appropriate. Used for coding validation and quality checks. Null if no maximum age restriction applies.
The minimum patient age (in years) for which this ICD code is clinically appropriate. Used for coding validation and quality checks. Null if no minimum age restriction applies.

Indicates whether this ICD code is valid for billing and reimbursement purposes. Only billable codes may be submitted on claims to payers. Non-billable codes are typically higher-level category codes used for classification only.
Indicates whether this diagnosis code is classified as a Complication or Comorbidity (CC) for DRG assignment and severity adjustment purposes.

The ICD-10 chapter to which this code belongs, representing the highest-level clinical category (e.g., Chapter 1: Certain infectious and parasitic diseases, Chapter 9: Diseases of the circulatory system).

business_glossary_term: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) Chapter Code Range
description: The alphanumeric range defining the chapter boundaries (e.g., A00-B99 for infectious diseases). Used for hierarchical grouping and reporting.

business_glossary_term: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) Code Type
description: Indicates whether this code represents a diagnosis (ICD-10-CM) or a procedure (ICD-10-PCS).
The date and time when this ICD code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The date on which this ICD code became valid for use in clinical documentation, billing, and reporting. Aligns with annual CMS ICD-10 update cycles (typically October 1).
Indicates whether this code represents the underlying etiology or cause of a condition and should be sequenced before manifestation codes per ICD-10-CM coding conventions.
The date on which this ICD code was retired or replaced and is no longer valid for use. Null for currently active codes.
Indicates whether this ICD code is applicable only to male patients, only to female patients, or to both. Used for clinical validation and coding accuracy.
Indicates whether this diagnosis code represents a Hospital-Acquired Condition (HAC) as defined by CMS. HACs may result in payment penalties under the Hospital-Acquired Condition Reduction Program.
The three-character category code representing a group of related conditions or procedures within a chapter (e.g., A01 for typhoid and paratyphoid fevers).
The full ICD-10-CM diagnosis code or ICD-10-PCS procedure code as published by WHO and maintained by CMS. Format follows alphanumeric pattern with optional decimal subdivision (e.g., A01.0, Z99.89).

business_glossary_term: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) Code Long Description
description: Full clinical description of the diagnosis or procedure code, providing comprehensive detail for clinical documentation, coding accuracy, and medical record completeness.
Indicates whether this code represents a manifestation of an underlying disease and must be sequenced after the underlying condition code per ICD-10-CM coding conventions.

Indicates whether this diagnosis code is classified as a Major Complication or Comorbidity (MCC) for DRG assignment and severity adjustment purposes. MCCs have greater impact on reimbursement than standard CCs.
Indicates whether this diagnosis code is exempt from the CMS Present on Admission (POA) reporting requirement for inpatient claims. POA-exempt codes do not require a POA indicator on UB-04 claims.

business_glossary_term: Replacement ICD (International Classification of Diseases) Code
description: The ICD code that replaces this code if the code has been retired or superseded. Used for code mapping and historical claims reprocessing.

business_glossary_term: ICD (International Classification of Diseases) Code Short Description
description: Abbreviated clinical description of the diagnosis or procedure code, typically used in constrained display contexts such as billing forms and summary reports.

business_glossary_term: SNOMED CT (Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms) Mapping
description: The corresponding SNOMED CT concept identifier(s) mapped to this ICD code, enabling interoperability with clinical terminology systems and EHR platforms.
reference: SNOMED International ICD-10 to SNOMED CT Map

description: The four-character subcategory code providing additional clinical specificity within a category (e.g., A01.0 for typhoid fever).
Indicates whether this diagnosis code is prohibited from being used as the principal diagnosis on an inpatient claim per CMS coding guidelines.
The date and time when this ICD code record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Indicates whether this code is valid for use in clinical documentation and coding. Codes may be invalid due to retirement, replacement, or administrative reasons.
Unique identifier for the terminology crosswalk mapping record.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: Crosswalk mappings are version-specific - ICD-10-CM to SNOMED CT mappings change with each release of both terminologies. The crosswalk table has source_code_system_version (STRING), target_code_syste
Indicates whether the mapping is an approximate or inexact match (True) requiring clinical review, or an exact semantic match (False).
Indicates whether the target code is one of multiple valid choices for the source code, requiring clinical judgment to select the most appropriate target.
Indicates whether this mapping requires multiple target codes to fully represent the source code (True) or is a single 1:1 or 1:0 mapping (False).
The date and time when this crosswalk mapping record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the mapping is valid in one direction (forward: source to target only, backward: target to source only) or both directions (bidirectional).
reference: CMS ICD-10 General Equivalence Mappings (GEMs) Directionality Rules

description: The date on which this crosswalk mapping becomes valid and available for use.
reference: HL7 FHIR ConceptMap.date, CMS Code Set Effective Dates

description: The most recent date on which this mapping was reviewed and validated by clinical or coding subject matter experts.
reference: Internal Terminology Governance and Quality Assurance

description: Grouping identifier for related mappings that should be considered together (e.g., all mappings for a specific clinical domain or use case).
Numeric ranking indicating the preferred order when multiple target codes are valid for a single source code (lower numbers indicate higher priority).
reference: UMLS Mapping Rank, Internal Terminology Governance

description: The authoritative source or organization that published or validated this mapping (e.g., CMS, NLM, SNOMED International, Regenstrief Institute, internal clinical terminology team).
reference: HL7 FHIR ConceptMap.publisher, UMLS Source Vocabulary Authority

description: The intended use case or business purpose for this mapping (e.g., claims submission, clinical documentation, quality reporting, SDOH screening, interoperability, legacy system migration).
The confidence or precision level of the mapping: exact (perfect semantic match), high (strong clinical equivalence), moderate (acceptable with context), low (weak or conditional match).
reference: UMLS Mapping Quality Indicators, CMS GEMs Quality Flags

description: Textual description of any conditional logic, clinical context, or business rules that govern when and how this mapping should be applied.
reference: HL7 FHIR ConceptMap.group.element.target.comment, UMLS Mapping Notes

description: The semantic relationship between source and target codes: equivalent (1:1 exact match), broader (source is more specific than target), narrower (source is more general than target), related (associated but not hierarchical), inexact (approximate match), unmatched (no suitable target).
Indicates whether the source code has no suitable equivalent in the target code system (True) or a valid mapping exists (False).
reference: CMS ICD-10 GEMs No Map Flag

description: Free-text field for additional context, clinical guidance, or special instructions related to this mapping.
Identifies specific clinical or billing scenarios where this mapping applies (e.g., inpatient vs outpatient, primary vs secondary diagnosis).
The specific code value in the source code system being mapped from (e.g., E11.9 for ICD-10, 73211009 for SNOMED CT).
Human-readable display text or description for the source code.
The originating terminology or code system from which the mapping is derived (e.g., ICD-10-CM, SNOMED CT, CPT, LOINC, NDC, RxNorm).
reference: HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Resource, UMLS Metathesaurus

description: The operational system or data source from which this crosswalk mapping was originally loaded or derived (e.g., Epic Caboodle, 3M Encoder, NLM UMLS, internal terminology service).
reference: Internal Data Lineage and Source System Registry

description: The specific code value in the target code system being mapped to (e.g., 73211009 for SNOMED CT, 99213 for CPT).
Human-readable display text or description for the target code.
The destination terminology or code system to which the mapping is made (e.g., ICD-10-CM, SNOMED CT, CPT, LOINC, NDC, RxNorm).
reference: HL7 FHIR CodeSystem Resource, UMLS Metathesaurus

description: The date on which this crosswalk mapping is no longer valid or has been superseded. Null indicates the mapping is still active.
reference: HL7 FHIR ConceptMap.date (end), CMS Code Set Termination Dates

description: The date and time when this crosswalk mapping record was last modified.
The number of times this crosswalk mapping has been applied in production systems, used for mapping quality assessment and optimization.
reference: Internal Analytics and Terminology Usage Tracking

description: The name or identifier of the clinical or coding expert who last validated this mapping.
reference: Internal Terminology Governance and Quality Assurance

business_glossary_term: LOINC (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes) Code ID
description: Unique identifier for the LOINC code record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: LOINC codes are maintained by Regenstrief Institute with biannual releases. The loinc_code table has version_first_released (STRING), version_last_changed (STRING), and status attributes that should b
The high-level category or domain of the observation (e.g., CHEM=Chemistry, HEM/BC=Hematology/Blood Count, MICRO=Microbiology, RAD=Radiology, VITALS=Vital Signs).
The substance or entity being measured or observed (e.g., Glucose, Hemoglobin, Creatinine). This is the analyte, the target of the measurement.
A patient-friendly, lay-language name for the observation or test, used in patient portals and consumer-facing applications.
The date and time when this LOINC code record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.
The date when this LOINC code was deprecated or retired. Null if the code is still active.
The preferred display name for this LOINC code in the current implementation. May be customized by the organization while maintaining the standard LOINC number.
The date when this LOINC code became active and available for use in clinical systems.

business_glossary_term: Example UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure) Units
description: The standardized UCUM representation of the example units. Used for interoperability and unit conversion.
reference: UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure)

description: Common units of measure for this observation (e.g., mg/dL, mmol/L, %). Provides guidance for result reporting.
reference: Regenstrief Institute LOINC Standard, UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure)

description: Copyright or licensing information for LOINC codes that incorporate third-party content (e.g., SNOMED CT, CPT). Required for compliance with external terminology licenses.
reference: Regenstrief Institute LOINC Standard, Third-Party Terminology Licenses

business_glossary_term: HL7 (Health Level Seven) Field Subfield ID
description: The HL7 v2.x message field identifier where this LOINC code is typically used (e.g., OBX-3 for observation identifier). Supports HL7 message mapping.

business_glossary_term: HL7 (Health Level Seven) Version 3 Code System OID (Object Identifier)
description: The HL7 v3 object identifier for the LOINC code system (typically 2.16.840.1.113883.6.1). Used in CDA documents and HL7 v3 messages.
Boolean flag indicating whether this LOINC code is currently active and available for use in the organization. True=active, False=inactive or deprecated locally.
The date when this LOINC code mapping was last reviewed and verified by clinical informatics or laboratory staff. Supports ongoing terminology governance.
Organization-specific internal code or identifier mapped to this LOINC code. Used for legacy system integration and local terminology management.

business_glossary_term: LOINC (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes) Number
description: The unique LOINC code identifier assigned by Regenstrief Institute. Format: numeric-numeric (e.g., 2345-7). This is the authoritative external identifier for the observation or test.
The full, human-readable display name for the LOINC code. This is the primary display term used in clinical systems and reports.
The method or technique used to make the observation or measurement (e.g., Electrophoresis, Immunoassay, Microscopy). May be null if method is not specified.
Indicates whether this LOINC code is used for ordering tests, recording observations, or both. Order=orderable panel or test, Observation=result only, Both=can be used for ordering and results.
Indicates if this LOINC code represents a panel (group of related tests), battery (predefined test group), or set (collection of observations). Null for individual observations.
The characteristic or attribute of the analyte being measured (e.g., Mass Concentration, Substance Concentration, Presence, Volume).
The scale of measurement for the observation result. Qn=Quantitative (numeric), Ord=Ordinal (ranked), Nom=Nominal (named categories), Nar=Narrative (free text), Doc=Document (attached file).
An abbreviated display name for the LOINC code, used when space is limited in user interfaces or reports.
The system of record from which this LOINC code mapping was sourced (e.g., Epic Beaker, Cerner PathNet, MEDITECH LIS). Supports data lineage and troubleshooting.
The source instrument or questionnaire from which the survey question originates (e.g., PHQ-9, PROMIS, CAHPS).
For LOINC codes representing survey or assessment questions, this field contains the full question text as presented to the patient or clinician.
The specimen or body system from which the observation was made (e.g., Blood, Serum, Urine, Plasma, Cerebrospinal fluid).
The time dimension of the measurement (e.g., Point in time, 24 hour, 8 hour). Indicates whether the observation is a single point measurement or collected over a time interval.
The date and time when this LOINC code record was last modified. Audit trail field.
Unique identifier for the NDC drug product record in the enterprise data warehouse.

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: NDC drug products are maintained by the FDA with weekly updates to the NDC Directory. The ndc_drug table has ndc_status attribute and marketing_start_date/marketing_end_date that track product lifecyc

Foreign key linking to reference.snomed_concept. Business justification: ndc_drug has a snomed_ct_code STRING field storing a SNOMED CT code for the drug product. snomed_concept is the authoritative SNOMED CT master catalog in this domain. Replacing the denormalized string
The chemical substance or compound responsible for the therapeutic effect of the drug.
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory, UNII

description: The FDA application number associated with the drug approval (e.g., NDA number, ANDA number, BLA number).
The WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical classification code, used internationally for drug classification.
reference: WHO Collaborating Centre for Drug Statistics Methodology

description: Indicates whether the drug is a biosimilar product, highly similar to an already-approved biological product.
Indicates whether the drug carries an FDA black box warning, the strictest warning for serious or life-threatening risks.
The controlled substance schedule classification assigned by the DEA (CI through CV), indicating the drug's potential for abuse and accepted medical use. Empty if not a controlled substance.
The physical form in which the drug is administered (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution, cream, inhaler).
The date on which this NDC drug record became effective in the enterprise reference data system.
The date on which this NDC drug record is no longer valid or was superseded. Null if currently active.

business_glossary_term: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) Medication Code
description: The standardized medication code used in FHIR MedicationRequest and Medication resources for interoperability.
Indicates whether the drug is included in the organization's formulary and any restrictions or preferences applied.
The Medi-Span Generic Product Identifier, a 14-character hierarchical classification code used in pharmacy systems.
Indicates whether the drug is classified as a high-alert medication by ISMP, requiring special handling and verification procedures.
The name of the company or entity that manufactures, repackages, or distributes the drug product.
The regulatory pathway under which the drug was approved (e.g., NDA, ANDA, BLA, OTC Monograph).
The date on which the drug product was discontinued or withdrawn from the market. Null if still active.
The date on which the drug product was first marketed or made available for distribution.
The 11-digit National Drug Code assigned by the FDA, uniquely identifying the labeler, product, and package size. Format: 5-4-2 (labeler-product-package).
The generic or non-proprietary name of the drug, typically the active ingredient name established by the United States Adopted Names (USAN) Council.
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory, USAN

description: The therapeutic equivalence code from the FDA Orange Book, indicating whether a generic drug is therapeutically equivalent to the brand-name drug.
Indicates whether the drug is available for purchase without a prescription.
A textual description of the package size and configuration (e.g., bottle of 100 tablets, box of 10 vials).
The numeric quantity of units contained in the package.
The unit of measure for the package quantity (e.g., tablet, capsule, mL, vial).
The FDA pregnancy category classification indicating the risk of fetal harm (A=safest, X=contraindicated, N=not classified). Note: FDA replaced this with narrative labeling in 2015.
reference: FDA Pregnancy and Lactation Labeling Rule

description: The full product name as registered with the FDA, including strength and dosage form.
The brand or trade name under which the drug is marketed to consumers and healthcare providers.
The timestamp when this NDC drug record was first created in the enterprise data warehouse.
The timestamp when this NDC drug record was last updated in the enterprise data warehouse.
Indicates whether the drug requires refrigerated storage to maintain stability and efficacy.
The path by which the drug is administered into the body (e.g., oral, intravenous, topical, subcutaneous, intramuscular).
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory, HL7 FHIR RouteOfAdministration

description: The RxNorm Concept Unique Identifier assigned by the National Library of Medicine, providing a normalized drug concept mapping for interoperability.
The normalized drug name as defined in RxNorm, representing the clinical drug concept.
The source vocabulary from which the RxNorm concept was derived (e.g., RXNORM, VANDF, MMSL, NDDF).
Indicates whether the RxNorm concept is suppressed from use (N=not suppressed, Y=suppressed, O=obsolete, E=entry term).
The RxNorm term type classification (e.g., IN for ingredient, BN for brand name, SCD for semantic clinical drug, GPCK for generic pack).
The concentration or potency of the active ingredient(s) in the drug product, expressed as a numeric value with unit (e.g., 500 mg, 10 mg/mL).
The unit of measure for the drug strength (e.g., mg, mL, mcg, IU, %).
reference: FDA National Drug Code Directory, UCUM

description: The therapeutic or pharmacological classification of the drug (e.g., antibiotic, antihypertensive, analgesic).
reference: AHFS Drug Information, VA Drug Class

description: The FDA Unique Ingredient Identifier code for the active ingredient, used for substance identification.
Indicates whether the drug product is a vaccine used for immunization.
Primary key for npi_registry

Foreign key linking to reference.code_set_version. Business justification: The NPI registry is a versioned CMS dataset (NPPES) that enterprises refresh periodically. Linking npi_registry to code_set_version tracks which snapshot/load of the NPPES data is active, enabling lif
The first name of the authorized official for the organizational provider (Type 2 NPIs only). Used for credentialing verification and organizational accountability.

The last name of the authorized official who is legally responsible for the organizational provider (Type 2 NPIs only). This individual has authority to bind the organization and attest to the accuracy of NPI application information.
The middle name of the authorized official for the organizational provider (Type 2 NPIs only). Optional field for complete identification.
The telephone number of the authorized official for the organizational provider. Used for credentialing verification, attestation confirmation, and regulatory inquiries.

The job title or position of the authorized official within the organization (e.g., CEO, CFO, Administrator, President, Medical Director). Used to verify organizational authority and credentialing compliance.

The date the NPI was deactivated by the provider or CMS. Indicates the provider is no longer actively using this identifier. Used for claims validation, credentialing status checks, and provider directory management.
The IRS Employer Identification Number (EIN) for organizational providers (Type 2 only). Used for tax reporting and organizational identification. Also known as Federal Tax ID.

Indicates whether the NPI is assigned to an individual provider (Type 1) or an organization (Type 2). Type 1 = Individual healthcare provider (physician, nurse, therapist). Type 2 = Healthcare organization (hospital, clinic, group practice, pharmacy).

The date the NPI was originally assigned to the provider by NPPES. Represents the providers initial enrollment in the national provider registry. Critical for credentialing timelines and provider history tracking.

The date the NPI record was last updated in NPPES. Reflects the most recent change to provider demographic information, address, taxonomy, or other registry data. Used to track data freshness and trigger downstream updates.
The city name for the providers mailing address. Used for geographic segmentation and correspondence routing.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS Address Standards

description: The three-letter ISO country code for the providers mailing address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for international provider tracking and cross-border claims.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; CMS NPPES Data Dissemination

description: The fax number associated with the providers mailing address. Used for document transmission during credentialing, prior authorization, and claims correspondence.
The first line of the providers mailing address (street address, PO Box, or suite number). Used for official correspondence, credentialing documents, and regulatory communications.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS Address Standards

description: The second line of the providers mailing address (apartment, suite, building, floor). Optional field for additional address detail.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS Address Standards

description: The ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the providers mailing address. Used for mail delivery, geographic analysis, and service area determination.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS ZIP Code Standards

description: The two-letter state or territory code for the providers mailing address (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Used for state-level reporting and regulatory compliance.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS State Abbreviations

description: The telephone number associated with the providers mailing address. Used for provider contact and verification during credentialing and claims inquiries.

The 10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS NPPES. Unique identifier for healthcare providers in the United States. This is the authoritative business identifier for provider lookup across claims, credentialing, and directory systems.
The legal business name of the healthcare organization for Type 2 (organizational) NPIs. Examples: hospital name, clinic name, group practice name, pharmacy name.
Alternate name, trade name, or doing-business-as (DBA) name for the healthcare organization. Used when the organization operates under a name different from its legal name.
The three-letter ISO country code for the providers practice location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for international provider tracking and cross-border service delivery.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; CMS NPPES Data Dissemination

description: The first line of the providers primary practice location address (street address, suite number). This is the physical location where the provider sees patients or delivers services. Used for provider directories, patient navigation, and service area mapping.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS Address Standards

description: The second line of the providers practice location address (apartment, suite, building, floor). Optional field for additional address detail.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; USPS Address Standards

description: The fax number for the providers practice location. Used for medical records transmission, referral coordination, and prior authorization requests.
The telephone number for the providers practice location. Used for patient appointment scheduling, provider directory listings, and referral coordination.

The primary NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy code assigned to this NPI. A 10-digit alphanumeric code that classifies the providers type, classification, and specialization (e.g., 207Q00000X = Family Medicine, 208D00000X = General Practice). This is the providers main specialty for claims submission and network assignment.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; HIPAA

description: Indicates whether the taxonomy code is designated as the providers primary taxonomy. Y = Yes (primary), N = No (secondary). Used to distinguish the main specialty from additional specialties for claims routing and network credentialing.
The professional credential or designation of the individual provider (e.g., MD, DO, RN, DDS, PharmD, PhD, LCSW). Indicates the providers professional qualifications and licensure type.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; State Licensing Boards

description: The legal first name (given name) of the individual healthcare provider for Type 1 NPIs. Used for provider identification, credentialing, and claims submission.
The gender of the individual provider for Type 1 NPIs. M = Male, F = Female. Used for provider demographic reporting and patient preference matching.
The legal last name (family name or surname) of the individual healthcare provider for Type 1 NPIs. Used for provider identification, credentialing, and claims submission.
The middle name of the individual healthcare provider for Type 1 NPIs. Optional field used for complete provider identification.
The name prefix or title for the individual provider (e.g., Dr., Mr., Ms., Rev.). Used for formal provider identification and correspondence.
The name suffix for the individual provider (e.g., Jr., Sr., II, III). Used for complete provider identification and to distinguish between providers with similar names.
An alternate or additional professional credential for the individual provider. Used when a provider holds multiple credentials or has changed credentials.
reference: CMS NPPES Data Dissemination; State Licensing Boards

description: An alternate or previous first name for the individual provider. Used to track provider name changes and maintain historical continuity.
An alternate or previous last name for the individual provider (e.g., maiden name, former married name). Used to track provider name changes and maintain historical continuity.
An alternate or previous middle name for the individual provider. Used to track provider name changes and maintain historical continuity.
An alternate name prefix for the individual provider. Used to track provider name changes.
An alternate name suffix for the individual provider. Used to track provider name changes.
The date a previously deactivated NPI was reactivated. Indicates the provider has resumed use of this identifier. Used to track provider status changes and maintain accurate active provider lists.

business_glossary_term: SNOMED CT (Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms) Concept Identifier
description: Unique identifier for the SNOMED CT concept as assigned by SNOMED International. This is the primary key and corresponds to the SCTID (SNOMED CT Identifier) in the SNOMED CT release files.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Release Format

description: The SNOMED CT release version identifier from which this concept was loaded (e.g., '20230901', '2023-09-01'). Supports version tracking and change management across SNOMED CT releases.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Release Notes

description: Identifier of the immediate parent concept in the SNOMED CT IS-A hierarchy. Used to navigate the taxonomy and determine subsumption relationships. A concept may have multiple parents in a polyhierarchy.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Concept Model

description: The typical section(s) of clinical documentation where this concept is used (e.g., 'Problem List', 'Allergies', 'Past Medical History', 'Assessment', 'Plan', 'Procedures'). Supports context-aware search and documentation templates.
reference: HL7 Consolidated Clinical Document Architecture (C-CDA) Implementation Guide

description: Additional classification of the concept beyond the semantic tag, used for grouping and filtering (e.g., 'diagnosis', 'symptom', 'surgical procedure', 'medication', 'laboratory test'). May be derived from semantic tag or additional metadata.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Editorial Guide

description: A formal textual definition of the concept providing clinical context and meaning. May be sourced from SNOMED International or supplemented with organizational definitions for clarity.

Current lifecycle status of the SNOMED CT concept. Active concepts are available for use in clinical documentation; inactive concepts are deprecated and should not be used for new entries but may appear in historical data.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Editorial Guide

business_glossary_term: CPT (Current Procedural Terminology) Map Target Code
description: The CPT code(s) to which this SNOMED CT procedure concept is mapped, if applicable. Supports billing and revenue cycle management by linking clinical procedures to reimbursable CPT codes.
reference: American Medical Association (AMA) CPT to SNOMED CT Map

description: The timestamp when this concept record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Indicates whether the concept is primitive (defined only by its stated relationships) or fully defined (sufficient conditions provided for automated classification). Fully defined concepts can be automatically classified by description logic reasoners.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Technical Implementation Guide

description: The date on which this version of the concept became effective in the SNOMED CT release. Represents the snapshot date of the concept state in the release cycle (typically in YYYYMMDD format in source, stored as DATE).
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Release File Specifications

business_glossary_term: FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) Value Set Membership
description: Comma-separated list of FHIR value set identifiers (URIs) to which this concept belongs. Used to support FHIR-based interoperability and constrain coded elements in FHIR resources.

The complete, unambiguous name of the SNOMED CT concept including the semantic tag in parentheses (e.g., 'Pneumonia (disorder)'). The FSN uniquely identifies the concept and includes the semantic category to distinguish homonyms.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Compositional Grammar Specification

description: The depth of this concept in the SNOMED CT hierarchy, with the root concept at level 0. Used for hierarchy traversal, rollup queries, and determining concept granularity.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Concept Model

description: The correlation type between the SNOMED CT concept and the ICD-10 map target, indicating the semantic equivalence of the mapping (e.g., exact match, SNOMED concept is narrower than ICD-10 code, SNOMED concept is broader, partial overlap, or not mappable).
reference: SNOMED International ICD-10 Map Reference Set

business_glossary_term: ICD-10 (International Classification of Diseases 10th Revision) Map Target Code
description: The ICD-10 code(s) to which this SNOMED CT concept is mapped, if applicable. Supports billing, reporting, and interoperability with systems that use ICD-10 for diagnosis coding. Multiple codes may be pipe-separated.
reference: SNOMED International ICD-10 Map Reference Set

business_glossary_term: Is EHR (Electronic Health Record) Preferred Concept Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this concept is designated as a preferred term for use in the organization's EHR system. Preferred concepts are promoted in search results and pick lists to standardize documentation.
Boolean flag indicating whether this concept is a leaf node in the hierarchy (has no child concepts). Leaf concepts represent the most granular clinical terms available.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Concept Model

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the concept is primitive (true) or fully defined (false). Primitive concepts require manual classification; fully defined concepts can be automatically classified by reasoners.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Technical Implementation Guide

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this concept represents a condition that must be reported to public health authorities (e.g., notifiable diseases, healthcare-associated infections). Used to trigger automated reporting workflows.
reference: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) National Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System (NNDSS)

description: The most recent date on which this concept was used in clinical documentation within the organization. Used to identify obsolete or rarely used concepts for potential retirement or training needs.

business_glossary_term: LOINC (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes) Map Target Code
description: The LOINC code(s) to which this SNOMED CT concept is mapped, if applicable. Used for laboratory and clinical observations to support interoperability with laboratory information systems and FHIR resources.
reference: SNOMED International LOINC to SNOMED CT Map

description: Identifier of the SNOMED CT module (namespace) to which this concept belongs. Modules represent different editions or extensions (e.g., International Edition, US Edition, UK Edition) and control versioning and governance.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Module Dependency Reference Set

description: A simplified, patient-friendly description of the concept for use in patient portals, after-visit summaries, and patient education materials. Avoids medical jargon and uses plain language.
reference: Plain Language Medical Dictionary, Patient Education Standards

description: The preferred human-readable term for the concept in the default language (typically English). This is the term most commonly used in clinical documentation and user interfaces, without the semantic tag.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Language Reference Sets

description: Comma-separated list of quality measure identifiers (e.g., HEDIS, CMS, MIPS) for which this concept is included in the numerator, denominator, or exclusion criteria. Supports automated quality reporting and value-based care programs.
reference: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) HEDIS Technical Specifications, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Quality Measures

description: The total number of active relationships (both stated and inferred) associated with this concept. Relationships define the concept's meaning and position in the ontology.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Relationship File

description: The RxNorm code(s) to which this SNOMED CT pharmaceutical/biologic product concept is mapped, if applicable. Enables medication interoperability and e-prescribing by linking to the NLM RxNorm drug terminology.
reference: National Library of Medicine (NLM) RxNorm to SNOMED CT Map

description: The high-level semantic category of the concept extracted from the FSN (e.g., 'disorder', 'procedure', 'finding', 'body structure', 'substance', 'organism'). Used to classify concepts into broad clinical domains.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Concept Model

description: The system or module from which this concept record was sourced (e.g., 'SNOMED International RF2', 'Epic Clarity', 'Cerner Millennium', 'NLM UMLS'). Supports data lineage and troubleshooting.

Comma-separated list of medical specialties for which this concept is particularly relevant (e.g., 'Cardiology', 'Oncology', 'Pediatrics', 'Emergency Medicine'). Used to customize pick lists and search results by provider specialty.
The number of active synonym descriptions associated with this concept. Synonyms provide alternative terms for the same concept to support varied clinical documentation styles and search.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Description File

description: The top-level SNOMED CT hierarchy to which this concept belongs (e.g., 'Clinical Finding', 'Procedure', 'Body Structure', 'Pharmaceutical / Biologic Product', 'Organism', 'Substance', 'Physical Object', 'Qualifier Value', 'Observable Entity', 'Situation', 'Event', 'Environment', 'Social Context', 'Staging and Scales', 'Linkage Concept', 'Special Concept', 'Navigational Concept', 'Record Artifact'). Derived from the root ancestor in the IS-A hierarchy.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT Concept Model

description: The timestamp when this concept record was last updated in the enterprise data platform. Used for change tracking and incremental data processing.
Rank of this concept based on usage frequency in clinical documentation within the organization. Lower numbers indicate higher usage. Used to prioritize search results and optimize user interfaces.
Unique identifier for the scheduled appointment. Primary key for the appointment record across all care settings and modalities.
FK to scheduling.appointment_type
Unique identifier for the insurance authorization or pre-certification associated with this appointment, if required by the payer.

Foreign key linking to clinical.care_plan. Business justification: Appointments are scheduled as part of care plan execution; care plans drive visit frequency, type, and multidisciplinary coordination. Essential for chronic care management, care coordination billing,
Unique identifier for the healthcare facility or clinic location where the appointment is scheduled to take place.

Foreign key linking to claim.claim. Business justification: Appointment-to-cash reconciliation: revenue cycle teams must trace each scheduled appointment to its resulting claim for denial management, no-show billing, and appointment conversion rate reporting.

Foreign key linking to claim.prior_authorization. Business justification: Appointments requiring prior authorization must link to the authorization record for verification at scheduling and check-in. Schedulers validate authorization status, approved units, and date ranges

Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Non-surgical clinical orders (imaging, procedure, therapy orders) result in scheduled appointments. Linking scheduling_appointment to the originating clinical_order supports order-completion tracking,
Unique identifier for the primary provider scheduled to deliver care during this appointment.

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_coverage. Business justification: Insurance verification at scheduling: the billing coverage record verified at time of appointment booking must be captured for 'coverage at time of service' audits and payer dispute resolution. Revenu

Foreign key linking to insurance.coverage_policy. Business justification: At appointment booking, staff verify the applicable coverage policy to confirm the service is covered, check medical necessity criteria, and identify PA requirements. Healthcare schedulers routinely r
Unique identifier for the patient associated with this appointment. Links to the patient master record.

Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_diagnosis. Business justification: Appointments are scheduled for specific diagnoses that drive specialty routing, authorization requirements, and visit type determination. Essential for pre-visit planning and specialty access manageme

Foreign key linking to patient.eligibility_check. Business justification: scheduling_appointment.insurance_verification_status reflects the result of a specific eligibility_check performed at scheduling time. Linking to the eligibility_check record enables revenue cycle sta

Foreign key linking to claim.eligibility. Business justification: Real-time eligibility verification at appointment booking confirms active coverage, copay amounts, and authorization requirements. Schedulers perform eligibility checks before confirming appointments

Foreign key linking to insurance.eligibility_span. Business justification: Appointment booking requires verifying the patient's active eligibility span to confirm coverage dates, PCP assignment, and referral requirements. The existing eligibility_id links to claim.eligibilit

Foreign key linking to radiology.protocol. Business justification: When scheduling a radiology appointment, the imaging protocol determines slot duration, patient prep instructions, fasting requirements, and contrast screening needs. Schedulers must reference the pro

Foreign key linking to patient.insurance_coverage. Business justification: scheduling_appointment carries insurance_verification_status and insurance_verification_timestamp, requiring a reference to the specific coverage verified. Drives prior authorization workflows, eligib

Foreign key linking to insurance.member_enrollment. Business justification: Appointments must verify active enrollment status at scheduled date to prevent scheduling patients without coverage. Schedulers check enrollment effective/termination dates and PCP assignment requirem

Foreign key linking to patient.mpi_record. Business justification: Enterprise scheduling dashboards, patient safety duplicate detection, and cross-facility appointment history reports require appointments linked to the master patient identity (MPI). Demographics FK e

Foreign key linking to provider.network_affiliation. Business justification: Network status determines patient cost-sharing and appointment eligibility per payer contracts. Links appointment to specific network tier and panel status, enabling real-time verification of in-netwo

Foreign key linking to scheduling.open_slot. Business justification: When a patient books an appointment, they occupy a specific open slot. This FK links the appointment back to the slot it was booked into, enabling slot utilization tracking, overbooking analysis, and

Foreign key linking to insurance.payer. Business justification: Appointments require real-time insurance verification at booking. Schedulers validate payer eligibility, authorization requirements, and network status before confirming appointments. Payer determines

Foreign key linking to insurance.prior_auth_rule. Business justification: When booking an appointment, scheduling systems check the governing prior auth rule to determine if PA is required before the visit. This drives PA workflow initiation at scheduling time and is a core
Unique identifier for the specific exam room, procedure room, or virtual room assigned to this appointment.

Foreign key linking to facility.service. Business justification: Appointments are scheduled under a specific clinical service (e.g., Cardiology, Orthopedics). Service-level appointment volume reporting, referral routing, and capacity management require this link. A

Foreign key linking to order.standing_order. Business justification: Individual recurring appointments (weekly dialysis, monthly INR checks, regular wound care visits) are generated from standing orders. Linking each scheduling_appointment to its originating standing_o
Unique identifier for the associated visit or encounter record if the appointment has been fulfilled. Null for future or cancelled appointments.

business_glossary_term: Visit Reason Icd Code Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Visit reason codes require ICD-10 validation for clinical accuracy, insurance authorization, billing compliance, and quality reporting. Essential for appointment booking workflows and claim submission
Human-readable business identifier for the appointment, typically displayed to patients and staff. May follow facility-specific numbering conventions.

Current lifecycle status of the appointment. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|checked-in|roomed|arrived|in-progress|completed|cancelled|no-show|rescheduled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date and time when the patient physically arrived at the facility or joined the virtual waiting room for telehealth appointments.
Indicates whether the appointment is eligible for billing based on completion status, documentation, and payer rules. Used by revenue cycle to determine billable encounters.
The channel or interface through which the appointment was originally scheduled. Used for patient access analytics and channel optimization.
The date and time when the appointment was originally created or booked in the scheduling system.
Free-text or coded explanation for why the appointment was cancelled. May indicate patient-initiated, provider-initiated, or system-initiated cancellation.
Standardized code representing the cancellation reason category (e.g., patient request, provider unavailable, weather, no-show conversion).
The date and time when the appointment was cancelled. Null for non-cancelled appointments.
Indicates which party initiated the cancellation of the appointment.

The physical or virtual care environment where the appointment will take place. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: outpatient|emergency|inpatient-consult|procedural|surgical|telehealth|home-health — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date and time when the patient checked in for the appointment, either at a kiosk, front desk, or via mobile check-in.
Indicates whether the patient has confirmed their intent to attend the appointment. Separate from appointment status to track patient engagement.
The date and time when the patient confirmed the appointment, either through automated reminder response or manual confirmation.
The date and time when this appointment record was first created in the scheduling system. Audit trail for record creation.
The planned duration of the appointment in minutes. Calculated from scheduled start and end times or set by appointment type template.
The actual date and time when the clinical encounter concluded and the patient was discharged from the appointment.
Indicates whether patient insurance eligibility and benefits have been verified for this appointment. Critical for revenue cycle and patient financial counseling.
The date and time when insurance eligibility was last verified for this appointment.
Indicates whether the patient failed to attend the scheduled appointment without prior cancellation. Used for no-show tracking and patient access policies.
The type of device the patient used to access the telehealth appointment (e.g., smartphone, tablet, desktop, laptop). Used for technical support and quality improvement.
Clinical urgency or priority level assigned to the appointment. Influences scheduling order and resource allocation.
Indicates whether the provider has attested that the telehealth visit met all clinical and regulatory requirements for billing and documentation. Required for telehealth reimbursement.
The date and time when the patient was placed in an exam room or virtual consultation room and is ready to be seen by the provider.
The calendar date on which the appointment is scheduled to occur.
The precise date and time when the appointment is scheduled to end, including timezone.
The precise date and time when the appointment is scheduled to begin, including timezone.
The actual date and time when the provider began the clinical encounter. May differ from scheduled start time.
The meeting ID, access code, or PIN required to join the telehealth session. Used for platforms that require separate authentication.
Real-time or final status of the telehealth session connection. Tracks technical success of the virtual visit.
The technology platform or vendor used to conduct the virtual visit (e.g., Epic Video Visit, Zoom for Healthcare, Amwell, Doxy.me). Null for in-person appointments.
The unique web link or meeting URL provided to the patient and provider to join the virtual visit. Contains session-specific access credentials.
