Indicates whether the team member is scheduled, on-call, backup, or responding to an emergency for this case. Used for staffing and scheduling analytics.
The date and time when this team member assignment was cancelled. Null if assignment was not cancelled.
Reason why this team member assignment was cancelled. May include provider unavailability, case cancellation, or scheduling changes.
The date and time when the team member confirmed their participation in the surgical case.
Status indicating whether the team member has confirmed their availability and commitment to participate in this surgical case.
Detailed description of any scheduling conflict identified for this team member assignment. Includes conflicting case number or appointment details.
Indicates whether this team member assignment has a scheduling conflict with another commitment. True if conflict exists, False otherwise.
The date and time when this team member assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The date and time when the team member's credentials and privileges were verified for this surgical case.
Indicates whether the team member's credentials and privileges have been verified for this specific surgical case. True if verified, False if not verified or pending.
reference: Joint Commission Standards, CMS Conditions of Participation

description: The date and time when this team member assignment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The professional license number of the team member. Required for regulatory compliance and credentialing verification.
reference: State Medical Board Requirements, Nursing Board Requirements

description: Two-letter state code where the team member's professional license was issued. Must match the facility's state or have reciprocity agreement.
Total duration in minutes of the team member's actual participation in the surgical case. Calculated from actual start and end times.
Indicates whether this team member holds the primary responsibility for their role category (e.g., primary surgeon vs. assisting surgeon). True if primary, False if secondary or assisting.

Full name of the primary surgeon. Used for operational communication, patient information, and surgical schedule displays. [Moved from surgical_case: Denormalized display attribute derived from the primary surgeon relationship. Should be removed as it can be derived from the surgical_case_team association joined to clinician.]
reference: Epic Provider Master, Cerner Personnel Management

description: Code representing the specific clinical privilege that authorizes this team member to perform their role in this surgical case. Links to the provider's credentialing and privileging records.
reference: Joint Commission Standards, Medical Staff Bylaws

description: Indicates whether this team member assignment is subject to quality measurement or reporting requirements. True if applicable, False otherwise.
reference: CMS Quality Reporting, Joint Commission Core Measures

business_glossary_term: Replacement Team Member National Provider Identifier (NPI)
description: The NPI of the replacement team member if this assignment was substituted. Used to track last-minute staffing changes.
The date and time when this team member was requested for assignment to the surgical case.
The date and time when the team member is scheduled to complete their participation in the surgical case.
The date and time when the team member is scheduled to begin their participation in the surgical case.
Time in minutes spent by the team member in surgical scrub preparation. Applicable to scrubbed team members (surgeons, scrub techs).
Name of the source system from which this team member assignment record originated (e.g., Epic OpTime, Cerner SurgiNet).
Unique identifier for this team member assignment in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The NPI of the attending physician or supervising provider if this team member is a resident, fellow, or student. Required for teaching case billing compliance.
Indicates whether this is a teaching case with resident or student participation under supervision. True if teaching case, False otherwise. Impacts billing and documentation requirements.
reference: CMS Teaching Physician Rules, ACGME Standards

description: Full legal name of the care team member assigned to this surgical case. Used for case documentation and credentialing verification.

Standardized code representing the role of the team member in the surgical case. Values: SURGEON (primary surgeon), CO_SURGEON (assisting surgeon), ANESTHESIOLOGIST (physician anesthesiologist), CRNA (Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist), SCRUB_TECH (scrub technician), CIRCULATING_NURSE (circulating nurse). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SURGEON|CO_SURGEON|ANESTHESIOLOGIST|CRNA|SCRUB_TECH|CIRCULATING_NURSE|PERFUSIONIST|FIRST_ASSIST|SURGICAL_RESIDENT|ANESTHESIA_RESIDENT|STUDENT_NURSE — promote to reference product]
reference: Epic OpTime, Cerner SurgiNet, AORN Standards

description: Human-readable description of the team member's role and responsibilities during the surgical case.
Total time in minutes that the team member spent in the operating room for this case. Used for efficiency and utilization analysis.
Unique surrogate identifier for the goods receipt record in the healthcare supply chain lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Receiving

description: Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Patient-specific implant receipts linked to the clinical order awaiting the device (e.g., custom knee implant arrives for scheduled surgery). Enables order-to-receipt workflow and surgical scheduling
Reference to the healthcare facility (hospital, clinic, outpatient center) where the goods were physically received and inventoried.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Inventory; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: Pharmaceutical receiving requires drug-specific validation: DEA schedule verification, REMS program compliance, hazardous drug handling protocols. Linking goods_receipt to drug_master enables automate
Reference to the specific storage location, warehouse bin, or department within the facility where goods were received (e.g., central supply, pharmacy, OR storeroom).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Inventory

description: Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: goods_receipt has material_number and item_description which are authoritative attributes of material_master. Each goods receipt line receives a specific material item. This is standard N:1 relationsh

Foreign key linking to reference.ndc_drug. Business justification: Pharmaceutical receiving requires linking each goods receipt to the NDC drug master for DEA schedule verification, lot/expiration validation, formulary compliance checks, and 340B drug receiving audit
Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt was posted. Enables three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) for accounts payable processing in Revenue Cycle Management (RCM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Procurement; GAAP and FASB

description: Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order_line. Business justification: goods_receipt is the physical receipt event against a specific PO line item. Line-to-header rule applies - goods_receipt should link to purchase_order_line (not just purchase_order header) for traceab

Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: Goods receipts are delivered to specific units (pharmacy, ICU supply room, central sterile). Unit-level delivery tracking supports receiving workflow, departmental charge-back, and supply utilization
Reference to the vendor or supplier who shipped the goods. Used for vendor performance tracking, recall management, and accounts payable matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Procurement

description: The physical condition of the goods as assessed by the receiving staff at time of delivery. Drives quality hold, vendor return, or discrepancy workflow initiation. 'damaged' or 'contaminated' triggers a quality block in inventory.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC); FDA 21 CFR Part 820; SAP S/4HANA MM Quality Management

description: Timestamp when this goods receipt record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary values on this goods receipt (e.g., USD). Required for multi-currency environments and financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; GAAP and FASB; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The vendor's packing slip or delivery note number accompanying the shipment. Used for three-way match (purchase order, delivery note, invoice) and discrepancy resolution with the vendor.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Receiving; GAAP and FASB

description: Indicates whether a discrepancy was identified between the purchase order, delivery note, and actual goods received (quantity, item, condition, or pricing mismatch). When True, triggers a discrepancy resolution workflow and may block accounts payable posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Receiving; GAAP and FASB

description: Free-text description of the discrepancy observed at receipt, entered by the receiving staff. Provides context for vendor dispute resolution, accounts payable hold, and quality management follow-up.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Receiving; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Categorizes the nature of the discrepancy identified at receipt: quantity variance, wrong item delivered, price mismatch against purchase order, unacceptable condition, or missing documentation. Populated only when discrepancy_flag is True.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Receiving

description: The manufacturer-assigned expiration or use-by date for the received goods. Critical for par-level replenishment, FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, and preventing use of expired medical supplies per Joint Commission standards.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; The Joint Commission (TJC); SAP S/4HANA MM Shelf Life Management

description: Indicates whether the received item is an implantable medical device subject to FDA UDI scanning requirements and implant registry tracking (e.g., orthopedic implants, cardiac devices, vascular grafts). Drives UDI capture workflow.
reference: FDA UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; The Joint Commission (TJC); CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Indicates whether the goods receipt has successfully triggered an inventory quantity update in the warehouse management system. False may indicate a posting error or quality block preventing inventory movement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Inventory; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Manufacturer's lot or batch number assigned to the received goods. Critical for recall management, expiration tracking, and traceability of medical-surgical supplies and implantable devices per FDA requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 820; FDA UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management

description: The date on which the goods were manufactured, as indicated on the product label or UDI barcode. Used for shelf-life calculation, quality assurance, and FDA UDI traceability.
reference: FDA UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; FDA 21 CFR Part 820

description: The SAP MM or Infor Lawson inventory movement type code classifying the nature of the goods receipt transaction (e.g., SAP movement type 101=GR for PO, 122=Return to Vendor, 161=GR for Returns). Drives accounting document generation and inventory valuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 101|122|161|501|521|531 — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: The SAP plant code or Infor Lawson site code representing the organizational unit (hospital campus, distribution center) where the goods receipt is posted. Used for inventory segmentation and financial posting to the correct cost center.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI; Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: The exact date and time the goods receipt was posted in the ERP system (SAP MIGO or Infor Lawson), triggering inventory update and accounts payable liability. Distinct from the physical receipt date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO; GAAP and FASB

description: The quantity of goods specified on the originating purchase order line. Used to calculate over/under receipt discrepancies and to support three-way match for accounts payable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Procurement; GAAP and FASB

description: The actual quantity of goods physically received and accepted at the receiving dock. Compared against the purchase order quantity and delivery note quantity for three-way match and discrepancy detection.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO; Infor Lawson Receiving; GAAP and FASB

description: Indicates whether the received lot or item has been subsequently identified as subject to an FDA or manufacturer recall. When True, triggers recall management workflow to quarantine and return affected inventory.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: The FDA or manufacturer-assigned recall identification number associated with this goods receipt when a recall has been issued. Populated only when recall_flag is True. Supports regulatory reporting to FDA and OIG.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; Office of Inspector General (OIG); FDA GUDID

description: The calendar date on which the physical receipt of goods occurred at the receiving dock. This is the principal business event date used for inventory valuation, expiration tracking, and accounts payable aging.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO; GAAP and FASB; Infor Lawson Receiving

description: Externally-known business identifier for this goods receipt document, corresponding to the SAP MIGO material document number or Infor Lawson receiving document number. Used in three-way match and audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Receiving

description: Current lifecycle state of the goods receipt document. 'posted' indicates inventory has been updated; 'reversed' indicates a cancellation posting; 'blocked' indicates a quality or discrepancy hold pending resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: Manufacturer's serial number for serialized items such as capital equipment, durable medical devices, or high-value implants. Supports individual asset tracking and FDA UDI compliance.
reference: FDA UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; SAP S/4HANA MM Serial Number Management

description: The native document number from the source system (e.g., SAP material document number from MIGO, or Infor Lawson receiving document number). Enables traceability back to the originating system for audit and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO; Infor Lawson Receiving; ISO 27001

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this goods receipt was sourced (SAP S/4HANA MM MIGO, Infor Lawson Receiving, MEDITECH, or manual entry). Required for data lineage and Extract Transform Load (ETL) reconciliation in the lakehouse.
reference: ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Indicates whether the received item requires sterile processing or reprocessing before clinical use. When True, routes the item to the Sterile Processing Department (SPD) workflow rather than direct inventory stocking.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC); AAMI ST79; FDA 21 CFR Part 820; OSHA

description: Required storage condition for the received goods as specified by the manufacturer (e.g., refrigerated for biologics and vaccines, frozen for certain pharmaceuticals, ambient for standard medical-surgical supplies). Drives putaway routing in the warehouse.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211; USP <1079>; The Joint Commission (TJC); SAP S/4HANA MM WM

description: Indicates whether a temperature excursion was detected during transit or at receipt for cold-chain items (vaccines, biologics, temperature-sensitive pharmaceuticals). When True, triggers quality review and potential rejection per cold-chain management protocols.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211; USP <1079>; CDC Vaccine Storage and Handling Toolkit; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Current status of the three-way match process between the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice. 'matched' enables accounts payable payment; 'exception' indicates a variance requiring resolution before payment release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/MM; GAAP and FASB; Infor Lawson Procurement

description: The total monetary value of goods received, calculated as quantity received multiplied by unit cost. Used to post the goods receipt liability in accounts payable and for inventory valuation in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO/MM; GAAP and FASB

description: The FDA-mandated Unique Device Identifier (UDI) Device Identifier (DI) portion scanned from the device label barcode (GS1, HIBCC, or ICCBBA format) for implantable and Class II/III medical devices. Required for FDA UDI compliance and implant registry reporting.
reference: FDA UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; FDA GUDID Database; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: The FDA-mandated Unique Device Identifier (UDI) Production Identifier (PI) portion containing lot/batch number, serial number, manufacturing date, and/or expiration date as encoded on the device label. Complements the UDI Device Identifier for full UDI compliance.
reference: FDA UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; FDA GUDID Database

description: The contracted or invoice unit cost per unit of measure for the received item. Used for inventory valuation, three-way match, and cost accounting in SAP FI/CO. Classified confidential as it reflects negotiated contract pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO/MM; GAAP and FASB; Infor Lawson Procurement

description: The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., EA=Each, BX=Box, CS=Case, PK=Pack, BT=Bottle, VI=Vial). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure and Infor Lawson stocking unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|BX|CS|PK|BT|VI|DZ|TU|KG|LT — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; ISO 80000

description: Timestamp when this goods receipt record was last modified in the source system (e.g., reversal, correction, or status change). Supports incremental Extract Transform Load (ETL) processing.
reference: ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The vendor's invoice number associated with this shipment, if provided at time of receipt. Supports early three-way match initiation and accounts payable processing in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/MM; GAAP and FASB; Infor Lawson Procurement

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory balance record representing the on-hand position of a material item at a specific storage location. Primary key for this data product.
reference: SAP MM Inventory Management; Infor Lawson Inventory

description: Reference to the healthcare facility (hospital, clinic, outpatient center) where this inventory balance is held. Supports multi-facility enterprise inventory visibility.
reference: SAP MM Plant (T001W); Infor Lawson Facility Master

description: Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: DEA requires accurate scheduled substance inventory records; REMS programs require quantity tracking. Linking supply inventory_balance to pharmacy drug_master replaces denormalized ndc_code and enable
Reference to the storage location (e.g., storeroom, OR supply room, nursing unit, pharmacy satellite, or warehouse bin) where this inventory balance is maintained.
reference: SAP MM Storage Location (MARD); Infor Lawson Location Master

description: Foreign key linking to reference.ndc_drug. Business justification: Pharmacy inventory management requires linking on-hand drug balances to the NDC drug reference for formulary status reporting, DEA controlled substance inventory reconciliation, drug shortage monitori
Reference to the material master record identifying the specific medical-surgical supply, implantable device, pharmaceutical, or consumable item tracked in this inventory balance.
reference: SAP MM Material Master (MM60); Infor Lawson Item Master

description: Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: PAR-level inventory balances are managed at the nursing unit level (e.g., ICU Pyxis, ED supply room). Unit-level inventory balance reporting is a core healthcare supply chain operation for replenishme

Foreign key linking to supply.vendor_contract. Business justification: inventory_balance has contract_number as STRING. For consignment inventory or contract-specific pricing/ownership, this should be FK to vendor_contract for referential integrity and to enable joining
Reference to the primary vendor or supplier associated with this inventory item, used for consignment reconciliation and replenishment sourcing.

description: ABC inventory classification indicating the relative value and usage frequency of the item. 'A' items are high-value/high-usage requiring tight control; 'B' items are moderate; 'C' items are low-value/low-usage; 'X' items are unclassified or obsolete.
reference: SAP MM ABC Indicator (ABCIN); Infor Lawson ABC Analysis; AHRMM Inventory Stratification

description: The timestamp at which this inventory balance record was captured or last refreshed from the source system. Represents the as-of time for all quantity fields, enabling point-in-time inventory reporting.
reference: SAP MM Inventory Management; Infor Lawson Inventory Snapshot; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory Reporting

description: Indicates whether the current on-hand quantity has fallen below the defined reorder point, signaling that a replenishment order should be initiated. Distinct from stockout_flag which indicates zero or critically low stock.

description: The vendor account number for consignment stock held at this location. Populated only when ownership_type is 'consignment'. Used for consignment reconciliation, liability reporting, and vendor settlement.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit cost and inventory valuation amounts (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency enterprise environments.

description: Number of days from the current date until the earliest expiration date for on-hand stock. Used to trigger expiration alerts and prioritize consumption. Supports proactive waste management and patient safety.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) Supply Expiration Policy; FDA 21 CFR Part 211; AHRMM Expiration Management

description: The earliest expiration date among current on-hand stock for this item at this location. Used for expiration alerting, FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking, and waste reduction. Critical for patient safety compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211; The Joint Commission (TJC) Medication and Supply Expiration Standards; SAP MM Shelf Life (MHDRZ)

description: Indicates whether this item is on the facility's approved formulary or preferred item list. Supports value analysis, standardization initiatives, and contract compliance monitoring.

description: Current operational status of the inventory balance record. 'blocked' indicates stock held pending quality review; 'recalled' indicates FDA or manufacturer recall action; 'quarantine' indicates items pending investigation or inspection.

description: High-level classification of the inventory item type. Drives handling rules, regulatory tracking requirements, and reporting segmentation. reference: SAP MM Material Type (MTART); Infor Lawson Item Category; FDA Device Classification

description: Date of the most recent inventory transaction (receipt, issue, transfer, or adjustment) for this item at this location. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete stock.

description: Date of the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count performed for this item at this location. Used to assess inventory accuracy, schedule next count, and support audit compliance.
reference: SAP MM Physical Inventory (MI01); Infor Lawson Cycle Count; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory Accuracy; The Joint Commission (TJC) Supply Chain Audit

description: The quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count for this item at this location. Used to calculate inventory variance and assess system accuracy.

description: Date of the most recent goods receipt for this item at this location. Used to assess replenishment cycle performance and vendor delivery timeliness.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); Infor Lawson Purchase Order Receipt

description: Manufacturer or internal lot/batch number for the current stock of this item. Required for expiration tracking, recall management, and FDA traceability. Populated only when lot_tracking_enabled is true.

description: Indicates whether lot/batch number tracking is active for this item at this location. Required for items subject to FDA recall management, expiration tracking, and chain-of-custody compliance.

description: The maximum quantity of the item that should be held at this storage location. Prevents overstocking, reduces waste from expiration, and optimizes storage space utilization.

description: Indicates the ownership arrangement for this inventory. 'consignment' means the vendor retains title until use; 'loaner' applies to implant trays or devices temporarily on-site for a specific procedure; 'owned' means the facility has purchased and holds title.
reference: SAP MM Special Stock Indicator (SOBKZ); Infor Lawson Consignment Tracking; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory

description: The target stock quantity (Periodic Automatic Replenishment level) that should be maintained at this location. When on-hand quantity falls below par, a replenishment order is triggered to restore stock to this level.

description: Quantity of the item that has been shipped from the vendor or a central warehouse but has not yet been received at the destination storage location. Supports in-transit visibility for supply chain planning.

description: Current unrestricted quantity of the item physically present and available for use at the storage location. This is the primary stock level metric used for replenishment decisions and inventory valuation.

description: Total quantity of the item currently on open purchase orders or requisitions that have not yet been received. Used to project future availability and prevent duplicate ordering.

description: Quantity of the item currently held in quarantine stock pending quality inspection, recall investigation, or regulatory hold. Not available for clinical use until released.
reference: SAP MM Quality Inspection Stock (EINME); FDA Recall Guidance; ISO 13485 Quality Management

description: Quantity of the item reserved for specific procedures, surgical cases, or patient orders but not yet consumed. Reduces available-to-promise quantity for replenishment calculations.

description: Indicates whether any on-hand stock of this item at this location is subject to an active FDA or manufacturer recall. When true, the item should be quarantined and removed from clinical use pending recall resolution.

description: FDA or manufacturer-assigned recall identification number associated with the active recall affecting this inventory. Populated only when recall_flag is true. Used for recall tracking and regulatory reporting.
reference: FDA Recall Database; FDA 21 CFR Part 7; The Joint Commission (TJC) Recall Procedures

description: The minimum quantity threshold at which a replenishment order must be initiated to avoid stockout, accounting for lead time and safety stock. Used in min-max and reorder-point replenishment methods.

description: The method used to trigger restocking of this item at this location. description: Buffer stock quantity maintained above the reorder point to protect against demand variability and supply lead time uncertainty. Critical for high-utilization clinical items to prevent patient care disruptions.

description: Indicates whether individual serial number tracking is active for this item. Typically enabled for high-value implantable devices, capital equipment, and items requiring UDI-level traceability.

description: The natural key or composite identifier from the source system (e.g., SAP plant/storage location/material combination or Infor Lawson item/location key) that uniquely identifies this balance record in the originating system.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this inventory balance record was sourced (e.g., SAP MM, Infor Lawson). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; HL7 FHIR Supply Delivery Resource

description: Indicates whether the current on-hand quantity is zero or below the safety stock threshold, representing a stockout condition. Used to trigger emergency procurement and escalation workflows.

description: The specific bin, shelf, or slot identifier within the storage location where this item is physically stored. Supports directed putaway, picking, and cycle count operations.

description: FDA-mandated Unique Device Identifier for implantable and high-risk medical devices. Enables device tracking, recall management, and post-market surveillance per FDA UDI regulation (21 CFR Part 830). Null for non-device items.

description: The standard or moving average cost per unit of measure for this item at this location. Used for inventory valuation, charge capture, and cost accounting. Classified as confidential due to vendor pricing sensitivity.
reference: SAP MM Moving Average Price (VERPR); GAAP ASC 330 Inventory Valuation; CMS Cost Report

description: The base unit of measure in which inventory quantities are tracked (e.g., EA=Each, BX=Box, CS=Case, PK=Pack, BG=Bag, DZ=Dozen). Aligns with the material master UOM for consistent quantity reporting.
reference: SAP MM Base Unit of Measure (MEINS); Infor Lawson UOM; GS1 Unit of Measure Codes

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each physical or logical inventory storage location within the healthcare supply chain. Primary key for this master record.

Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: Inventory locations (PAR locations, Pyxis machines, supply closets) are assigned to specific nursing units. Unit-level supply location management is core to healthcare supply chain for PAR replenishme

Physical and system access control level for this location. Drives security configuration in ADC systems, badge access systems, and EHR supply dispensing workflows. Controlled substance locations require dual-key or pharmacist-only access per DEA regulations.

description: Manufacturer of the Automated Dispensing Cabinet (ADC) installed at this location. Used for maintenance scheduling, vendor management, and integration configuration with pharmacy systems.

description: Aisle designation within the storage location (e.g., 'A', 'B', '01'). Used in warehouse management and central storeroom operations to direct pickers to the correct aisle.

description: Timestamp when this inventory location master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance with HIPAA Security Rule audit control requirements.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule — Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312(b); ISO 27001 — Information Security Management

description: Scheduled frequency for physical inventory cycle counts at this location. Drives the cycle count calendar in the materials management system. High-value or controlled substance locations typically require more frequent counts per Joint Commission and DEA requirements.
reference: The Joint Commission — Supply Chain Standards; DEA — Controlled Substance Inventory Requirements; Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: Date on which this inventory location was or is scheduled to be deactivated or decommissioned. Nullable for currently active locations. Supports historical analysis and facility transition planning.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Master; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Date on which this inventory location became or is scheduled to become operationally active. Used for location lifecycle management, historical reporting, and integration with facility opening/renovation projects.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Master; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Indicates whether expiration date tracking is enforced for items stored at this location. When true, the system monitors item expiration dates and generates alerts for items approaching or past expiration, supporting patient safety and regulatory compliance.
reference: The Joint Commission — Medication Management Standards; FDA — Drug Expiration Dating 21 CFR Part 211

description: Floor or level within the building where the inventory location is situated. Used for delivery routing, pick-path optimization, and emergency supply logistics.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Address

description: General Ledger (GL) account code to which supply expenses from this location are posted. Supports financial reporting, cost allocation, and budget variance analysis per GAAP and FASB standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI General Ledger; GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this location is approved for storage of hazardous materials including chemotherapy agents, flammable chemicals, or OSHA-regulated hazardous substances. Drives compliance with OSHA Hazard Communication Standard and EPA regulations.
reference: OSHA — Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; EPA — Hazardous Waste Regulations; The Joint Commission

description: Indicates whether this location has active humidity control. Required for certain medical supplies, sterile packaging, and pharmaceutical products sensitive to moisture per FDA and USP storage standards.

Date on which the most recent physical inventory cycle count was completed at this location. Used to identify overdue counts, support audit readiness, and ensure compliance with Joint Commission and DEA inventory requirements.
reference: The Joint Commission — Supply Chain Standards; DEA — Controlled Substance Inventory Requirements

description: Source system location identifier from Infor Lawson Supply Chain. Used for ETL reconciliation, cross-system data lineage, and integration with procurement and inventory modules.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Master

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location, as assigned in Infor Lawson or SAP MM. Used on requisitions, pick tickets, and transfer orders to reference the physical or logical location.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the storage location (e.g., 'Central Storeroom A', '3rd Floor Nursing Supply Closet', 'OR Supply Room 2'). Used in reports, labels, and user interfaces.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Master

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or access directions relevant to this inventory location. Examples: 'Key held at nursing station', 'Refrigerator alarm monitored by facilities', 'Restricted to OR staff only'.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Master

description: Current lifecycle status of the inventory location. Controls whether the location is available for receiving, picking, and replenishment transactions. Inactive or decommissioned locations are excluded from par-level replenishment runs.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Status

description: Categorical classification of the storage location by its operational role within the supply chain. Drives replenishment rules, access controls, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: central_storeroom|or_supply_room|nursing_unit_closet|adc|off_site_warehouse|satellite_pharmacy|procedure_room|loading_dock — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Type; SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Type

description: Date on which the next physical inventory cycle count is scheduled for this location. Supports proactive scheduling of materials management staff and compliance monitoring.
reference: The Joint Commission — Supply Chain Standards; Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Cycle Count Schedule

description: Indicates whether this location is managed using par-level replenishment methodology. When true, the system monitors on-hand quantities against defined par levels and triggers automatic replenishment requisitions when stock falls below the par threshold.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Par Level Management; Joint Commission Supply Standards

description: The replenishment methodology used to restock this location. Automated Dispensing Cabinet (ADC) locations use automated dispensing; nursing units may use cart exchange or Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) rounds; central storerooms may use demand-based replenishment.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Replenishment Method

description: Name of the primary responsible person (e.g., charge nurse, materials manager, storeroom supervisor) accountable for this inventory location. Used for escalation, cycle count coordination, and supply chain communications.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Contact

description: Phone number of the primary responsible contact for this inventory location. Used for supply delivery coordination, urgent replenishment requests, and recall notifications.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Contact; HIPAA Privacy Rule

description: Indicates whether this location is enrolled in the automated recall management process. When true, FDA and manufacturer recall notifications trigger automated inventory holds and quarantine workflows for affected items at this location.
reference: FDA — Medical Device Recall 21 CFR Part 806; FDA — Drug Recall; OIG Compliance Guidance

description: How frequently this location is scheduled for replenishment review and restocking. Drives supply chain scheduling and staffing for materials management rounds.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Replenishment Schedule

description: Specific room or suite identifier within the building and floor for this inventory location. Provides precise physical address for supply delivery and cycle counting.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Address

description: Four-character SAP MM storage location code corresponding to this inventory location. Used for integration with SAP S/4HANA Materials Management for goods movements, purchase orders, and inventory postings.

Indicates whether this location is designated and secured for storage of DEA Schedule II–V controlled substances. Locations flagged true must comply with DEA 21 CFR Part 1301 physical security requirements and are subject to enhanced audit and access controls.
reference: DEA — Controlled Substances Act 21 CFR Part 1301; HIPAA Security Rule

description: Indicates whether this location serves as a staging area for sterile processing and reprocessing of reusable surgical instruments and devices. Locations flagged true are subject to sterile processing compliance standards and AAMI/AORN guidelines.
reference: AAMI ST79 — Comprehensive Guide to Steam Sterilization; AORN Perioperative Standards; The Joint Commission

description: Physical volumetric capacity of the storage location in cubic feet. Used for space planning, facility management, and determining whether bulk or oversized items can be stored at this location.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Capacity; SAP S/4HANA MM Warehouse Management

description: Maximum number of individual supply units or item slots this location can physically hold. Used for capacity planning, space utilization analysis, and par-level setting. Represents the principal quantitative capacity fact for this resource.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain — Location Capacity; SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Bin Capacity

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Exceedances trigger environmental monitoring alerts and may require product quarantine or disposal per FDA and USP guidelines.
reference: FDA — Drug Storage Requirements; USP <1079>; ISO 13485 — Medical Devices Quality Management

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Used for environmental monitoring alerts and compliance reporting with FDA and USP storage standards.
reference: FDA — Drug Storage Requirements; USP <1079>; ISO 13485 — Medical Devices Quality Management

description: Required temperature storage condition for items held at this location. Critical for compliance with FDA storage requirements for pharmaceuticals, biologics, and temperature-sensitive medical supplies. Ambient = 15–25°C; Refrigerated = 2–8°C; Frozen = -20°C; Ultra-Low Frozen = -80°C.
reference: FDA — Drug Storage Requirements; USP <1079> Good Storage and Distribution Practices

business_glossary_term: Unique Device Identification (UDI) Tracking Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this location participates in FDA Unique Device Identification (UDI) tracking for implantable devices and high-risk medical supplies. When true, all UDI-regulated items received or dispensed at this location are captured in the UDI audit trail per FDA 21 CFR Part 830.
reference: FDA — UDI Rule 21 CFR Part 830; CMS Conditions of Participation; The Joint Commission Supply Standards

description: Timestamp when this inventory location master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture, ETL incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule — Audit Controls 45 CFR §164.312(b); ISO 27001 — Information Security Management

description: Primary key for inventory_transaction

Foreign key linking to facility.care_site. Business justification: Inventory transactions (issues, transfers, returns) must be attributed to a care site for departmental cost allocation, regulatory inventory audits, and charge-back reporting. Healthcare supply chain

Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Supply issues and returns tied to specific clinical orders for charge capture audit and inventory reconciliation. Links supply movement to clinical event for billing validation and waste analysis.

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.dispense_event. Business justification: Medication dispense from ADC/pharmacy generates a supply inventory deduction transaction. Linking inventory_transaction to dispense_event enables closed-loop medication use reconciliation, controlled

Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Inventory transactions for receipts should link to the goods_receipt record for full traceability of the receiving event (who received, condition on receipt, discrepancies, UDI capture, etc.). This co

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.mar_record. Business justification: Closed-loop medication administration: nurse ADC pull creates both an inventory transaction and a MAR entry simultaneously. Linking these enables medication administration-to-inventory reconciliation,

Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: inventory_transaction has material_number and material_description which are authoritative in material_master. Every inventory movement is for a specific material item. Adding material_master_id FK en

Foreign key linking to reference.ndc_drug. Business justification: Drug dispensing and movement transactions must reference the NDC drug record for DEA controlled substance transaction reporting (ARCOS), 340B split-billing compliance, drug diversion detection, and fo

Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: inventory_transaction has purchase_order_number as STRING. For movements related to PO receipts, this should be FK for referential integrity and to enable joining to PO header attributes (vendor, cont

Foreign key linking to supply.requisition. Business justification: inventory_transaction has requisition_number as STRING. For movements related to requisition fulfillment (issues to departments), this should be FK to requisition for traceability to the requesting de

The native transaction identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP MM material document item number or Infor Lawson transaction sequence number). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (MBLNR/ZEILE); Infor Lawson Inventory Transactions; SOC 2; ISO 27001

description: Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: Supply transactions are issued to specific nursing units (ICU, ED, OR). Unit-level consumption tracking drives PAR-level management and departmental cost reporting. issuing_department_code is a denorm

Reference to the vendor or supplier associated with this inventory transaction, applicable for goods receipt and return-to-vendor movements. Links to the vendor master for supplier performance analysis and accounts payable reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Vendor Management

description: Reference to the patient encounter associated with this inventory transaction, applicable when supplies are issued directly to a patient care event. Enables implant charge capture support and supply cost allocation to specific patient encounters for revenue cycle management.
reference: Epic EHR (OpTime; ClinDoc); Cerner Millennium; CMS Conditions of Participation; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Foreign key linking to encounter.visit_procedure. Business justification: Procedure-level supply cost accounting and charge capture reconciliation require linking each inventory consumption transaction to the specific procedure performed. OR supply cost-per-case reporting a

The financial cost center to which the supply cost is allocated. Corresponds to SAP CO cost center (KOSTL) and is used for departmental cost reporting, budget variance analysis, and CMS cost report preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; GAAP and FASB; CMS Cost Reporting (Form 2552)

description: The difference between the physical count quantity and the system book quantity for physical inventory and cycle count transactions. A positive value indicates a surplus; a negative value indicates a shortage. Used for inventory accuracy measurement and shrinkage analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory (MI); Infor Lawson Cycle Count; GAAP and FASB Inventory Valuation

description: The monetary value of the physical count variance, calculated as count_variance_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Used for financial reporting of inventory adjustments and write-offs in the general ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory (MI); GAAP and FASB Inventory Valuation; CMS Cost Reporting

description: The timestamp when this inventory transaction record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security Management; HITRUST CSF; SOC 2

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values in this transaction (e.g., USD). Required for multi-entity health systems operating across different currency jurisdictions.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards

description: The receiving storage location or inventory location to which the supply item was moved. Applicable for transfer and receipt transactions. Corresponds to SAP MM receiving storage location or Infor Lawson destination location.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory

description: The date the physical goods movement or source document was created, which may differ from the posting date. Used for audit trail and reconciliation between physical activity and financial posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (BLDAT); GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards

description: The expiration date of the specific lot or batch of the supply item involved in this transaction. Critical for expiration tracking, FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, and compliance with FDA and Joint Commission requirements for expired product management.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211 (Expiration Dating); The Joint Commission (TJC) EC.02.05.01; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management

description: Total cost of the inventory transaction calculated as quantity multiplied by unit cost. Represents the monetary value of the goods movement for cost center allocation, departmental charge capture, and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Valuation; GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards; CMS Cost Reporting

description: The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the inventory transaction value is posted in SAP FI. Supports financial reconciliation between supply chain and the general ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI General Ledger; GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted goods movement. When True, the transaction negates the original movement for audit and financial correction purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement Reversal; GAAP and FASB; SOC 2 Audit Controls

description: Manufacturer lot or batch number assigned to the supply item. Critical for recall management, expiration tracking, and traceability of medical-surgical supplies and implantable devices. Corresponds to SAP MM batch number (CHARG).
reference: FDA UDI Rule; The Joint Commission (TJC); SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management

description: The externally-known SAP MM material document number (MBLNR) generated upon goods movement posting. Serves as the primary business identifier for the transaction, traceable in SAP MIGO and Infor Lawson transaction logs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement (MIGO); Infor Lawson Inventory Transactions

description: The fiscal year associated with the material document number in SAP MM. Required in combination with material_document_number to uniquely identify a goods movement document in SAP.

High-level classification of the inventory movement for analytics and reporting. 'issue' = supply issued to patient care area or department; 'return' = unused supply returned to stock; 'transfer' = movement between storage locations; 'adjustment' = manual inventory correction; 'waste' = disposal or expiration write-off; 'count' = physical or cycle count result; 'receipt' = goods receipt from vendor or PO. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: issue|return|transfer|adjustment|waste|count|receipt — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory; SAP S/4HANA MM; CMS Cost Reporting
SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement (MIGO); Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory

description: Human-readable description of the movement type code, such as 'Goods Issue to Cost Center', 'Transfer Posting', 'Physical Inventory Count Adjustment', 'Scrapping/Waste Disposal', or 'Return from Patient Care Area'. Supports reporting and analytics without requiring code lookups.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement (MIGO); Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory

description: The PAR (Periodic Automatic Replenishment) location code identifying the nursing unit, procedure room, or supply cabinet from which the item was issued or replenished. Used for par-level replenishment analysis and supply utilization reporting by care area.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain PAR Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location

description: SAP MM plant code (WERKS) representing the facility or organizational unit where the inventory transaction occurred. Maps to a hospital, clinic, or outpatient facility within the health system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Facility

description: The principal real-world business event date on which the inventory movement was posted to the ledger. Determines the accounting period for cost allocation and financial reporting. Corresponds to SAP MIGO Posting Date (BUDAT) and Infor Lawson transaction date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (BUDAT); GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards; CMS Cost Reporting

description: The quantity of the supply item involved in the inventory movement. Positive values indicate goods receipt or return; negative values indicate goods issue or write-off, consistent with SAP MM sign convention. Supports supply utilization analysis and inventory accuracy measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement (MIGO); Infor Lawson Inventory Transactions

description: Coded reason explaining why the inventory transaction occurred, particularly for adjustments, waste disposals, and returns. Examples include EXPIRED (expiration date reached), DAMAGED (item damaged), RECALLED (manufacturer recall), COUNT_VARIANCE (physical count discrepancy), PATIENT_USE (issued for patient care). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXPIRED|DAMAGED|RECALLED|COUNT_VARIANCE|PATIENT_USE|OVERSTOCK|WRONG_ITEM|THEFT|SPOILAGE|TRANSFER_REQUEST — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement Reason Code; Infor Lawson Inventory; FDA Recall Management

description: Free-text description providing additional context for the reason code, such as specific details about damage, recall notice number, or count discrepancy explanation. Supports audit trail and investigation workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; The Joint Commission (TJC) Audit Requirements

description: Indicates whether the supply item involved in this transaction is subject to an active manufacturer or FDA recall notice. When True, triggers recall management workflows and patient safety notifications.
reference: FDA Recall Management; The Joint Commission (TJC) Patient Safety; 21 CFR Part 7

description: The material document number of the original transaction that this reversal transaction cancels. Populated only when is_reversal is True. Enables full audit trail linking reversals to their originating documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement Reversal; SOC 2 Audit Controls; ISO 27001

description: Manufacturer serial number for serialized items such as implantable devices, capital equipment components, or high-value prosthetics. Enables individual unit-level traceability for patient safety and recall management.
reference: FDA UDI Rule; The Joint Commission (TJC); SAP S/4HANA MM Serial Number Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this inventory transaction was sourced. Supports data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; MEDITECH Expanse; HITRUST CSF; SOC 2

description: Indicates whether the supply item involved in this transaction is subject to sterile processing (reprocessing/sterilization) workflows. When True, the item is tracked through the sterile processing department (SPD) for reuse cycle management.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) IC.02.02.01; AAMI ST79 Sterilization Standards; AHRQ

description: Current lifecycle status of the inventory transaction. 'posted' indicates the goods movement has been successfully recorded; 'reversed' indicates a cancellation document has been posted; 'pending' indicates awaiting system confirmation; 'error' indicates a posting failure requiring resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement (MIGO); Infor Lawson Inventory Transactions

description: The FDA-mandated Unique Device Identifier (UDI) for implantable devices and high-risk medical devices. Composed of the Device Identifier (DI) and Production Identifier (PI) components. Required for implant charge capture, recall management, and FDA UDI compliance reporting.
reference: FDA UDI Rule (21 CFR Part 830); FDA GUDID Database; The Joint Commission (TJC) Implant Tracking

description: The cost per unit of the supply item at the time of the transaction, sourced from the material master valuation price or moving average price. Used for cost allocation to departments and cost center accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Valuation; GAAP and FASB — Financial Reporting Standards; CMS Cost Reporting

description: The unit of measure in which the transaction quantity is expressed, such as EA (each), BX (box), CS (case), PK (pack), or ML (milliliter). Corresponds to SAP MM base unit of measure (MEINS) or Infor Lawson unit of measure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; ISO 80000 Units of Measurement

description: The timestamp when this inventory transaction record was last modified, such as upon reversal, status change, or correction. Supports change data capture and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Security Management; HITRUST CSF; SOC 2

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the internal supply requisition record. Primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM Purchase Requisitions

description: Identifier of the hospital, clinic, or outpatient facility from which the requisition originates. Supports multi-facility health system reporting and inventory allocation.
reference: CMS Facility Reporting; The Joint Commission; Infor Lawson

description: Foreign key linking to facility.or_suite. Business justification: OR suites submit case-specific supply requisitions for implants and preference card items. Direct OR suite linkage supports surgical supply management, case cost tracking, and implant traceability — a

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.inventory. Business justification: Par-level replenishment requisitions are triggered by pharmacy inventory falling below reorder point. Linking requisition to the specific pharmacy inventory record that triggered it (is_par_triggered=

Reference to the purchase order generated from this requisition when the fulfillment method is direct purchase. Null for stock pull fulfillments. Links the requisition to the procurement and accounts payable workflow.
reference: SAP MM EBELN (Purchase Order Number); Infor Lawson PO Module; GAAP

description: Foreign key linking to provider.clinician. Business justification: Clinician-initiated supply requisitions (surgeon preference items, procedure-specific supplies) are a core healthcare supply chain process. Linking enables clinician-level spend analytics, preference

Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Supply requisitions often triggered BY clinical orders (e.g., surgical case order triggers OR supply pull, patient-specific implant order triggers procurement). Natural parent-child relationship for s

Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: Requisitions are submitted by specific nursing units requesting supplies. Unit-level requisition tracking is essential for departmental budget management and supply utilization reporting. requesting_d

Reference to the active supply contract or group purchasing organization (GPO) agreement under which this requisition's items are procured. Ensures contract compliance and enables contract utilization reporting.
reference: SAP MM Contract (EKKO); Infor Lawson Contract Management; AHRMM GPO Standards

description: Identifier of the preferred or contracted vendor for direct purchase fulfillments. Populated when fulfillment_method is 'direct_purchase' or 'consignment'. Links to the vendor master for contract pricing and performance tracking.
reference: SAP MM Vendor Master (LFA1); Infor Lawson Vendor Master; OIG Excluded Parties List

description: Foreign key linking to encounter.visit. Business justification: Patient-specific supply requisitions (e.g., custom implants, patient-specific surgical trays) must be traceable to the originating patient visit for visit-level supply cost tracking, case costing, and

Actual total cost of all fulfilled line items on the requisition, updated upon fulfillment. May differ from estimated_total_cost due to substitutions, partial fulfillments, or price changes. Used for budget variance analysis and financial reporting.
reference: SAP MM; Infor Lawson; GAAP; FASB; CMS Cost Reporting

description: Current state of the requisition within the approval workflow. Distinct from requisition_status, which tracks overall fulfillment lifecycle. Approval status specifically tracks the authorization decision by supply chain management or department leadership.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions Approval Workflow; SAP MM Release Strategy; OIG Compliance Guidelines

description: Date and time when the requisition was approved (or rejected) by the authorized approver. Used for cycle time analysis, SLA compliance, and procurement audit trails.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM; OIG Compliance Guidelines; ISO 27001

description: Fiscal budget period (year and quarter or month) to which this requisition's costs are allocated (e.g., '2024-Q3' or '2024-07'). Used for departmental budget tracking and financial close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Infor Lawson GL; GAAP; FASB

description: Free-text narrative provided by the requester explaining the clinical or operational reason for the supply request. Required for non-routine or high-cost requisitions. Supports CDI and compliance review.
reference: The Joint Commission Supply Chain Standards; CMS Conditions of Participation; Infor Lawson Requisitions

description: Date and time when the requisition record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; ISO 27001; Infor Lawson Requisitions

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this requisition (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency health systems and international procurement.

Specific physical delivery point within the facility where the requested supplies should be delivered (e.g., 'ICU 4N Med Room', 'OR Suite 3 Supply Closet', 'ED Trauma Bay'). More granular than department; used for last-mile storeroom delivery routing.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM Delivery Address; The Joint Commission

description: Estimated total cost of all line items on the requisition at time of submission, based on material master standard costs or contract pricing. Used for budget pre-authorization and spend visibility. Not a calculated aggregate — sourced directly from the requisition system at submission time.
reference: SAP MM; Infor Lawson; GAAP; FASB; CMS Cost Reporting

description: Indicates whether items on this requisition require lot/expiration date tracking at fulfillment. Applies to pharmaceuticals, biologics, sterile supplies, and reagents. Supports FIFO inventory management and recall readiness.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 211 (Pharmaceutical); The Joint Commission MM Standards; USP <797>; Infor Lawson Inventory

description: Date on which the requisition was fully fulfilled and materials were delivered to the requesting department. Used for cycle time measurement, SLA reporting, and supply chain performance analytics.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM; AHRMM Supply Chain KPIs

description: Method by which the requisition will be fulfilled. Stock pull draws from central storeroom inventory; direct purchase triggers a purchase order to a vendor; consignment pulls from vendor-managed inventory on-site; loaner is for temporary implant or device use; transfer moves stock between facilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock_pull|direct_purchase|consignment|loaner|transfer — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM; AHRMM Supply Chain Best Practices

description: General ledger account code used to classify the supply expense for financial reporting. Derived from the charge description master or material master. Supports revenue cycle and financial close processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; Infor Lawson GL; GAAP; FASB

description: Indicates whether any items on this requisition are classified as hazardous materials (e.g., chemotherapy agents, radioactive materials, flammable chemicals). Triggers special handling, transport, and storage protocols per OSHA and regulatory requirements.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 (Hazard Communication); FDA; DOT Hazmat Regulations; The Joint Commission EC Standards

description: Indicates whether any items on this requisition are classified as capital expenditures (equipment or assets above the capitalization threshold) rather than operating supply expenses. Triggers different approval and financial coding workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; GAAP; FASB ASC 360; Infor Lawson

description: Indicates whether this requisition was automatically generated by a par-level replenishment trigger rather than manually submitted. True = system-generated par replenishment; False = manual ad hoc request. Supports par management analytics and storeroom automation reporting.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions Par Management; SAP MM MRP; AHRMM Supply Chain Standards

description: Indicates whether this requisition was generated in response to a product recall event, requiring replacement of recalled medical-surgical supplies or implantable devices. Supports FDA recall compliance tracking and patient safety reporting.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 806 (Medical Device Recalls); The Joint Commission; Infor Lawson

description: Date and time when the requisition record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; ISO 27001; Infor Lawson Requisitions

description: Date by which the requested materials must be available for clinical use. Drives fulfillment prioritization, vendor lead time calculations, and storeroom scheduling. Maps to SAP MM LFDAT (Delivery Date) and Lawson required date field.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM LFDAT; The Joint Commission Supply Standards

description: Free-text field for additional instructions, special handling requirements, or communication between the requester and supply chain staff. Examples include delivery location specifics, substitution preferences, or sterile processing instructions.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM Text Fields; The Joint Commission

description: Free-text or coded reason provided by the approver when a requisition is rejected. Supports process improvement, requester feedback, and compliance documentation.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM; OIG Compliance

description: Externally-known, human-readable requisition identifier assigned by the source system (Infor Lawson or SAP MM). Used for cross-system tracking, communication with supply chain staff, and audit trail. Maps to Lawson RQ10 requisition number or SAP BANFN field.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions Module; SAP MM BANFN (Purchase Requisition Number)

description: Current workflow lifecycle state of the requisition. Drives approval routing, fulfillment actions, and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|rejected|partially_fulfilled|fulfilled|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM Purchase Requisition Status; The Joint Commission Supply Chain Standards

description: Classification of the requisition by its origination method. Par replenishment is auto-triggered when stock falls below par level; ad hoc is a manual one-time request; surgical case is linked to an OR procedure BOM; direct purchase bypasses storeroom stock. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: par_replenishment|ad_hoc|surgical_case|direct_purchase|emergency — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM Document Type; AHRMM Supply Chain Standards

description: Operational system of record from which this requisition record was sourced. Supports data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing. Values: Infor Lawson Requisitions module, SAP MM Purchase Requisitions, Epic OpTime (surgical case supply), or manual entry.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM; Epic OpTime; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312; ISO 27001

description: Natural key or internal identifier of this requisition record in the originating source system (e.g., Lawson RQ10 internal ID or SAP BANFN). Used for ETL reconciliation, deduplication, and cross-system traceability.
reference: Infor Lawson Requisitions; SAP MM BANFN; ISO 27001; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.312

description: Indicates whether items on this requisition require sterile processing or reprocessing before delivery to the requesting department. Triggers coordination with the sterile processing department (SPD) and tracks instrument tray readiness.

Reference to the vendor or manufacturer from whom the device was procured. Used for vendor performance tracking, recall management, and procurement analytics.

reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830; CMS Implant Registry Requirements; Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet

description: The anatomical location in the patient's body where the device was implanted (e.g., left hip, thoracic cavity, lumbar spine). Supports clinical documentation, implant registry reporting, and post-market surveillance.
reference: SNOMED CT (Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms); HL7 FHIR Device.location; CMS Implant Registry

description: Current lifecycle status of the implantable device. Tracks whether the device is available in inventory, has been implanted in a patient, has been explanted (removed), wasted, recalled, or quarantined pending investigation.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830; HL7 FHIR Device.status; The Joint Commission (TJC); Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: Indicates whether the device is classified as implantable per FDA UDI regulations. Implantable devices require mandatory UDI documentation at the point of care and patient-level tracking in the medical record.

description: The FDA-accredited issuing agency that assigned the UDI system used for this device. Valid values include GS1 (Global Standards One), HIBCC (Health Industry Business Communications Council), ICCBBA (International Council for Commonality in Blood Banking Automation), and IFA (Informationsstelle für Arzneispezialitäten).
The side of the body where the device was implanted (left, right, bilateral, or not applicable). Used for surgical documentation, implant registry reporting, and clinical analytics.
reference: SNOMED CT; HL7 FHIR; Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet

description: The manufacturer-assigned lot or batch number for the device. Used for recall management, quality investigations, and traceability to the manufacturing batch. Part of the Production Identifier (PI) component of the UDI.

description: The date on which the device was manufactured, as labeled by the manufacturer. Used for shelf-life calculations, quality investigations, and traceability. Part of the Production Identifier (PI) component of the UDI.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830.3; FDA GUDID; HL7 FHIR Device.manufactureDate

description: Indicates whether an adverse event or malfunction associated with this device has been determined to be reportable to the FDA under Medical Device Reporting (MDR) regulations. Triggers mandatory FDA MedWatch reporting workflow.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 803 (Medical Device Reporting); FDA MedWatch; OIG

description: The conditional, variable portion of the UDI that identifies one or more of the following: lot/batch number, serial number, expiration date, manufacturing date, and distinct identification code. Corresponds to the Production Identifier (PI) component per FDA UDI regulations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830.3; FDA GUDID; HL7 FHIR Device.udiCarrier.carrierHRF

description: The FDA classification of the recall by severity: Class I (serious adverse health consequences or death), Class II (temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences), Class III (unlikely to cause adverse health consequences). Drives urgency of recall response.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7.3; FDA Recall Database

description: Indicates whether this specific device unit has been identified as subject to an active FDA recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert. True when the device's lot number or serial number matches an active recall notice.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7 (Recall Policy); FDA MedWatch; The Joint Commission (TJC) RC.02.01.01

description: The FDA-assigned recall identification number associated with this device if it is subject to a recall. Used to cross-reference the FDA recall database and track recall remediation actions.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; FDA Recall Database; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Current status of recall remediation actions taken for this device unit. Tracks whether the facility has been notified, the device has been returned to the manufacturer, replaced, destroyed, or if no action is required.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; The Joint Commission (TJC); OIG Compliance Guidance

description: The date on which the device was received at the healthcare facility from the vendor or manufacturer. Marks the start of the facility's custody and responsibility for the device. Used for inventory aging and shelf-life calculations.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain; SAP S/4HANA MM; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: The manufacturer-assigned serial number uniquely identifying an individual device unit within a lot. Required for implantable devices and high-risk devices. Part of the Production Identifier (PI) component of the UDI.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830.3; FDA 21 CFR Part 801.45; HL7 FHIR Device.serialNumber

description: Indicates whether the device is labeled as single-use only by the manufacturer. Single-use devices (SUDs) must not be reprocessed or reused unless compliant with FDA reprocessing regulations. Critical for patient safety and regulatory compliance.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 801.109; FDA Guidance on Reprocessing SUDs; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: The operational system of record from which this UDI record was sourced. Used for data lineage, ETL (Extract Transform Load) audit, and reconciliation between supply chain and clinical systems.

description: The native identifier of this UDI record in the originating operational system (e.g., Infor Lawson item transaction ID, Epic OpTime implant record ID). Supports data lineage tracing and reconciliation with source systems.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain; SAP S/4HANA MM; Epic OpTime; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001

description: Indicates whether the device is labeled as sterile by the manufacturer. Sterile devices require specific storage conditions and handling protocols. Used by sterile processing and supply chain teams.

description: The manufacturer-specified storage condition required to maintain device integrity and sterility. Used by supply chain and sterile processing teams to ensure proper storage and prevent device degradation.

description: The full UDI string as it appears in human-readable form (HRF) on the device label, combining both the Device Identifier (DI) and Production Identifier (PI). Used for manual verification and label reconciliation.

description: The timestamp when this UDI record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing.

Foreign key linking to insurance.payer. Business justification: Healthcare supply items have payer-specific coverage policies and reimbursement codes. Real business process: determining if a supply item (especially implants, DME) is covered by a specific payer bef

Date on which the supply item was approved for addition to the organization's supply formulary by the Value Analysis Committee (VAC) or equivalent governance body. Marks the start of authorized procurement and use.
reference: The Joint Commission; CMS; Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: Standard catalog or list price per stocking unit of measure as published in the item catalog or charge description master (CDM). Used as the baseline for charge capture, contract price comparison, and cost accounting. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS CDM Requirements; SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; GAAP

description: Charge Description Master (CDM) code linking the supply item to the organization's revenue cycle charge capture system. Used for patient billing, revenue cycle management (RCM), and CMS cost reporting.

description: SAP cost center code representing the organizational unit responsible for the supply item's default cost allocation. Used for departmental budget tracking and financial reporting.
Timestamp when the material master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA and organizational data governance policies.

description: DEA controlled substance schedule (CI through CV) for pharmaceutical items. Determines storage security requirements, dispensing controls, and DEA reporting obligations. Null for non-controlled items.

description: Date on which the supply item was or is scheduled to be discontinued from the formulary and removed from active procurement. Null if the item is currently active. Used for transition planning and inventory rundown management.

description: FDA regulatory classification for medical devices: Class I (low risk, general controls), Class II (moderate risk, special controls), or Class III (high risk, premarket approval required). Drives regulatory compliance requirements, approval workflows, and recall risk stratification.

description: Indicates whether the supply item is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, labeling, and disposal per OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) and EPA regulations. Triggers safety data sheet (SDS) requirements.

description: Indicates whether the supply item is an implantable device requiring UDI tracking, patient implant card issuance, and post-market surveillance per FDA regulations. Triggers mandatory lot/serial tracking and patient-level implant documentation.

description: Indicates whether the supply item is certified latex-free. Critical for patient safety in latex-allergic patients and required for allergy-safe supply chain management per Joint Commission patient safety standards.

description: Indicates whether the supply item is supplied in a sterile state and requires sterile handling, storage, and processing. Drives sterile processing department (SPD) workflows, storage segregation, and expiration monitoring.

description: Internal classification code representing the supply item's position within the organization's commodity hierarchy (e.g., cardiovascular implants, orthopedic prosthetics, wound care). Used for spend analytics, formulary management, and par-level grouping.

description: Human-readable name of the supply item's commodity category corresponding to item_category_code. Supports reporting, catalog browsing, and clinical staff communication.

description: Full descriptive name of the supply item as it appears in the item catalog and on purchase orders, requisitions, and charge description master (CDM). Provides human-readable identification for clinical and supply chain staff.
