description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the supply item within the organization's item catalog (e.g., Infor Lawson item number or SAP material number). Used across procurement, inventory, and clinical systems as the primary business key.

description: Current lifecycle status of the material master record indicating whether the item is available for procurement and use. Controls ordering eligibility and inventory replenishment.

description: High-level classification of the supply item by its functional category within the healthcare supply chain. Drives regulatory handling, storage requirements, and charge capture rules. description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the material master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization across integrated systems (Epic, Infor Lawson, SAP), and audit compliance.

description: Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to receipt of the supply item from the preferred vendor. Used for reorder point calculation, safety stock determination, and supply chain risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Procurement

description: Indicates whether lot/batch number tracking is required for this supply item. Mandatory for implantable devices, biologics, and items subject to FDA recall management. Enables traceability from receipt to patient use.

description: The manufacturer's own catalog or part number for the supply item. Used for cross-referencing with vendor catalogs, purchase orders, and recall notices. Distinct from the internal item number.

description: Legal name of the company that manufactures the supply item. Used for vendor management, recall notifications, FDA adverse event reporting, and GPO contract compliance.
reference: FDA Device Registration; SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain

description: FDA-assigned National Drug Code (NDC) for pharmaceutical and drug-related supply items. Structured as labeler-product-package segments. Required for pharmacy formulary management, drug charge capture, and CMS billing compliance.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; CMS; 21 CFR Part 207

description: The unit of measure used when placing purchase orders with vendors (e.g., CS=case, PK=pack). May differ from the stocking unit of measure. The conversion factor between order UOM and stock UOM is used for receiving and inventory valuation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Procurement

description: Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) level representing the target on-hand quantity for this item at a stocking location. Used by supply chain technicians during PAR cart rounds to determine replenishment needs.
reference: Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The preferred vendor's own catalog or part number for this supply item. Used on purchase orders and for EDI transactions with the vendor. Supports automated order matching and invoice reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Infor Lawson Supply Chain; EDI X12 850

description: Current FDA recall status for the supply item. Class I recalls indicate serious health hazard; Class II indicate temporary adverse health consequences; Class III indicate unlikely health hazard. Triggers quarantine and removal workflows.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7; FDA Recall Database; The Joint Commission; OIG

description: Inventory quantity level at which a replenishment order is automatically triggered for this supply item. Expressed in the stocking unit of measure. Core parameter for par-level replenishment and stockout prevention.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory

description: Standard quantity to be ordered when the reorder point is reached. Expressed in the order unit of measure. Optimized based on usage patterns, lead time, and storage capacity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; Infor Lawson Supply Chain Inventory

description: Indicates whether individual serial number tracking is required for this supply item. Applies to high-value implantable devices and capital equipment requiring unique unit-level traceability for patient safety and recall management.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830; FDA UDI Rule; The Joint Commission NPSG

description: Maximum number of days the supply item can be stored from manufacture or receipt before expiration. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, expiration date tracking, and waste reduction programs.

description: Required storage environment type for the supply item. Determines physical storage assignment within the warehouse or storeroom and drives compliance with safety and regulatory storage requirements.

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this supply item. Used in conjunction with minimum storage temperature to define the acceptable temperature range for storage compliance and cold chain management.

description: Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this supply item. Critical for temperature-sensitive biologics, vaccines, and reagents. Used to assign items to appropriate storage locations (refrigerator, freezer, ambient).
reference: FDA 21 CFR; USP <1079>; The Joint Commission; OSHA

description: FDA-mandated Unique Device Identifier for implantable devices and medical equipment. Composed of a Device Identifier (DI) and Production Identifier (PI). Required for device tracking, recall management, and adverse event reporting under FDA 21 CFR Part 830.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 830; FDA UDI Rule; HIPAA; The Joint Commission

description: The base unit of measure in which the supply item is stocked, issued, and consumed (e.g., EA=each, BX=box, CS=case, PK=pack, ML=milliliter, GM=gram). Governs inventory counting, par-level management, and charge capture.

description: United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC) providing a globally standardized commodity classification for the supply item. Used for spend analytics, group purchasing organization (GPO) benchmarking, and procurement reporting.

description: Numeric conversion factor between the order unit of measure and the stocking unit of measure (e.g., 1 case = 24 each → factor = 24). Used for accurate receiving, inventory valuation, and par-level calculations.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the purchase order record in the healthcare supply chain data platform. Primary key for the purchase_order data product.

description: Reference to the healthcare facility (hospital, clinic, outpatient center) that issued this purchase order. Used for facility-level spend reporting and budget allocation.

Reference to the specific delivery location (warehouse, storeroom, department, or clinical unit) where ordered goods are to be received. May differ from the ordering facility.
reference: SAP MM Delivery Address; Infor Lawson Ship-To Location

Foreign key linking to insurance.payer. Business justification: When purchasing high-cost implantable devices or capital equipment, healthcare organizations track which payer will reimburse to ensure coverage compliance. Real business process: capital equipment pu
Reference to the vendor contract or Group Purchasing Organization (GPO) contract under which this purchase order is issued. Supports contract compliance validation and pricing verification.
reference: SAP MM Contract (ME31K); Infor Lawson Contract Management

description: Reference to the vendor (supplier) from whom goods or services are being procured. Links to the vendor master record for contract compliance and spend analytics.
reference: SAP MM Vendor Master (LFA1); Infor Lawson Vendor Master

description: The date on which goods were physically received at the ship-to location. Used to calculate vendor on-time delivery rate and actual lead time versus requested lead time.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); Infor Lawson Receiving; AHRMM Supply Chain KPIs

description: Current approval workflow status of the purchase order. Distinct from overall PO status — captures the authorization state within the procurement approval chain (e.g., department head, CFO, supply chain director).
reference: SAP MM Release Strategy (ME28); Infor Lawson Approval Workflow; OIG Compliance Guidelines

description: Date and time when the purchase order received final procurement approval. Used for approval cycle time analysis and audit trail compliance.
reference: SAP MM Release (EKKO-FRGDT); Infor Lawson Approval Log; OIG Compliance; SOC 2

description: The fiscal year against which this purchase order is budgeted and encumbered. Used for annual budget consumption tracking and year-end financial close processes.
reference: SAP FI Fiscal Year (EKKO-GJAHR); Infor Lawson Fiscal Year; GAAP; FASB

description: Business reason for cancellation of the purchase order (e.g., duplicate order, vendor unable to fulfill, product recall, budget freeze). Populated only when po_status is 'cancelled'. Used for procurement analytics and process improvement.
reference: SAP MM Cancellation Reason; Infor Lawson PO Cancellation; AHRMM Procurement Standards

description: The delivery date confirmed by the vendor in response to the purchase order. Compared against requested_delivery_date to measure vendor on-time delivery performance.
reference: SAP MM Order Acknowledgment; Infor Lawson Vendor Confirmation; AHRMM Vendor Performance Metrics

description: Financial cost center code to which the purchase order expenditure is allocated. Used for departmental budget tracking, financial reporting, and cost accounting in SAP FI/CO.

description: Date and time when the purchase order record was first created in the source procurement system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and procurement cycle time measurement.

The type or brand of credit or debit card used for the payment: Visa, MasterCard, American Express, Discover, or other. Null for non-card payment methods.
The date on which the payment was deposited into the healthcare organization's bank account or financial system.
The trace or reference number for Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) payments, used for reconciliation and tracking. Null for non-EFT payment methods.
reference: NACHA ACH Standards, Internal Treasury Standards

description: The identifier of the ERA 835 file from which this payment record was extracted, used for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: HIPAA EDI 835 Standard, CMS Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Standards

description: The date and time when the payment record was last modified or updated in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
reference: Internal Audit and Compliance Standards, HIPAA Security Rule

description: The identifier of the lockbox service or location through which the payment was received, if applicable. Null for non-lockbox payments.
Free-text notes or comments related to the payment, including special instructions, reconciliation notes, or explanations for exceptions.
The financial category or classification of the payment based on patient responsibility and insurance coverage: copay, coinsurance, deductible, full payment, or partial payment.
reference: CMS Revenue Cycle Management Standards, Epic Resolute PB/HB

description: The date on which the payment was made or received by the healthcare organization. This is the business event date for the payment transaction.
reference: GAAP, FASB, CMS Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: The financial instrument or mechanism used to make the payment: Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT), check, credit card, debit card, cash, or wire transfer.
reference: PCI DSS, Epic Resolute PB/HB, Cerner Revenue Cycle

description: Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the payment transaction, used for tracking and reconciliation.
reference: Epic Resolute PB/HB, Cerner Revenue Cycle

description: The origin or source of the payment: insurance payer, patient self-pay, guarantor, third-party, government program, or charity care.
reference: CMS Revenue Cycle Management Standards, Epic Resolute PB/HB

description: The current lifecycle status of the payment: pending (received but not yet posted), posted (recorded in system), applied (allocated to charges), reversed (payment reversed), refunded (payment returned to payer), or voided (payment cancelled).
reference: Epic Resolute PB/HB, Cerner Revenue Cycle

description: The classification or category of the payment: standard (regular payment against charges), prepayment (payment before service), overpayment (excess payment requiring refund), adjustment (correction or write-off), or settlement (negotiated payment).
reference: GAAP, FASB, CMS Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: The date on which the payment was posted or recorded in the revenue cycle management system and applied to patient or payer accounts.
reference: GAAP, FASB, Epic Resolute PB/HB, Cerner Revenue Cycle

description: The status of the payment posting and application process: unposted (not yet recorded), posted (recorded but not applied), partially applied (some amount applied), or fully applied (entire amount allocated).
reference: Epic Resolute PB/HB, Cerner Revenue Cycle

description: The amount of this payment that has been refunded to the payer or patient, in US dollars (USD). Null if no refund has been issued.
reference: GAAP, FASB, CMS Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: The date on which the refund was issued to the payer or patient. Null if no refund has been issued.
reference: GAAP, FASB, Internal Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: Indicates whether a refund has been issued for this payment. True if refunded, False otherwise.
reference: GAAP, FASB, Internal Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: The business reason or explanation for issuing the refund, such as overpayment, duplicate payment, or service cancellation. Null if no refund has been issued.
The date on which the payment was reversed or cancelled. Null if the payment has not been reversed.
reference: GAAP, FASB, Internal Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: Indicates whether this payment has been reversed or cancelled. True if reversed, False otherwise.
reference: GAAP, FASB, Internal Revenue Cycle Management Standards

description: The business reason or explanation for reversing the payment, such as duplicate payment, incorrect amount, or payer request. Null if the payment has not been reversed.
The portion of the payment amount that remains unapplied or unallocated to specific charges, held as credit on the account, in US dollars (USD).
Unique identifier for the payment plan. Primary key.
Reference to the guarantor who is financially responsible for this payment plan.
Reference to the patient who is the primary party responsible for this payment plan.

Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Payment plans are financial agreements issued by a specific org_provider (hospital/health system). IRS 501(r) compliance, financial assistance reporting, and org-level collections management require k
Reference to the patient account for which this payment plan was established.
Actual date when the payment plan was completed, either through full payment or early payoff. Null if plan is still active or was cancelled/defaulted.
Date when the payment plan agreement was signed by the patient or guarantor.
Indicates whether the patient or guarantor has signed the payment plan agreement. True means a signed agreement is on file, false means signature is pending or not required.
Date when the payment plan was reviewed and approved by authorized staff.

Indicates whether automatic recurring payments are enabled for this payment plan. True means installments are automatically charged to the stored payment method on the due date, false means manual payment is required.
Date when the payment plan was cancelled by either the patient or the healthcare organization.
Free-text explanation of why the payment plan was cancelled, including patient request, non-compliance, account paid in full, or administrative reasons.
Timestamp when the payment plan record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail for record creation.
Date when the payment plan was declared in default due to missed payments exceeding the grace period.
Explanation of why the payment plan defaulted, such as consecutive missed payments, insufficient funds, or patient non-response.
Initial payment made at the time of plan enrollment, if required. This amount is applied to the balance before installments begin. Expressed in USD.
Scheduled date when the payment plan is expected to be completed based on the original terms. Nullable for open-ended plans.
Date when the payment plan becomes active and the first installment is due. This is the binding start date of the agreement.

Channel through which the patient or guarantor enrolled in the payment plan. Patient portal indicates online self-service enrollment, phone indicates enrollment via call center, in-person indicates enrollment at a facility registration or billing desk, mail indicates paper application, email indicates enrollment via email correspondence, and mobile app indicates enrollment through a mobile application.
Date when the patient or guarantor enrolled in the payment plan.
Total finance charges or interest applied to the payment plan over its lifetime. Zero for interest-free plans. Expressed in USD.

Code identifying the financial assistance or charity care program under which this payment plan was approved, if applicable. Links to the organizations financial assistance policy and eligibility criteria.
Number of days after the installment due date during which a late payment can be made without penalty or default.
Fixed amount due for each scheduled installment payment. Expressed in USD.

Frequency at which installment payments are scheduled. Weekly indicates every 7 days, biweekly every 14 days, monthly on the same day each month, quarterly every 3 months, and custom indicates a non-standard schedule.
Count of installment payments that have been successfully received and posted to date.
Count of installment payments still outstanding to complete the payment plan.
Annual percentage rate (APR) applied to the payment plan balance, if applicable. Zero for interest-free plans. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).
Timestamp when the payment plan record was most recently updated. Represents the audit trail for record modification.
Amount of the most recent installment payment received. Expressed in USD.
Date when the most recent installment payment was received and posted.
Standard late fee charged for each missed or late installment payment, as defined in the payment plan agreement. Expressed in USD.
Total number of installment payments that were missed or not received by the due date during the life of the payment plan.
Date when the next scheduled installment payment is due. Null if plan is completed, cancelled, or defaulted.
Free-text notes and comments related to the payment plan, including special arrangements, patient circumstances, or administrative instructions.
Total number of scheduled installment payments required to complete the payment plan.
Total outstanding patient balance at the time the payment plan was established. This is the starting principal amount before any plan payments are applied. Expressed in USD.

Primary payment instrument used for installment payments. Credit card and debit card indicate card-based payments, ACH (Automated Clearing House) indicates electronic bank transfer, check indicates paper check, cash indicates in-person cash payment, and money order indicates certified payment instrument.
reference: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)

description: Externally visible unique business identifier for the payment plan, used in patient communications and statements.

Current lifecycle status of the payment plan. Pending indicates awaiting approval or first payment, active indicates plan is in good standing with payments being made, completed indicates all installments paid in full, defaulted indicates missed payments beyond grace period, cancelled indicates plan was terminated by patient or organization, suspended indicates temporarily paused by mutual agreement.

Classification of the payment plan based on terms and eligibility criteria. Standard plans follow default terms, interest-free plans waive interest charges, hardship plans are for patients with demonstrated financial need, extended plans have longer durations, short-term plans are for smaller balances, and custom plans have negotiated terms.
Current outstanding balance on the payment plan after all payments to date have been applied. Expressed in USD.
Cumulative sum of all payments received and applied to this payment plan to date, including down payment and all installments. Expressed in USD.
Total amount to be paid under the payment plan, including any interest, fees, or finance charges. May equal original balance for interest-free plans. Expressed in USD.
Unique identifier for the patient billing statement. Primary key.
Reference to the guarantor or patient responsible for payment of this statement.
Reference to the patient for whom services were rendered and billed on this statement.

Reference to an active payment plan or financial assistance arrangement associated with this statement.
Total adjustments (credits, write-offs, contractual adjustments) applied during the current statement cycle.
The aging category of the outstanding balance based on the number of days since the original service date or first statement.
The first line of the guarantor or patient billing address to which the statement was sent.
The second line of the guarantor or patient billing address (apartment, suite, unit number).
The city of the guarantor or patient billing address.
The three-letter ISO country code of the guarantor or patient billing address.
The postal code or ZIP code of the guarantor or patient billing address.
The state or province of the guarantor or patient billing address.
The current collection status indicating whether the account is in collections, legal action, or bad debt.
The date and time when this statement record was first created in the system.
New charges added during the current statement cycle that are included in this statement.
The billing cycle or period identifier for this statement (e.g., monthly cycle, weekly cycle, ad-hoc).
The method or channel used to deliver the statement to the guarantor or patient.
The current delivery status indicating whether the statement successfully reached the guarantor or patient.
The date and time when the statement was delivered or sent to the guarantor or patient.
The email address to which electronic statements or notifications were sent.
Indicates whether the guarantor or patient has been approved for financial assistance or charity care that applies to this statement.
Amount currently pending adjudication or payment from insurance payers, not yet reflected in patient responsibility.
The two-letter ISO language code indicating the preferred language for statement communication.
The date and time when this statement record was last updated or modified.
The amount of the most recent payment received from the guarantor or patient.
The date of the most recent payment received from the guarantor or patient on this account.
Custom message or note printed on the statement for the guarantor or patient (e.g., payment reminders, financial assistance information).
The minimum payment amount requested from the guarantor or patient for this statement cycle.
The date by which payment is requested or expected from the guarantor or patient.
Indicates whether the guarantor or patient is enrolled in a payment plan for the balance on this statement.
Total payments received from the guarantor or patient during the current statement cycle.
The contact phone number of the guarantor or patient for statement inquiries and follow-up.
The outstanding balance carried forward from the prior statement cycle before current charges and payments.
The date when a mailed statement was returned as undeliverable.
Indicates whether a mailed paper statement was returned as undeliverable by the postal service.
The reason provided by the postal service for returning the statement (e.g., address unknown, moved, refused).
The date the statement was generated and issued to the guarantor or patient.
Externally-known unique statement number printed on the billing statement and used for patient reference and payment tracking.
reference: Epic Resolute PB/HB Statement Numbering Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the statement indicating its processing and delivery state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: generated|sent|delivered|returned|paid|partial_paid|outstanding — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The format or medium of the statement delivery (paper mailed, electronic via portal, email, SMS notification).
Indicates whether statement generation and delivery has been suppressed for this account due to patient request, legal hold, or other reason.
The reason why statement delivery was suppressed (e.g., patient request, deceased, legal hold, bankruptcy).
The total outstanding balance owed by the guarantor or patient as of the statement date, including all charges, adjustments, and prior payments.

Date and time the payer completed adjudication and determined payment or denial for this claim.
Total amount of contractual adjustments, write-offs, and other reductions applied by the payer. Calculated as billed amount minus allowed amount.
Date the patient was admitted to the facility for inpatient or observation care. Required for institutional claims.
Date the appeal was formally submitted to the payer for reconsideration of the claim denial or underpayment.
Indicates whether an appeal has been filed for this claim following a denial or partial payment. True if appeal is in process or completed.
Three-digit code on UB-04 institutional claims indicating the type of facility, care type, and billing sequence (e.g., 111 for hospital inpatient admit through discharge).
Externally-known unique claim identifier assigned by the provider organization. Used for tracking and correspondence with payers.

Current lifecycle status of the claim in the revenue cycle management process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|accepted|rejected|adjudicated|denied|appealed|paid|voided — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of the claim based on the type of service and billing form used. Professional claims use CMS-1500 form, institutional claims use UB-04 form.
Indicates whether this claim involves coordination of benefits with multiple payers. True when patient has primary and secondary insurance coverage.
Date and time this claim record was first created in the revenue cycle management system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this claim. Typically USD for US healthcare claims.
Standardized code indicating the reason the claim or a portion of the claim was denied by the payer. Used for denial management and appeals.
Human-readable description of why the claim was denied, corresponding to the denial reason code.
Date the patient was discharged from the facility. Required for institutional claims to calculate length of stay.
Date and time the payer issued payment to the provider for this claim. Corresponds to the remittance advice date.
Total amount the patient is responsible for paying, including deductibles, copayments, and coinsurance as determined by the payer.
Unique claim identifier assigned by the payer organization upon receipt or adjudication. Also known as payer control number (PCN).
Two-digit code identifying the location where services were rendered (e.g., 11 for office, 21 for inpatient hospital, 23 for emergency department).
reference: CMS Place of Service Code Set

description: Indicates whether this claim was submitted to the primary insurance payer. True for primary claims, false for secondary or tertiary claims.
Indicates whether this claim has been selected for or is under review by a Recovery Audit Contractor for potential overpayment recovery.
Start date of the service period covered by this claim. For professional claims, this is the date services began.
End date of the service period covered by this claim. For single-day services, this equals service_from_date.
Name of the operational system from which this claim record originated (e.g., Epic Resolute PB, Epic Resolute HB, Cerner Revenue Cycle).
Original unique identifier for this claim in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Channel or method used to submit the claim to the payer. EDI 837P for professional claims, EDI 837I for institutional claims.
Date and time the claim was submitted to the payer for adjudication. Critical for tracking timely filing requirements.
Total amount the payer has determined is allowable for the services rendered, based on contracted rates or fee schedules. May be less than billed amount.
Total charges submitted to the payer for all services on this claim, before any adjustments or payments. Represents the provider's full charge master pricing.
Total amount paid by the payer to the provider for this claim, after applying deductibles, coinsurance, and other patient responsibility amounts.
Date and time this claim record was last modified in the revenue cycle management system.

Reference to the parent claim for which this status transition was recorded. Links to the claim master record.

Foreign key linking to claim.denial. Business justification: Claim status transitions to denied states are triggered by specific denial events. The status_history product has denial_category (STRING) as a denormalized classification field. Adding denial_id FK l

Foreign key linking to claim.remittance. Business justification: Claim status transitions are frequently triggered by remittance (ERA) receipt events — when a payer sends an ERA, the claim status changes to paid/partially paid/denied. The status_history product has

Boolean flag indicating whether this status transition is associated with an appeal or reconsideration request. True if the status change is part of an appeal workflow; False otherwise. Supports denial management and appeals tracking.

business_glossary_term: Check or Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) Number
description: The check number or Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) trace number associated with the payment that triggered this status transition to a paid state. Null if no payment was issued. Used for payment reconciliation and cash application.
The unique trace or reference number assigned by the clearinghouse for this status update transaction. Used for troubleshooting and reconciliation with clearinghouse reports.
The payer sequence number in coordination of benefits (COB) scenarios (1 for primary, 2 for secondary, 3 for tertiary). Indicates which payer this status applies to when multiple payers are involved.
reference: ANSI X12 837 - Healthcare Claim, CMS Coordination of Benefits Guidelines

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this status transition is associated with a corrected or resubmitted claim (frequency type code 7 in ANSI X12 837). True if this is a corrected claim status; False for original claim submissions.
reference: ANSI X12 837 - Healthcare Claim, CMS Claims Processing Manual

description: The timestamp when this status history record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail creation time, which may differ slightly from the effective_timestamp if there is processing lag.
reference: HITRUST CSF - Audit Logging, SOC 2 - Audit Trail Requirements

description: The number of calendar days the claim remained in the prior status before this transition. Calculated metric used for aging analysis, bottleneck identification, and workflow optimization in Revenue Cycle Management (RCM).

The precise date and time when this status became effective for the claim. Represents the real-world business event time of the status transition, critical for Service Level Agreement (SLA) monitoring and timely filing compliance tracking.

Boolean flag indicating whether this status represents a terminal state in the claim lifecycle (e.g., fully paid, denied and closed, written off). True if no further status transitions are expected; False if the claim remains in an active workflow state.
The timestamp when this status history record was last modified. Typically immutable for status history records, but may be updated if corrections or annotations are applied.
reference: HITRUST CSF - Audit Logging, SOC 2 - Audit Trail Requirements

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by revenue cycle staff or system processes to provide additional context for this status transition. May include follow-up actions, payer correspondence details, or resolution steps.

The unique identifier assigned by the payer to this claim, as reported in the 277 or 835 transaction. Critical for payer correspondence, appeals, and coordination of benefits (COB) across Health Maintenance Organization (HMO), Preferred Provider Organization (PPO), Point of Service (POS), Medicare, and Medicaid payers.
reference: ANSI X12 835 - Healthcare Claim Payment/Advice, CMS Medicare Claims Processing Manual

description: The standardized response code provided by the payer in the Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) or 277 transaction, indicating the reason for the status (e.g., claim adjustment reason codes, remark codes). Aligns with CARC and RARC code sets.
reference: ANSI X12 835 - Healthcare Claim Payment/Advice, CMS CARC/RARC Code Lists

description: Human-readable explanation of the payer response code, providing additional context for denial management, appeals, and clinical documentation improvement (CDI) efforts.
The status code that was in effect immediately before this transition. Enables tracking of status progression and identification of status reversals or loops.

Boolean indicator of whether this status transition occurred within the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) timeframe for the claim type and payer contract. True if compliant; False if SLA was breached. Supports operational performance monitoring and payer contract compliance.
reference: CMS Timely Filing Requirements, Payer Contract Terms

description: The system or module that originated this status transition (e.g., 'Epic Resolute PB', 'Cerner Revenue Cycle', 'Payer Portal', 'Clearinghouse', 'Manual Entry'). Enables traceability and audit of status change origins.

Classification of the status origin: whether it is a payer-defined status received via Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) or 277 response, an internal workflow state, a system-generated transition, or a manual override by revenue cycle staff.
reference: ANSI X12 835 - Healthcare Claim Payment/Advice, ANSI X12 277 - Claim Status Notification

description: The standardized code representing the claim status at this point in time. Aligns with ANSI X12 277 Claim Status Category Codes and internal workflow states.

Human-readable description of the claim status, providing context for the status code (e.g., 'Submitted to Payer', 'Pending Additional Information', 'Adjudicated - Paid', 'Denied - Medical Necessity').

Internal reason or note explaining why this status transition occurred, particularly for manual overrides, appeals, or corrected claims. Supports denial management and Recovery Audit Contractor (RAC) audit responses.

The ANSI X12 transaction set control number or identifier associated with the Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transaction that triggered this status change (e.g., 277 response, 835 ERA). Enables correlation with source EDI files for audit and reconciliation.
reference: ANSI X12 Standards - Transaction Set Control

description: The name of the automated process, batch job, or workflow engine that triggered this status transition (e.g., 'Nightly Clearinghouse Sync', 'ERA Import Job', 'Auto-Denial Workflow'). Null if manually triggered.
Boolean flag indicating whether this status transition represents a voided or cancelled claim submission (frequency type code 8 in ANSI X12 837). True if the claim was voided; False otherwise.

The name of the revenue cycle work queue or team to which this claim was assigned following this status transition (e.g., 'Denials Management', 'Prior Auth Follow-Up', 'Payment Posting', 'Appeals'). Supports workflow routing and workload balancing.
Primary key for diagnosis_link
Reference to the parent claim (UB-04 or CMS-1500) to which this diagnosis is attached. Required for all diagnosis links.

business_glossary_term: Diagnosis Code Set Version Id (Foreign Key)

Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Diagnosis coding requires ICD code metadata for validation, DRG grouping, HCC risk adjustment, denial prevention, and coding compliance audits. Every diagnosis_link.diagnosis_code must resolve to refe

Indicates whether this diagnosis is currently active on the claim. False if the diagnosis was removed or replaced during coding review or appeals.
Indicates whether a CDI query was issued to the provider to clarify or support this diagnosis. True if CDI intervention occurred.
Indicates whether this diagnosis represents a chronic condition per CMS Chronic Condition Warehouse (CCW) definitions. Used for population health management and risk adjustment.
reference: CMS Chronic Conditions Data Warehouse, HEDIS Chronic Condition Measures

description: Origin of the diagnosis code assignment. Physician=provider-documented, Coder=HIM professional coding, CDI=Clinical Documentation Improvement query, CAC=Computer-Assisted Coding, Imported=external system.
reference: AHIMA Coding Standards, 3M CDI Best Practices

description: Date and time when this diagnosis code was assigned or last validated by a coder. Used for coding turnaround time (TAT) measurement and compliance audits.
Indicates whether this diagnosis qualifies as a complication or comorbidity (CC) or major complication or comorbidity (MCC) per MS-DRG grouper logic. Impacts DRG severity and reimbursement.
reference: CMS MS-DRG Definitions Manual, ICD-10-CM CC/MCC List

description: Date and time when this claim diagnosis link record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.
Indicates whether this diagnosis has historically high denial rates or requires additional documentation to support medical necessity. Used for denial prevention and appeals management.
High-level clinical category or chapter of the ICD-10-CM code (e.g., Endocrine, Circulatory, Injury). Derived from the first character of the ICD-10-CM code for analytics and quality reporting.

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases 10th Revision Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) Diagnosis Code
description: The ICD-10-CM diagnosis code assigned to this claim. Alphanumeric code up to 7 characters (e.g., E11.9, S72.001A). Used for DRG grouping, quality reporting, and payer adjudication.
reference: ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, WHO ICD-10, CMS

description: Date when the diagnosis was clinically established or documented by the provider. May differ from claim service date.
Human-readable description of the ICD-10-CM diagnosis code. Provides clinical context for reporting and audit purposes.
Letter (A-L) linking this diagnosis to specific service lines on CMS-1500 professional claims. Maps diagnosis to procedures/services that treat the condition.
Ordinal position of this diagnosis on the claim. Sequence 1 is the principal diagnosis. Supports up to 25 diagnosis codes per UB-04 and CMS-1500 specifications.
reference: CMS UB-04 Form Locator 67, CMS-1500 Box 21

description: Classification of the diagnosis role on the claim. Principal diagnosis drives DRG assignment. Admitting diagnosis reflects condition at admission. External cause codes (V, W, X, Y) capture injury circumstances.
reference: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines, UB-04 and CMS-1500 Instructions

description: Indicates whether this diagnosis was used as input to the DRG grouper algorithm for reimbursement calculation. True if the diagnosis influenced the assigned DRG.
