Unique identifier for the member (patient) who holds this insurance coverage. Links to the patient domain master record.
reference: HL7 FHIR Patient Resource, HIPAA 45 CFR 164.514

description: Unique identifier for the insurance payer organization responsible for adjudicating and paying claims under this coverage.
Self-referencing FK on eligibility_span (prior_eligibility_span_id)
Unique identifier for the provider network associated with this eligibility span. Defines which providers are in-network for this coverage.
reference: HL7 FHIR Organization, CMS Network Adequacy

description: Unique identifier for the primary subscriber (policyholder) of the insurance plan. May be the same as member_id if the member is the subscriber, or different if the member is a dependent.
The end date of the benefit period for deductible and out-of-pocket maximum accumulation. Deductibles and accumulators reset after this date.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage, CMS Benefit Period

description: The start date of the benefit period for deductible and out-of-pocket maximum accumulation. Typically aligns with plan year or calendar year.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage, CMS Benefit Period

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Indicator
description: Indicates whether this eligibility span represents COBRA continuation coverage. True if the member is continuing coverage under COBRA provisions after a qualifying event.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Qualifying Event
description: The qualifying event that triggered COBRA continuation coverage eligibility. Populated only when cobra_indicator is true.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Qualifying Event Date
description: The date on which the COBRA qualifying event occurred. Used to determine COBRA continuation period limits.
Indicates whether coordination of benefits rules apply because the member has multiple insurance coverages. True if this is a secondary or tertiary payer.
reference: NAIC Model COB Regulation, HL7 FHIR Coverage.order

description: The tier of coverage indicating whether the plan covers only the subscriber or includes dependents.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage.class, X12 834 Benefit Enrollment

description: Indicates the order of liability when multiple coverages exist. Primary coverage pays first; secondary and tertiary coverages pay after primary according to COB rules.
reference: NAIC Model COB Regulation, HL7 FHIR Coverage.order

description: The type of healthcare services covered under this eligibility span. Defines the scope of benefits available to the member.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage.type, CMS Coverage Type Codes

description: The timestamp when this eligibility span record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail for record creation.
Indicates whether the member is dually eligible for both Medicare and Medicaid. True if the member qualifies for both programs simultaneously.

Specifies the type of dual eligibility status. QMB (Qualified Medicare Beneficiary), SLMB (Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary), QI (Qualifying Individual), QDWI (Qualified Disabled and Working Individual). Populated only when dual_eligibility_indicator is true.
The date on which the member's insurance coverage terminates under this eligibility span. Null indicates open-ended active coverage.
The date on which the member's insurance coverage becomes effective under this eligibility span. Represents the beginning of the continuous eligibility period.

Current lifecycle status of the eligibility span. Active indicates coverage is in force; terminated indicates coverage has ended; suspended indicates temporary hold; COBRA indicates continuation coverage under COBRA provisions; pending indicates coverage not yet effective.
The date on which eligibility was last verified with the payer through a 270/271 transaction or other verification method.

The status of the most recent eligibility verification attempt. Verified indicates successful confirmation; unverified indicates no recent verification; failed indicates verification was attempted but unsuccessful.
The method by which the enrollment was submitted. Distinguishes between online enrollment, paper application, phone enrollment, in-person enrollment, or automatic enrollment.
reference: X12 834 Benefit Enrollment, CMS Enrollment Standards

description: The source or channel through which the member enrolled in this coverage. Indicates whether enrollment was through an employer, exchange, direct purchase, broker, or government program.
Indicates whether this coverage was purchased through a health insurance exchange (marketplace) established under the Affordable Care Act.
Indicates whether the member has Medicaid coverage. True if the member is enrolled in a state Medicaid program.
Indicates whether the member has Medicare coverage. True if the member is enrolled in Medicare Part A, B, C, or D.
Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this eligibility span. Used to capture context not represented in structured fields.
The date on which the current primary care physician was assigned to or selected by the member.
Indicates whether the member is required to select and maintain a primary care physician under this plan. True for HMO and POS plans that require PCP designation.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage, HMO Model Act

description: The premium amount charged for this coverage, typically per billing period. Represents the member or employer contribution for maintaining coverage.
The frequency at which premium payments are due for this coverage.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage, Insurance Policy Standards

description: Indicates whether certain services under this plan require prior authorization from the payer before services are rendered.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage, CMS Prior Authorization

description: Indicates whether the member must obtain a referral from their PCP before seeing a specialist. True for HMO plans with referral requirements.
reference: HL7 FHIR Coverage, Managed Care Regulations

description: Defines the relationship of the member to the primary subscriber. Determines dependent status and eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|child|other_dependent|life_partner|domestic_partner|parent|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The name or identifier of the source system from which this eligibility span record originated. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Data Governance Standards, HL7 FHIR Provenance

description: The amount of premium subsidy or tax credit applied to reduce the member's premium cost. Applicable for exchange plans with Advanced Premium Tax Credits (APTC).
The type of subsidy applied to this coverage. APTC (Advanced Premium Tax Credit), CSR (Cost-Sharing Reduction), employer subsidy, state subsidy, or none.

The reason for termination of the eligibility span, if applicable. Populated only when eligibility_status is terminated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|involuntary|employment_ended|non_payment|death|divorce|age_limit|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The user or system identifier that last updated this eligibility span record. Used for audit and accountability purposes.
The timestamp when this eligibility span record was last modified. Represents the audit trail for record updates.

Unique identifier for the employer group. Primary key for this entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key that uniquely identifies each employer group record in the master data management system.
Self-referencing FK on employer_group (parent_employer_group_id)
Identifier for the health insurance payer (carrier) that underwrites or administers this employer group. Links to the payer master entity.
reference: HIPAA Trading Partner Agreements, NAIC Company Database

description: Identifier for the provider network assigned to this employer group. Determines which providers are in-network for members of this group. Links to the network master entity.
reference: Network Adequacy Standards, State Insurance Regulations

business_glossary_term: Affordable Care Act (ACA) Applicable Large Employer (ALE) Indicator
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this employer group meets the ACA definition of an Applicable Large Employer (50 or more full-time equivalent employees). ALEs are subject to the employer shared responsibility mandate and must offer affordable minimum essential coverage or face penalties.
reference: ACA Section 4980H (Employer Shared Responsibility), IRS Form 1094-C and 1095-C Reporting

description: The first line of the employer group's primary business address. Typically includes street number and street name. Used for premium billing, regulatory correspondence, and legal notices.
reference: USPS Address Standards, HIPAA Privacy Rule

description: The second line of the employer group's primary business address. Typically includes suite, floor, or building number. Optional field.
The frequency at which premium invoices are generated and sent to the employer group. Most groups are billed monthly, but some large groups may have quarterly or annual billing cycles.
reference: Premium Billing Standards, ERISA Fiduciary Rules

description: The city of the employer group's primary business address. Used for geographic rating, network assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Administrator
description: The entity responsible for administering COBRA continuation coverage for terminated employees and qualifying dependents. Payer: health plan administers. Employer: employer self-administers. TPA: third party administrator handles. External vendor: specialized COBRA vendor.
reference: COBRA (Title X of the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985), DOL COBRA Regulations

description: The email address of the primary employer contact for benefits administration. Used for enrollment files, billing statements, and plan communications.
The name of the primary contact person at the employer organization for benefits administration, enrollment, and billing matters. Typically the HR director or benefits manager.
The phone number of the primary employer contact for benefits administration. Used for urgent enrollment issues, billing inquiries, and plan support.
The three-letter ISO country code of the employer group's primary business address. Typically USA for domestic groups. Used for international employer groups and regulatory jurisdiction.
The date and time when this employer group record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Data Governance Standards, Audit Trail Requirements

description: The date on which the employer group's health insurance coverage becomes active and members are eligible for benefits. This is the contract start date.
reference: HIPAA 834 Benefit Enrollment and Maintenance, Insurance Contract Standards

description: The number of eligible employees (subscribers) in the employer group, excluding dependents. Used for group size classification, premium calculations, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ERISA Reporting Requirements, ACA Employer Mandate

description: The percentage of the employee-only premium that the employer pays. The employee pays the remainder. Typical employer contributions range from 50% to 100%. Used for ACA affordability calculations and premium billing.
reference: ACA Employer Shared Responsibility (Employer Mandate), ERISA Plan Document

description: The federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) assigned by the IRS, also known as the Federal Tax Identification Number. This nine-digit number uniquely identifies the employer for tax reporting and regulatory purposes. Format: XX-XXXXXXX.

business_glossary_term: Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) Plan Indicator
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this employer group's health plan is subject to ERISA federal regulations. True for most private employer plans. False for government and church plans which are ERISA-exempt.
reference: ERISA Title I, DOL ERISA Exemptions

description: The financial arrangement model for how the employer group funds its health benefits. Fully insured: payer assumes all risk. Self-funded: employer assumes claims risk. Level-funded: hybrid with predictable monthly payments. Minimum premium: employer funds expected claims, payer covers excess. Stop-loss only: employer self-funds with catastrophic reinsurance.
reference: ERISA Section 3, State Insurance Regulations, Self-Funded Plan Administration Standards

description: The number of days after the premium due date during which the employer group can make payment without coverage lapsing. Typically 30 days for group coverage. During the grace period, coverage remains active but claims may be pended.
reference: State Insurance Grace Period Requirements, ERISA Plan Document Standards

description: The legal or doing-business-as name of the employer organization that sponsors the health insurance coverage for its employees. This is the primary human-readable identifier for the group.

The externally-known unique business identifier assigned to the employer group by the payer. This is the group number printed on member ID cards and used in all billing and claims transactions. Also known as the policy group number or contract group number.
reference: HIPAA 834 Benefit Enrollment and Maintenance, X12 Standard

description: The total number of enrolled lives (employees and dependents) covered under this employer group. This count determines small group vs. large group classification for regulatory purposes and impacts premium rating methodologies.
reference: ACA Small Group Market Definition (under 50 employees), State Insurance Regulations

description: The current lifecycle status of the employer group contract. Active: coverage in force. Pending: application submitted, not yet effective. Suspended: temporarily inactive, may be reinstated. Terminated: contract ended by mutual agreement. Cancelled: contract ended by payer. Lapsed: contract ended due to non-payment.
reference: State Insurance Contract Law, ERISA Plan Termination Rules

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this employer group's benefit plan is HSA-qualified (high deductible health plan meeting IRS requirements). If true, employees can contribute pre-tax dollars to a Health Savings Account.
reference: IRS Publication 969 (Health Savings Accounts), IRC Section 223

description: The risk classification based on the employer's industry and occupation mix. Used in underwriting and premium rating. Low: office/professional. Moderate: light manufacturing/retail. High: construction/transportation. Hazardous: mining/heavy industrial.
reference: Occupational Risk Classification Standards, Workers Compensation Industry Codes

description: The minimum percentage of eligible employees that must enroll in the health plan for the group to qualify for coverage. Typically 75% for small groups. Used during initial underwriting and at renewal to prevent adverse selection.
reference: State Small Group Insurance Regulations, Underwriting Standards

business_glossary_term: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code
description: The six-digit NAICS code that classifies the employer's industry sector. NAICS has largely replaced SIC for statistical and regulatory purposes. Used for industry analysis, risk stratification, and regulatory reporting.
The method by which the employer group remits premium payments to the payer. ACH and wire transfer are most common for large groups. Check and credit card may be used by small groups.
reference: Premium Payment Processing Standards, PCI DSS

description: The category of organization that sponsors the health plan. Private employer: for-profit or non-profit business. Union trust: labor union-sponsored plan. Government employer: federal, state, or local government entity. Association: group of small employers. Taft-Hartley: jointly trusteed union-management plan. MEWA: Multiple Employer Welfare Arrangement.
reference: ERISA Title I, Taft-Hartley Act Section 302(c)(5), DOL MEWA Regulations

description: The ZIP code or ZIP+4 code of the employer group's primary business address. Used for geographic rating, network assignment, and premium calculations. Format: XXXXX or XXXXX-XXXX.

The number of months for which the premium rates are guaranteed not to increase. Typically 12 months (annual rate guarantee) for most groups. Some large groups may negotiate multi-year rate guarantees.
reference: Insurance Contract Terms, Rate Guarantee Provisions

description: The annual date on which the employer group's contract renews. Typically the anniversary of the original effective date. Used for renewal underwriting, rate adjustments, and plan design changes.
reference: Insurance Contract Renewal Standards, ACA Rate Review Requirements

description: The four-digit Standard Industrial Classification code that categorizes the employer's primary line of business or industry. Used for risk assessment, underwriting, and industry benchmarking.
reference: U.S. Department of Labor SIC Manual

description: The state code that determines which state insurance regulations apply to this employer group. For fully insured plans, this is typically the state where the policy is issued. For self-funded ERISA plans, this may differ from the employer's physical location. Format: two uppercase letters.
reference: ERISA Preemption Rules, State Insurance Situs Regulations

description: The two-letter state abbreviation of the employer group's primary business address. Determines state insurance regulations, mandated benefits, and tax jurisdiction. Format: two uppercase letters (e.g., CA, NY, TX).
reference: USPS State Abbreviations, NAIC State Insurance Regulations

description: The date on which the employer group's coverage ends. Null if the group is currently active. Once set, no new claims with service dates after this date will be paid under this group.
reference: ERISA Plan Termination Rules, COBRA Continuation Coverage Requirements

description: The risk classification assigned to this employer group during underwriting. Standard: average risk. Preferred: lower than average risk, may receive rate discounts. Substandard: higher than average risk, may have rate loadings or exclusions. Declined: group was not accepted for coverage.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Group Health Insurance, Underwriting Standards

description: The date and time when this employer group record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Data Governance Standards, Change Data Capture Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this employer group has an active wellness or health promotion program integrated with their health plan. Wellness programs may include biometric screenings, health risk assessments, smoking cessation, weight management, and premium incentives.
reference: ACA Wellness Program Regulations, HIPAA Wellness Program Rules

business_glossary_term: Group Plan Offering - Group Plan Offering Id
description: Primary key for the group_plan_offering association

business_glossary_term: Group Plan Offering - Employer Group Id
description: Foreign key linking to the employer group that is electing this health plan offering.

business_glossary_term: Group Plan Offering - Health Plan Id
description: Foreign key linking to the health plan product being offered to the employer group.
The calendar year for which this group plan offering is in effect. Enables year-over-year historical tracking of which plans a group offered in each benefit year.

The date on which this specific group plan offering becomes active — i.e., when the employer group's election of this health plan takes effect. Belongs to the offering, not to the plan or group alone, as different groups activate the same plan on different dates.
reference: Identified in detection phase relationship data

description: The percentage of the employee-only premium that the employer pays for this specific plan offering. An employer may contribute 80% toward the HMO but only 70% toward the PPO — this value is plan-specific and belongs to the offering, not to the employer group record.
reference: Identified in detection phase relationship data; moved from employer_group.employer_contribution_percentage

description: The end date of the open enrollment window during which employees of this employer group may elect this specific health plan. May differ from the plan's general open enrollment dates.
reference: Identified in detection phase as group_enrollment_period_end

description: The start date of the open enrollment window during which employees of this employer group may elect this specific health plan. May differ from the plan's general open enrollment dates.
reference: Identified in detection phase as group_enrollment_period_start

description: The minimum percentage of eligible employees that must enroll in this specific health plan for the group offering to remain active. This threshold is negotiated per plan per group and belongs to the offering, not to either entity alone.
reference: Identified in detection phase relationship data; also present on employer_group as a group-level default — the offering-level value overrides it

description: The current lifecycle status of this group plan offering. Tracks whether the offering is currently active, pending activation, terminated, or suspended. Belongs to the offering record, not to the plan or group.
reference: Standard group benefits administration lifecycle attribute

description: The date on which this group plan offering ends. Enables historical tracking of which plans were offered in prior benefit years. Belongs to the offering, not to the plan or group alone.
reference: Identified in detection phase relationship data

description: Unique identifier for the member enrollment record. Primary key for this transactional enrollment entity.
Unique identifier for the members assigned primary care physician. Required for HMO and POS plans.

Foreign key linking to insurance.employer_group. Business justification: Member enrollment is frequently tied to a specific employer group — group-sponsored coverage is the dominant enrollment channel. The existing group_number STRING column is a denormalized reference to
Unique identifier for the specific health plan product the member is enrolled in. Links to the health plan master record.
Unique identifier for the member (subscriber or dependent) enrolled in the health plan. Links to the member master record.
Unique identifier for the insurance payer or health plan organization providing coverage. Links to the payer master record.
reference: CMS National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES)

description: External subscriber identifier assigned by the payer or health plan. Used for eligibility verification and claim adjudication.
Self-referencing FK on member_enrollment (prior_member_enrollment_id)
Unique identifier for the provider network associated with this enrollment. Determines in-network vs out-of-network benefits.
End date of the benefit period for deductible and out-of-pocket maximum tracking. Resets accumulators for next period.
Start date of the benefit period for deductible and out-of-pocket maximum tracking. Typically calendar year or plan year.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Indicator
description: Indicates whether this enrollment is a COBRA continuation coverage following employment termination.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Qualifying Event Date
description: Date of the qualifying event that triggered COBRA eligibility (e.g., employment termination, divorce). Nullable for non-COBRA enrollments.
Coverage tier indicating the scope of family members covered under this enrollment. Affects premium calculation.
Date when member eligibility was last verified with the payer. Used for real-time eligibility checks.
Status of the most recent eligibility verification attempt. Used to flag potential coverage issues.
Channel or interface through which the member completed the enrollment process.
Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit and data lineage tracking.
Date when the member coverage becomes effective and benefits are available. Used for eligibility determination.
Free-text notes or comments related to this enrollment. Used for documenting special circumstances or exceptions.

Source system or channel through which the enrollment was initiated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: employer_group|aca_exchange|medicare_cms|medicaid_agency|direct_enrollment|broker|navigator|other — promote to reference product]
Name of the source system that originated this enrollment record. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Current lifecycle status of the member enrollment. Used for eligibility verification and claim adjudication.
Date when the member coverage ends and benefits are no longer available. Nullable for active enrollments.
Type of enrollment event that initiated this coverage. Determines eligibility rules and enrollment period constraints.
Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified. Used for audit and change tracking.
Date of the most recent premium payment received for this enrollment. Used for grace period and termination calculations.
State-assigned Medicaid identifier for dual-eligible members. Used for coordination of benefits and claim adjudication.
Effective date of Medicare Part A coverage for dual-eligible members. Used for coordination of benefits.
Effective date of Medicare Part B coverage for dual-eligible members. Used for coordination of benefits.
Date when the primary care physician was assigned to this member. Used for continuity of care tracking.
Monthly premium amount for this member enrollment. May be employer-paid, member-paid, or split.
Frequency at which premium payments are due for this enrollment.
Current status of premium payments for this enrollment. Affects coverage eligibility and claim payment.
Relationship of the member to the primary subscriber. Determines dependent status and coverage eligibility rules.
Monthly premium subsidy or tax credit amount applied to this enrollment. Applicable for ACA marketplace plans.
Type of premium subsidy or cost-sharing reduction applied to this enrollment. APTC = Advanced Premium Tax Credit, CSR = Cost-Sharing Reduction.

Reason for enrollment termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary_disenrollment|employment_termination|loss_of_eligibility|non_payment|death|medicare_eligibility|medicaid_eligibility|plan_discontinuation|other — promote to reference product]

Unique identifier for the subscriber record. Primary key. The subscriber is the primary insured individual (policyholder) who holds the insurance contract with the payer and is responsible for premium payment.
Reference to the employer group sponsoring this coverage, if applicable. Null for individual/non-group plans.
Reference to the specific health plan product under which this subscriber is enrolled. Links to the plan master in the insurance domain.

Payer-assigned unique member identifier for the subscriber. This is the externally-known subscriber number used on insurance cards and in claims processing. Also known as subscriber number or policy number.
Reference to the insurance payer (health plan) that issued this subscriber contract. Links to the payer master in the insurance domain.
Self-referencing FK on subscriber (prior_subscriber_id)
Primary street address line for the subscriber's residence.
Secondary address line (apartment, suite, unit number) for the subscriber's residence.
City of the subscriber's residence.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the subscriber is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage after employment termination. True=eligible, False=not eligible.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) End Date
description: Date when COBRA continuation coverage ends, if applicable. Null if not on COBRA or coverage is ongoing.

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Start Date
description: Date when COBRA continuation coverage began, if applicable. Null if not on COBRA.
Three-letter ISO country code of the subscriber's residence. Typically USA for US-based subscribers.
Current lifecycle status of the subscriber's coverage. Active=currently covered, Inactive=coverage lapsed, Suspended=temporarily paused, Terminated=permanently ended, Pending=awaiting activation.
Type of insurance coverage provided under this subscriber contract. Indicates the primary benefit category.
Timestamp when this subscriber record was first created in the data platform.
Date of birth of the subscriber. Used for eligibility verification and age-based benefit determination.
Indicates whether the subscriber is dually eligible for both Medicare and Medicaid. True=dual eligible, False=not dual eligible. Critical for coordination of benefits and cost-sharing determination.
reference: CMS Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans

description: Date when the subscriber's coverage terminates. Null for open-ended active coverage.
Date when the subscriber's coverage becomes effective and benefits are available.
Email address for subscriber communication and electronic correspondence.
Legal first name of the subscriber as registered with the payer.
Administrative gender of the subscriber. M=Male, F=Female, U=Unknown, O=Other.
Employer or sponsor group number under which the subscriber is enrolled. Used for group billing and benefit determination.
Legal last name (family name) of the subscriber as registered with the payer.
Indicates whether the subscriber is eligible for Medicaid coverage. True=eligible, False=not eligible. Used for coordination of benefits and dual eligibility determination.
State-issued Medicaid identification number, if the subscriber is Medicaid-eligible. Used for coordination of benefits.
Indicates whether the subscriber is eligible for Medicare coverage. True=eligible, False=not eligible. Used for coordination of benefits.
Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) assigned by CMS, if the subscriber is Medicare-eligible. Used for coordination of benefits between Medicare and commercial insurance.
Middle name or initial of the subscriber, if provided.
Network tier or level assigned to the subscriber's plan. Determines cost-sharing and provider access levels.
Primary contact phone number for the subscriber.
ZIP code or postal code of the subscriber's residence.
Monthly or periodic premium amount the subscriber is responsible for paying. Amount in USD.
Frequency at which the subscriber pays premiums.

business_glossary_term: Primary Care Physician (PCP) National Provider Identifier (NPI)
description: National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the subscriber's designated primary care physician, if applicable. Used in managed care plans requiring PCP assignment.

Relationship of the subscriber to the primary insured. For subscriber records, this is typically 'self' since the subscriber IS the insured party. Used when subscriber may be a dependent on another policy.
Name of the source system from which this subscriber record originated (e.g., Epic Resolute, Cerner Revenue Cycle, payer enrollment system).
Unique identifier for this subscriber in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Social Security Number of the subscriber. Stored in masked or encrypted format per HIPAA security requirements. Used for identity verification and coordination of benefits.
State or province of the subscriber's residence. Two-letter state code for US addresses.
Name suffix of the subscriber (Jr, Sr, II, III, etc.), if applicable.
Reason for coverage termination, if applicable. Null for active coverage.
Timestamp when this subscriber record was last updated in the data platform.
Unique identifier for the benefit component within a health plan. Primary key for the benefit master record.

Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Benefits often require specific diagnoses for coverage (e.g., CGM for diabetes, PT for specific conditions). Medical necessity validation and claims auto-adjudication depend on diagnosis-benefit linka
Reference to the parent health plan that contains this benefit component. Links benefit to its plan container.
Self-referencing FK on benefit (parent_benefit_id)

Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Benefits are defined by specific CPT procedures with associated copays, coinsurance, and authorization requirements. Core to claims adjudication engine and member cost-sharing calculation in every pay

Foreign key linking to reference.hcpcs_code. Business justification: Benefits for DME, supplies, and non-physician services are defined by HCPCS codes. Essential for benefit determination, prior authorization rules, and claims payment calculation in healthcare payer op
Method used to determine the allowed amount for benefit adjudication. Defines how the plan calculates the maximum payable amount for a service.

High-level classification of the healthcare service category covered by this benefit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inpatient_hospital|outpatient_surgery|primary_care|specialist|emergency|mental_health|substance_use|preventive|durable_medical_equipment|home_health|hospice|vision|dental|pharmacy|rehabilitation|skilled_nursing — promote to reference product]
Unique business identifier for the benefit component used in plan documentation, member communications, and adjudication rules. Externally visible code.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Plan Benefit Package

description: Detailed narrative description of the benefit coverage, limitations, and member responsibilities. Used in Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) and member materials.
reference: CMS Summary of Benefits and Coverage Template

description: Human-readable name of the benefit component displayed to members and providers. Example: Inpatient Hospital Services, Primary Care Office Visit.
Current lifecycle status of the benefit component. Active benefits are available for adjudication; inactive benefits are historical or discontinued.

Tiering level of the benefit indicating cost-sharing structure. Commonly used for pharmacy benefits (generic, preferred brand, non-preferred brand, specialty) and provider networks (in-network tier 1, tier 2).
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Formulary Tiers

description: Percentage of allowed amount the member pays after deductible is met. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20%). Null if benefit uses copay instead of coinsurance.
reference: CMS Summary of Benefits and Coverage Template

description: Fixed dollar amount the member pays per service or visit as defined by the benefit. Null if benefit uses coinsurance instead of copay.
reference: CMS Summary of Benefits and Coverage Template

description: Actuarial value tier of the benefit indicating the percentage of costs the plan covers on average. Used for ACA marketplace plans.
reference: ACA Metal Tier Actuarial Value Standards

description: Percentage of allowed amount the plan pays for this benefit. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 80.00 for 80% plan payment). Inverse of coinsurance_percentage.
reference: CMS Summary of Benefits and Coverage Template

description: Timestamp when this benefit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Maximum number of days covered per benefit period for services measured in days (e.g., 90 days skilled nursing facility per benefit period). Null if no day limit applies.
reference: CMS Medicare Skilled Nursing Facility Coverage

description: Time period over which the day limit count applies. Example: per_admission for inpatient stays, per_year for annual day limits.
Maximum number of days supply allowed per prescription fill for pharmacy benefits. Common limits are 30, 60, or 90 days. Null if not applicable.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Days Supply Standards

description: Indicates whether the plan deductible must be met before this benefit pays. True if deductible applies, False if benefit is not subject to deductible (e.g., preventive services).
Type of diagnosis coding system used for medical necessity determination and benefit eligibility. ICD-10-CM is current standard in US; ICD-9-CM is legacy.
Maximum dollar amount the plan will pay for this benefit per limit period. Null if no dollar limit applies. Note: ACA prohibits lifetime and annual dollar limits on essential health benefits.
reference: ACA Lifetime and Annual Limit Prohibitions

description: Time period over which the dollar limit amount applies. Used for non-essential benefits where dollar limits are permitted.
Date when this benefit component becomes active and available for coverage. Must align with plan year or mid-year plan changes.
Indicates whether this benefit is classified as an Essential Health Benefit under ACA. EHBs cannot have annual or lifetime dollar limits.
Narrative description of services or conditions explicitly excluded from this benefit. Critical for claims denial and member communication.
Pharmacy formulary tier for drug benefits indicating cost-sharing level. Tier 1 typically generic, Tier 2 preferred brand, Tier 3 non-preferred brand, Tier 4 specialty drugs.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Formulary Requirements

description: Indicates whether this benefit is compatible with Health Savings Account (HSA) requirements. True if benefit meets HSA-qualified high-deductible health plan (HDHP) rules.
Narrative description of limitations, restrictions, or conditions that apply to this benefit beyond the structured limit fields.
Indicates whether this pharmacy benefit can be fulfilled through mail order pharmacy with potentially different cost-sharing. True if mail order option available.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Mail Order Requirements

description: Timestamp when this benefit record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
Indicates whether this benefit applies to in-network providers, out-of-network providers, or both. Critical for cost-sharing calculation and provider network adjudication.
Indicates whether member cost-sharing for this benefit counts toward the annual out-of-pocket maximum. True if it counts, False if excluded (e.g., non-covered services).
Standard two-digit code identifying where the service is delivered (e.g., 11=Office, 21=Inpatient Hospital, 22=Outpatient Hospital, 23=Emergency Room). Used for benefit adjudication rules.
Indicates whether this benefit is classified as preventive care under ACA requirements. Preventive services must be covered at 100% with no cost-sharing when delivered in-network.
Indicates whether prior authorization from the health plan is required before the service is covered. True if PA required, False otherwise.
Type of procedure coding system used to identify services covered by this benefit. CPT for physician services, HCPCS for supplies and equipment, ICD-10-PCS for inpatient procedures, CDT for dental.
reference: AMA CPT, CMS HCPCS, WHO ICD-10-PCS, ADA CDT

description: Indicates whether this benefit has quantity or frequency limits (e.g., maximum number of visits, days supply, units per fill). True if limits apply.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Quantity Limits

description: Indicates whether a referral from Primary Care Physician (PCP) is required to access this benefit. Common in Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) plans.
Standard code identifying the type of healthcare service covered by this benefit. Aligns with X12 837 claim service type codes for electronic transactions.
reference: X12 837 Health Care Claim Service Type Codes

description: Indicates whether step therapy protocol must be followed (trying lower-cost alternatives before higher-cost treatments). Primarily used for pharmacy benefits.
reference: CMS Medicare Part D Step Therapy Requirements

description: Detailed subcategory within the benefit category providing granular service classification. Example: Acute Inpatient, Observation Stay, Ambulatory Surgery.
Date when this benefit component is no longer active. Null for currently active benefits with no planned end date.
Maximum number of visits or services allowed per benefit period (e.g., 20 physical therapy visits per year). Null if no visit limit applies.
Time period over which the visit limit count applies. Example: per_year for annual limits, per_lifetime for lifetime maximums.
reference: ACA Lifetime and Annual Limit Prohibitions

description: Unique identifier for the coverage policy record. Primary key.

Reference to the specific health plan under which this coverage policy applies.

Foreign key linking to reference.ndc_drug. Business justification: Coverage policies govern medical benefit drug coverage (e.g., infused biologics, chemotherapy). Linking to ndc_drug enables drug-specific coverage determination, step therapy policy enforcement, and C
Reference to the insurance payer or health plan that owns this coverage policy.
Reference to the coverage policy that replaces this policy when it is retired or superseded. Nullable if the policy is still active or has not been replaced.

Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Medical policies define coverage criteria by specialty (e.g., orthopedic surgery criteria, cardiology device policies). Essential for claims adjudication, medical necessity determination, and specialt
Age-based limitations or requirements for coverage under this policy (e.g., 'covered for patients 18 years and older', 'pediatric use only').
Indicates whether coverage denials based on this policy can be appealed by the provider or patient. True = appeals allowed; False = no appeals allowed.
Detailed description of the appeals process for coverage denials based on this policy, including timelines, required documentation, and contact information.
Reference to the clinical evidence, guidelines, or literature that supports the coverage determination (e.g., 'NCCN Guidelines', 'FDA approval', 'peer-reviewed clinical trial').
reference: AHRQ Evidence-Based Practice Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Final determination of whether the service, procedure, or technology is covered under this policy. Covered = reimbursable; Non-Covered = not reimbursable; Conditional = covered only if specific criteria met; Investigational/Experimental = not covered due to lack of evidence; Not Medically Necessary = does not meet medical necessity criteria.
reference: CMS Coverage Policy Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Timestamp when the coverage policy record was first created in the system.
Date on which the coverage policy becomes active and enforceable for claims adjudication and prior authorization decisions.
Specific services, procedures, diagnoses, or circumstances that are explicitly excluded from coverage under this policy.
Date on which the coverage policy ceases to be active. Nullable for policies with no defined end date.
Limitations on how often the service or procedure can be performed and reimbursed within a specified time period (e.g., 'once per calendar year', 'maximum 12 visits per year').
Gender-based limitations or requirements for coverage under this policy (e.g., 'female patients only', 'male patients only', 'no gender restrictions').
Date when the coverage policy was last modified or revised.
Clinical criteria defining when the service or procedure is considered medically necessary and therefore eligible for coverage under this policy.
reference: CMS Medical Necessity Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Indicates whether the service or procedure must be performed by an in-network provider to be covered. In-Network Only = only in-network providers eligible; Out-of-Network Allowed = both in-network and out-of-network providers eligible (may have different reimbursement); No Restriction = network status does not affect coverage.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Restrictions on where the service or procedure can be performed to be eligible for coverage (e.g., 'inpatient hospital only', 'outpatient facility or office', 'home health setting').
reference: CMS Place of Service Code Standards

description: Date on which the coverage policy was formally approved by the payer's medical policy committee or equivalent governance body.
Name or identifier of the individual or committee that approved the coverage policy.
High-level categorization of the coverage policy by clinical domain or service type (e.g., 'Radiology', 'Pharmacy', 'Surgical Procedures', 'Durable Medical Equipment').
Detailed narrative description of the coverage policy, including the clinical rationale, scope of coverage, and any special conditions or limitations.
Name or identifier of the individual or department responsible for maintaining and updating this coverage policy.

Current lifecycle status of the coverage policy. Active = in effect and enforceable; Inactive = temporarily suspended; Draft = not yet finalized; Under Review = being evaluated or updated; Retired = no longer in use; Superseded = replaced by a newer policy version.
Human-readable title or name of the coverage policy, describing the service or condition covered.

Classification of the coverage policy. Medical Policy = internal payer medical necessity policy; Coverage Determination = specific coverage decision; LCD = Local Coverage Determination (Medicare contractor-specific); NCD = National Coverage Determination (CMS national policy); Administrative Policy = non-clinical coverage rule; Clinical Guideline = evidence-based clinical criteria.
reference: CMS National Coverage Determination (NCD) and Local Coverage Determination (LCD) Standards

description: Version number of the coverage policy, used to track revisions and updates over time (e.g., '1.0', '2.3').
Detailed clinical and administrative criteria that must be met to obtain prior authorization for the service or procedure covered by this policy.
reference: CMS Prior Authorization Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Indicates whether prior authorization is required before the service or procedure can be performed and reimbursed under this policy. True = prior authorization required; False = no prior authorization required.
reference: CMS Prior Authorization Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Restrictions on which provider specialties are authorized to perform the service or procedure under this policy (e.g., 'board-certified cardiologist only', 'licensed physical therapist').
Limitations on the quantity or dosage of the service, supply, or medication that can be provided and reimbursed under this policy (e.g., 'maximum 30-day supply', 'up to 10 units per month').
Reference to the regulatory or statutory basis for the coverage policy (e.g., 'CMS NCD 210.1', 'State Medicaid Manual Section 4.5', 'ACA Essential Health Benefits').
reference: CMS Regulatory Standards, State Medicaid Standards, ACA Standards

description: Scheduled date for the next policy review or update cycle, ensuring the policy remains aligned with current clinical evidence and regulatory requirements.
reference: NCQA Quality Standards, Internal Policy Management Standards

description: Detailed criteria defining the step therapy requirements, including which treatments must be tried first and the conditions under which step therapy can be bypassed.
reference: CMS Coverage Policy Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Indicates whether step therapy (trial of less expensive or less invasive treatments first) is required before the service or procedure is covered. True = step therapy required; False = no step therapy required.
reference: CMS Coverage Policy Standards, NCQA Standards

description: Timestamp when the coverage policy record was last updated in the system.
Unique identifier for the health plan product. Primary key for the health plan entity.
Reference to the insurance payer organization that underwrites or administers this health plan.
Self-referencing FK on health_plan (predecessor_health_plan_id)
Reference to the provider network that defines in-network and out-of-network providers, facilities, and pharmacies for this plan.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State Insurance Department Network Standards

description: The calendar year for which this plan configuration and benefit structure are effective. Used to track annual plan changes and benefit resets.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Contract Number
description: The unique contract identifier assigned by CMS for Medicare Advantage, Medicare Part D, or Medicaid managed care plans. Used for regulatory reporting and plan identification.
reference: CMS Contract Management Standards, Medicare Advantage and Part D Regulations

description: The order in which this plan pays when a member has multiple insurance coverages. Primary pays first, Secondary pays after primary, Tertiary pays after secondary. Used for COB adjudication logic.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Coordination of Benefits, ANSI X12 837 COB Segment

description: The percentage of allowed charges that the member is responsible for paying after the deductible is met. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20% member responsibility).
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, ACA Actuarial Value Calculation

description: The timestamp when this health plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: HIPAA Audit Trail Requirements, SOC 2 Data Integrity Controls

description: The date on which this health plan configuration becomes active and available for member enrollment and coverage. Typically aligns with benefit year start or employer plan year start.
reference: CMS Plan Year Standards, ERISA Plan Document Requirements

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for an emergency department visit. May be waived if admitted. Null if the plan uses coinsurance instead of copay.
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, EMTALA (Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act) Compliance

description: The annual deductible amount in USD that a family unit must collectively pay out-of-pocket before the plan begins cost-sharing for covered services.
reference: ACA Essential Health Benefits Standards, CMS Cost-Sharing Standards

description: The annual maximum amount in USD that a family unit will collectively pay out-of-pocket for covered services. After reaching this limit, the plan pays 100% of covered costs for the remainder of the benefit year.
reference: ACA Section 1302, CMS Out-of-Pocket Maximum Standards

description: Indicates whether this plan allows members to participate in a Flexible Spending Account for pre-tax healthcare or dependent care expenses.
reference: IRS Section 125 Cafeteria Plans, DOL FSA Guidance

description: The financial arrangement model for the plan. Fully Insured means the payer assumes all risk; Self-Funded means the employer assumes risk and payer provides administrative services only (ASO); Level Funded is a hybrid with predictable monthly costs; Partially Self-Funded shares risk between employer and payer.
reference: ERISA (Employee Retirement Income Security Act) Plan Classification, DOL (Department of Labor) Guidance

description: The group identifier assigned to employer-sponsored or association-based plans, used for member eligibility verification and claims adjudication.
Indicates whether this plan is paired with an employer-funded Health Reimbursement Arrangement that reimburses employees for qualified medical expenses.
reference: IRS Notice 2002-45 Health Reimbursement Arrangements, ACA Integration Rules

description: Indicates whether this plan is HSA-qualified, meaning it meets IRS requirements for high-deductible health plans that allow members to contribute to a tax-advantaged Health Savings Account.
reference: IRS Publication 969 Health Savings Accounts, ACA HDHP Standards

description: The annual deductible amount in USD that an individual member must pay out-of-pocket before the plan begins cost-sharing for covered services.
reference: ACA Essential Health Benefits Standards, CMS Cost-Sharing Standards

description: The annual maximum amount in USD that an individual member will pay out-of-pocket for covered services. After reaching this limit, the plan pays 100% of covered costs for the remainder of the benefit year.
reference: ACA Section 1302, CMS Out-of-Pocket Maximum Standards

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays per inpatient hospital admission or per day. Null if the plan uses coinsurance instead of copay.
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, DRG (Diagnosis-Related Group) Payment Standards

description: The two-letter state code where this health plan is issued and regulated. Used for multi-state payers to track jurisdiction-specific plan configurations.
reference: USPS State Abbreviation Standards, CMS State-Based Marketplace Standards

description: The actuarial value tier for ACA (Affordable Care Act) marketplace plans indicating the percentage of covered healthcare costs the plan pays on average. Bronze (60%), Silver (70%), Gold (80%), Platinum (90%), Catastrophic (minimal coverage for under-30 or hardship exemption). Not Applicable for non-ACA plans.
reference: ACA Section 1302, CMS Actuarial Value Standards

description: The last date of the annual open enrollment period during which eligible individuals can enroll in or change their health plan selection without a qualifying life event.
reference: ACA Open Enrollment Standards, CMS Marketplace Enrollment Periods

description: The first date of the annual open enrollment period during which eligible individuals can enroll in or change their health plan selection without a qualifying life event.
reference: ACA Open Enrollment Standards, CMS Marketplace Enrollment Periods

description: Indicates whether this plan provides any coverage for out-of-network providers and facilities. True for PPO and POS plans, false for HMO and EPO plans (except emergency services).
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State Insurance Department Network Requirements

description: The annual deductible amount in USD for out-of-network services. Typically higher than in-network deductible. Null if plan does not cover out-of-network services.
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, NAIC Uniform Health Plan Benefit Standards

description: The annual maximum out-of-pocket amount in USD for out-of-network services. Typically higher than in-network OOP max. Null if plan does not cover out-of-network services.
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, NAIC Uniform Health Plan Benefit Standards

description: The web URL where the official plan document, Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC), or Evidence of Coverage (EOC) can be accessed by members and providers.
reference: ACA Summary of Benefits and Coverage Requirements, ERISA Plan Document Disclosure

description: The externally-recognized unique identifier for this plan. May be HIOS (Health Insurance Oversight System) Plan ID for ACA marketplace plans, CMS contract number for Medicare Advantage or Part D plans, or state-assigned Medicaid plan ID.
reference: CMS HIOS Plan ID Standard, CMS Contract Number Standard

description: The marketing or official name of the health plan product as presented to members and providers.
reference: CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services) Plan Naming Standards

description: The current lifecycle status of the health plan. Active means the plan is currently offered and accepting enrollments; Suspended means temporarily unavailable; Terminated means no longer offered; Pending Approval means awaiting regulatory approval; Grandfathered means exempt from certain ACA requirements; Closed to New Enrollment means existing members can renew but new members cannot enroll.
reference: CMS Plan Status Standards, State Insurance Department Licensing Requirements

description: The structural type of the health plan indicating network and coverage model. HMO (Health Maintenance Organization) requires PCP and referrals; PPO (Preferred Provider Organization) allows out-of-network care; EPO (Exclusive Provider Organization) requires in-network only; POS (Point of Service) hybrid model; HDHP (High Deductible Health Plan) paired with HSA; Medicare Advantage (MA) for Medicare beneficiaries; Medicare Part D for prescription drug coverage; Medicaid Managed Care for state Medicaid programs; CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program); Self-Funded for employer-administered plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HMO|PPO|EPO|POS|HDHP|Medicare Advantage|Medicare Part D|Medicaid Managed Care|CHIP|Self-Funded — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Plan Type Taxonomy, NAIC (National Association of Insurance Commissioners) Plan Classification

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for a Tier 1 (typically generic) prescription drug. Null if the plan uses coinsurance for pharmacy benefits.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Standards, NCPDP Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for a Tier 2 (typically preferred brand) prescription drug. Null if the plan uses coinsurance for pharmacy benefits.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Standards, NCPDP Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for a Tier 3 (typically non-preferred brand) prescription drug. Null if the plan uses coinsurance for pharmacy benefits.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Standards, NCPDP Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for a Tier 4 (typically specialty) prescription drug. Null if the plan uses coinsurance for pharmacy benefits.
reference: CMS Part D Formulary Standards, NCPDP Pharmacy Benefit Standards

description: Indicates whether preventive care services (annual physicals, immunizations, screenings) are covered at 100% with no member cost-sharing, as required by ACA for non-grandfathered plans.
reference: ACA Section 2713 Preventive Services, USPSTF (U.S. Preventive Services Task Force) Recommendations

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for a primary care physician office visit. Null if the plan uses coinsurance instead of copay.
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, NAIC Uniform Health Plan Benefit Standards

description: Indicates whether this plan requires prior authorization for certain services, procedures, or medications. When true, members or providers must obtain approval before services are rendered to ensure coverage.
reference: CMS Prior Authorization Standards, NCQA (National Committee for Quality Assurance) Utilization Management Standards

description: Indicates whether members are required to select and maintain a designated primary care physician. Typically true for HMO and POS plans, false for PPO and EPO plans.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards, State Insurance Department Plan Requirements

description: Indicates whether members must obtain a referral from their PCP before seeing a specialist. Typically true for HMO plans, false for PPO, EPO, and POS plans.
reference: CMS Managed Care Standards, State Insurance Department Plan Requirements

description: A textual description of the geographic service area where this plan is available, typically defined by counties, ZIP codes, or metropolitan areas.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy and Service Area Standards

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for a specialist physician office visit. Null if the plan uses coinsurance instead of copay.
reference: CMS Cost-Sharing Standards, NAIC Uniform Health Plan Benefit Standards

description: The unique filing or approval number assigned by the state insurance department for this health plan product. Required for regulatory compliance and rate filings.
reference: State Insurance Department Filing Requirements, NAIC Regulatory Standards

description: The date on which this health plan configuration is terminated and no longer available for coverage. Null for active plans with no planned termination.
reference: CMS Plan Termination Standards, State Insurance Department Notification Requirements

description: The timestamp when this health plan record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
reference: HIPAA Audit Trail Requirements, SOC 2 Data Integrity Controls

description: The fixed dollar amount in USD that a member pays for an urgent care facility visit. Null if the plan uses coinsurance instead of copay.
Unique identifier for the insurance payer, health plan sponsor, or government program. Primary key for the payer master record within the insurance domain.

Reference to the payer record that replaces this payer in the event of merger, acquisition, or consolidation. Used to redirect claims and maintain continuity. Null if no successor exists.

Indicates whether the payer accepts assignment of benefits, meaning the payer pays the provider directly rather than reimbursing the patient. True for most contracted payers; false for some out-of-network or self-pay arrangements.

Indicates whether this payer record is currently active and should be used for new claims and transactions. True if active; false if payer is inactive, merged, or no longer accepting claims. Inactive payers are retained for historical claim reference.
Number of days from claim denial date within which appeals must be submitted to this payer. Common values are 30, 60, 90, 180 days. Null if payer does not specify appeal deadlines.

Indicates whether the contract with this payer automatically renews at the end of the contract term. True if contract auto-renews unless either party provides notice; false if contract requires explicit renewal action.
Name of the third-party claim scrubbing or editing vendor used to validate claims before submission to this payer. Null if no scrubbing vendor is used or if internal scrubbing is performed.
Dedicated phone number for claim status inquiries and claim-related questions. May differ from general customer service phone.
URL, EDI address, or mailing address for submitting claims to this payer. Format varies by submission method.
Identifier for the clearinghouse or intermediary used to submit claims to this payer. Null if claims are submitted directly to the payer.

Indicates whether this payer requires coordination of benefits information when the patient has multiple insurance coverages. True if COB is required; false if payer does not coordinate benefits or is always primary.
reference: NAIC Model Regulation for Coordination of Benefits

description: Date and time when this payer record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Primary phone number for the payer's provider customer service line. Used for claim status inquiries, eligibility verification, and general provider support.
Payer identifier used in EDI transactions, typically the ISA06 qualifier value in X12 837 claim submissions and 835 remittance advice. May differ from NPI or external payer ID.

Indicates whether payments from this payer are received via electronic funds transfer (EFT) directly to the organization's bank account. True if EFT is enabled; false if payments are received by check or other method.
reference: NACHA Operating Rules for ACH Payments

description: Primary method for verifying patient eligibility and benefits with this payer. EDI 270/271 indicates HIPAA-compliant electronic eligibility inquiry and response; portal indicates web-based lookup; phone indicates manual call to payer; real-time API indicates direct system integration.
Payer's own identifier for their organization, often used in their systems and communications. May be a CPID (Carrier Payer ID), plan code, or other payer-assigned identifier.
Date on which this payer record was marked inactive. Null if payer is currently active. Used for audit trail and historical analysis.

Explanation for why this payer record was inactivated. Common reasons include contract termination, payer merger or acquisition, payer out of business, or duplicate record consolidation. Null if payer is active.
Free-text field for internal notes about this payer, including special billing instructions, known issues, contact preferences, or other operational guidance for revenue cycle staff.

High-level categorization of the payer organization. Government includes federal and state programs; commercial includes private insurance carriers; managed care includes HMOs, PPOs, and ACOs; TPA (Third Party Administrator) processes claims on behalf of self-insured employers; other includes specialty programs.
Full legal name of the insurance payer, health plan sponsor, or government program as registered with regulatory authorities. Used for contracts, claims submission, and legal correspondence.

Internal classification of payer by volume, strategic importance, or contract terms. Tier 1 represents highest volume or most favorable contract terms; Tier 4 represents lowest volume or least favorable terms. Used for prioritization in revenue cycle operations and contract negotiations.

Classification of the payer by program type. Determines billing rules, claim formats, and regulatory requirements. Commercial includes private insurance carriers and employer-sponsored plans; Medicare includes traditional Medicare and Medicare Advantage; Medicaid includes state Medicaid programs and managed Medicaid; CHIP is Children's Health Insurance Program; TRICARE is military health system; workers compensation covers occupational injuries; self-pay represents uninsured patients. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|medicare|medicaid|chip|tricare|workers_compensation|self_pay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Number of days within which the payer is contractually obligated to remit payment after claim adjudication. Common values are 14, 30, 45 days. Used for accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting.
Web address for the payer's provider portal used for eligibility verification, claim status inquiry, prior authorization submission, and remittance download.

Indicates whether this payer generally requires prior authorization for certain services, procedures, or medications. True if prior authorization is required for at least some service categories; false if payer does not require prior authorization. Specific authorization rules are maintained in benefit plan configurations.
reference: Payer Medical Policy and Authorization Requirements

description: Email address for the payer's provider relations or network management team. Used for contract inquiries, credentialing issues, and provider communications.
First line of the payer's remittance mailing address where paper checks and EOBs are sent. Includes street number and street name.
Second line of the payer's remittance mailing address. Typically contains suite, floor, department, or other secondary address information. Null if not applicable.
City name for the payer's remittance mailing address.

Method by which the payer delivers remittance advice. EDI 835 indicates electronic remittance advice via X12 835 transaction; ERA (Electronic Remittance Advice) is synonym for EDI 835; paper EOB (Explanation of Benefits) indicates physical mail; portal indicates download from payer website.
ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the payer's remittance mailing address. Format: XXXXX or XXXXX-XXXX.
Two-letter state or territory abbreviation for the payer's remittance mailing address. Follows USPS state abbreviation standards.
Abbreviated or commonly used name for the payer for display purposes in user interfaces and reports.

Name of the operational system from which this payer record originated. Examples include Epic Resolute, Cerner Revenue Cycle, MEDITECH Financial, or manual entry. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.

Primary method used to submit claims to this payer. EDI indicates electronic X12 837 transactions; portal indicates web-based submission through payer portal; direct indicates direct electronic connection; clearinghouse indicates submission through third-party clearinghouse; paper indicates physical mail submission.
Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax Identification Number for the payer organization. Used for tax reporting, contract execution, and financial reconciliation. Format: XX-XXXXXXX.

Number of days from date of service within which claims must be submitted to this payer to be considered timely filed. Claims submitted after this period may be denied. Common values are 90, 180, 365 days. Null if payer does not enforce timely filing limits.
Date and time when this payer record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute value changes. Used for change tracking and synchronization.
Unique identifier for the prior authorization rule record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to reference.drg. Business justification: Inpatient PA rules are DRG-based (e.g., PA required for DRG 470 major joint replacement). Payer UM departments define inpatient admission PA requirements by DRG for length-of-stay authorization and in

Foreign key linking to reference.hcpcs_code. Business justification: PA rules govern HCPCS codes for DME, supplies, and specialty drugs. Critical for authorization workflow automation, step therapy enforcement, and utilization management in payer operations.
Reference to the specific health plan product under which this prior authorization rule applies.

Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: PA rules are frequently diagnosis-specific (e.g., PA for MRI only with ICD-10 M54.5 low back pain). Payer PA systems require ICD FK for clinical criteria matching, medical necessity determination, and

Reference to the insurance payer organization that enforces this prior authorization requirement.

Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Prior authorization rules are specialty-specific (e.g., cardiology procedures, oncology treatments, orthopedic surgery). Critical for PA determination engines and clinical criteria application. Creati

Maximum patient age in years for which this prior authorization rule applies. Null if no age restriction.
Minimum patient age in years for which this prior authorization rule applies. Null if no age restriction.
Description of the process for appealing a prior authorization denial under this rule, including timelines and contact information.
Description of the criteria that must be met for automated approval of the prior authorization request.
Indicates whether this prior authorization rule supports automated approval based on predefined criteria without manual review.
Reference to the clinical guideline, medical policy, or evidence-based criteria document that defines the medical necessity standards for this prior authorization rule.
Payer fax number for prior authorization document submission related to this rule.
Payer contact phone number for prior authorization inquiries and submissions related to this rule.
Date and time when this prior authorization rule record was first created in the system.
List or description of clinical documentation that must be submitted with the prior authorization request (e.g., chart notes, lab results, imaging reports).
Date on which this prior authorization rule becomes active and enforceable by the payer.
Description of circumstances under which exceptions to the prior authorization requirement may be granted (e.g., emergency situations, continuity of care).
Maximum number of times the service can be performed within the frequency period without prior authorization. Null if no frequency limit applies.
Time period in days over which the frequency limit is measured. Null if no period applies.
Gender-specific applicability of the prior authorization rule. Values: male, female, all (no restriction), or other.
Payer-specific medical policy document number or identifier that governs this prior authorization rule.
Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or special instructions related to this prior authorization rule.

Classification of the prior authorization requirement level: required (mandatory PA), not required, clinical review (medical necessity review), peer-to-peer (physician discussion), notification only, or retrospective review.
Two-digit CMS Place of Service code identifying the location where the service is rendered (e.g., 11=office, 21=inpatient hospital, 22=outpatient hospital).
Web address of the payer portal for electronic prior authorization submission, if applicable.
