Category determines clinical privileges, committee eligibility, and meeting attendance requirements. A clinician may hold different medical staff categories in different departments (e.g., Active in Cardiology, Courtesy in ICU).

Indicates whether this is the clinician's primary organizational unit affiliation. Each clinician should have exactly one primary affiliation at any time. Used for reporting hierarchy, default scheduling location, and primary cost center assignment.
System of record for this affiliation record (Epic EHR provider master, Workday HCM, or Symplr credentialing system)
System timestamp when this affiliation record was last updated in the data platform
Unique surrogate identifier for each clinician record in the healthcare enterprise master. Primary key for the clinician data product within the provider domain.

Identifier of the primary facility or practice location where the clinician primarily delivers care. Used for scheduling, network adequacy reporting, and provider directory. Links to the facility master in the provider domain.
reference: Epic EHR Cadence; CMS Provider Enrollment; HL7 FHIR R4 PractitionerRole

description: Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Clinician's primary specialty should reference specialty table. Multiple FKs to same target (primary and secondary) require distinct labels. Removes primary_specialty STRING attribute.

Foreign key linking to reference.npi_registry. Business justification: Clinicians must validate against national NPI registry for CMS enrollment, payer credentialing, and primary source verification. Healthcare operations perform monthly NPI validation checks, deactivati

Expiration date of the clinician's primary ABMS board certification. Nullable for clinicians with lifetime certification or non-physician providers. Used to trigger recertification reminders in Symplr.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Indicates whether the clinician holds active board certification from an ABMS-recognized specialty board in their primary specialty. True = currently board certified; False = not board certified or certification lapsed. Used in credentialing, network adequacy, and quality reporting.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); The Joint Commission (TJC); NCQA

business_glossary_term: Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare (CAQH) Provider ID
description: Unique 8-digit identifier assigned to the clinician in the CAQH ProView universal credentialing database. Used to streamline payer credentialing and enrollment by providing a single repository of provider credentials. Sourced from Symplr and CAQH ProView.
reference: CAQH ProView; NCQA Credentialing Standards; CMS Provider Enrollment

description: Broad classification of the clinician's professional category. Drives role-based access, workflow routing, and analytics segmentation. Physician includes MD/DO; advanced_practice includes NP/PA; nursing includes RN/LPN; allied_health includes PT/OT/RT; pharmacy includes PharmD/RPh; behavioral_health includes LCSW/psychologist.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 Practitioner; CMS Provider Taxonomy; NPPES

description: Timestamp when the clinician record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Represents the record audit creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and HIPAA audit log requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312; ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; SOC 2

description: Date on which the clinician's current credentialing approval expires and re-credentialing must be completed. Typically a 2-year cycle per Joint Commission standards. Used to trigger re-credentialing workflows in Symplr.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Current status of the clinician's credentialing and privileging review within the organization. Credentialed indicates approved to practice; pending indicates initial or re-credentialing in progress; expired indicates lapsed credentials requiring renewal. Sourced from Symplr credentialing system.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards; URAC

description: Clinician's date of birth. Used for identity verification during credentialing, primary source verification, and DEA registration. PHI/PII — access restricted per HIPAA.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HL7 FHIR R4 Practitioner; DEA Registration

description: DEA registration number authorizing the clinician to prescribe controlled substances. Format is two letters followed by seven digits. Required for prescribing Schedule II–V medications. Sourced from Symplr credentialing and Epic Willow pharmacy module.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; Controlled Substances Act

description: Current employment lifecycle status of the clinician within the organization. Active indicates currently practicing; on_leave includes FMLA and medical leave; suspended indicates temporary practice restriction; terminated indicates separation. Sourced from Workday HCM.
reference: Workday HCM; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.05; OIG Exclusion List

description: Classification of the clinician's employment relationship with the healthcare organization. Drives payroll processing, malpractice coverage determination, and credentialing workflows. Sourced from Workday HCM Core HR.
reference: Workday HCM; IRS Worker Classification Guidelines; CMS Provider Enrollment

description: Date on which the clinician completed their fellowship training program. Nullable for clinicians without fellowship training. Used for subspecialty credentialing and privileging decisions. Sourced from Symplr.
reference: ACGME; ABMS; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Name of the fellowship training program completed by the clinician (e.g., Interventional Cardiology Fellowship at Cleveland Clinic). Part of the embedded postgraduate training history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Nullable for clinicians without fellowship training. Sourced from Symplr.
reference: ACGME; ABMS; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Legal first (given) name of the clinician as recorded in the provider master. Used for identity verification, credentialing, and provider directory display. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master and Symplr.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HL7 FHIR R4 HumanName

description: Clinician's self-reported gender identity. Used for workforce diversity reporting, EEOC compliance, and provider directory display where applicable. Restricted per HIPAA and applicable employment privacy regulations.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; EEOC; HL7 FHIR R4 Practitioner

description: Date on which the clinician was officially hired or onboarded by the healthcare organization. Used for tenure calculations, benefits eligibility, and workforce analytics. Sourced from Workday HCM Core HR.
Date on which the clinician completed their internship or PGY-1 training. Nullable where internship is embedded within residency program. Sourced from Symplr primary source verification.

Name of the internship or postgraduate year 1 (PGY-1) program completed by the clinician. Part of the embedded postgraduate training history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Nullable for clinicians where internship is embedded within residency. Sourced from Symplr.

Legal last (family) name of the clinician as recorded in the provider master. Used for identity verification, credentialing, provider directory, and billing. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master and Symplr.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HL7 FHIR R4 HumanName

description: Expiration date of the clinician's primary state medical or professional license. Used by credentialing teams to trigger renewal workflows and ensure continuous practice authorization. Sourced from Symplr credentialing.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; State Departments of Health

description: Two-letter US state abbreviation for the jurisdiction that issued the primary state medical or professional license. Used for practice authorization validation and regulatory reporting.
reference: USPS State Abbreviations; State Departments of Health

description: Expiration date of the clinician's professional liability (malpractice) insurance policy. Used to ensure continuous coverage and trigger renewal alerts in Symplr credentialing workflows.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Policy number of the clinician's professional liability (malpractice) insurance coverage. Required for credentialing and hospital privileging. Sourced from Symplr credentialing. Confidential business data.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards; State Departments of Health

description: Indicates whether the clinician is actively enrolled as a Medicaid provider in their primary state. True = enrolled and active; False = not enrolled. Critical for revenue cycle management and claims submission for Medicaid patients.
reference: CMS Medicaid Provider Enrollment; State Departments of Health; 42 CFR Part 455

description: Highest professional degree awarded to the clinician upon completion of medical or professional school (e.g., MD, DO, MBBS, PharmD, DNP). Used in credentialing, provider directory, and billing. Sourced from Symplr primary source verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|MBBS|PharmD|PhD|DNP|MSN|PA|NP|DDS|DPM — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: LCME; AOA (American Osteopathic Association); ACPE; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Date on which the clinician graduated from medical or professional school and received their degree. Used for credentialing, primary source verification, and training history completeness. Sourced from Symplr.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Name of the institution from which the clinician received their medical or professional degree (e.g., Johns Hopkins School of Medicine). Part of the embedded education history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Sourced from Symplr credentialing primary source verification.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards; LCME (Liaison Committee on Medical Education)

description: Indicates whether the clinician is actively enrolled as a Medicare provider with CMS. True = enrolled and active; False = not enrolled or opted out. Critical for revenue cycle management and claims submission. Sourced from CMS PECOS (Provider Enrollment, Chain, and Ownership System).
reference: CMS PECOS; 42 CFR Part 424; CMS Provider Enrollment

description: Legal middle name or initial of the clinician. Used for identity disambiguation when first and last names are not unique. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HL7 FHIR R4 HumanName

description: Generational or professional suffix appended to the clinician's name (e.g., Jr., Sr., II, III). Distinct from professional credentials/degrees which are stored separately.

Most recent date on which the clinician was screened against the OIG List of Excluded Individuals and Entities (LEIE). CMS requires monthly screening for all providers billing federal healthcare programs.
reference: OIG LEIE; CMS Provider Enrollment; 42 CFR Part 1001

business_glossary_term: Office of Inspector General (OIG) Exclusion Check Flag
description: Indicates whether the clinician has been screened against the OIG List of Excluded Individuals and Entities (LEIE). Required monthly per CMS guidance to prevent billing for services rendered by excluded providers. True = checked and clear; False = not yet checked or excluded.
reference: OIG LEIE; CMS Provider Enrollment; 42 CFR Part 1001

description: Aggregate enrollment status of the clinician with payers for billing purposes. Enrolled indicates the clinician is credentialed and enrolled with at least one active payer; pending indicates enrollment applications in progress. Detailed per-payer enrollment is tracked in the provider_payer_enrollment product. Sourced from Symplr provider enrollment module.
reference: CMS Provider Enrollment; Symplr Provider Enrollment; CAQH ProView

description: Indicates whether the clinician's credentials (education, training, licensure) have been verified directly with the issuing source (primary source verification) as required by The Joint Commission and NCQA credentialing standards. True = verified; False = not yet verified or verification pending.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards; URAC

description: Highest or primary professional credential/degree designation displayed after the clinician's name (e.g., MD, DO, NP, PA-C, RN, PharmD, PhD). Used in provider directories, clinical documentation headers, and credentialing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|NP|PA-C|RN|PharmD|PhD|DDS|DPM|OD|CRNA|CNM|LCSW|PT|OT — promote to reference product]
reference: AMA CPT; HL7 FHIR R4 Practitioner.qualification; The Joint Commission (TJC)

description: Indicates whether the clinician's residency program was accredited by the Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education (ACGME) at the time of completion. True = ACGME accredited; False = not accredited or international program. Required for privileging decisions.

Date on which the clinician completed their postgraduate residency training program. Used for credentialing and training history verification. Nullable for clinicians who did not complete a residency (e.g., some allied health professionals). Sourced from Symplr.
reference: ACGME; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Name of the ACGME-accredited residency program completed by the clinician (e.g., Internal Medicine Residency at Massachusetts General Hospital). Part of the embedded postgraduate training history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Sourced from Symplr.
reference: ACGME (Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education); The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Primary state medical or professional license number issued by the applicable state licensing board. Represents the clinician's authorization to practice in their primary state of licensure. Additional multi-state licenses are tracked in the clinician_license product.
reference: State Departments of Health — Facility Licensing; Federation of State Medical Boards (FSMB)

description: Date on which the clinician's employment or engagement with the organization ended. Nullable for active clinicians. Used to deactivate system access, revoke privileges, and trigger payer disenrollment workflows. Sourced from Workday HCM.
reference: Workday HCM; The Joint Commission (TJC); CMS Provider Enrollment

description: Timestamp when the clinician record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance. Updated on every write operation.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312; ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; SOC 2

description: Clinician's primary organizational work email address. Used for secure communications, system access provisioning, credentialing notifications, and provider directory. Sourced from Workday HCM and Epic provider master.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001

description: Clinician's primary work phone number including direct line or pager number. Used for clinical communications, on-call scheduling, and credentialing contact. Sourced from Epic provider master and Workday HCM.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HL7 FHIR R4 ContactPoint

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each credential record in the provider credentialing master. Primary key for the credential data product.

Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Procedure-specific credentials (surgical certifications, interventional licenses) authorize performance of specific CPT codes. Healthcare operations use this for privilege delineation, peer review cas
Reference to the individual clinician or healthcare professional who holds this credential. Links to the provider master record.

Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: DEA credentials authorize controlled substance prescribing by schedule (II-V). Material_master tracks controlled substances with dea_schedule. Link enables regulatory compliance verification that clin

Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Credentials are specialty-specific (e.g., board certifications, specialty licenses). credential.specialty_name should be normalized to specialty table. FK will be populated at credential creation.

Foreign key linking to compliance.training. Business justification: Credentials like DEA X-waiver require documented training completion. Credentialing systems verify training prerequisites before credential issuance/renewal per regulatory requirements (e.g., 8-hour b

The date on which the most recent disciplinary action or adverse action was taken by the issuing authority against this credential. Null if no board action exists. Used in conjunction with board_action_flag for credentialing compliance reporting.
reference: NPDB Regulations 45 CFR Part 60; State Medical Practice Acts; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03

description: Indicates whether a disciplinary action, sanction, reprimand, or adverse action has been taken by the issuing authority against this credential. True = board action exists; False = no board action. Triggers mandatory review in credentialing and privileging workflows.
reference: NPDB Regulations 45 CFR Part 60; State Medical Practice Acts; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; OIG LEIE

business_glossary_term: Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare (CAQH) ProView Submitted Flag
description: Indicates whether this credential has been submitted to the CAQH ProView universal credentialing database. CAQH ProView is the industry-standard repository used by most commercial payers for provider credentialing and enrollment verification.
reference: CAQH ProView Standards; NCQA Credentialing Standards; CMS Provider Enrollment 42 CFR Part 424

description: Full name of the specialty board that issued the board certification (e.g., 'American Board of Internal Medicine', 'American Board of Surgery', 'American Osteopathic Board of Internal Medicine'). Applicable only to board certification credential types.
reference: ABMS Board Certification Standards; AOA Specialty Board Standards; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: The organization that accredited the CME activity (e.g., 'AMA PRA', 'ACCME', 'ANCC', 'ACPE'). Determines whether the CME credits are recognized for state licensure renewal and board recertification purposes.
reference: ACCME Standards; AMA PRA Category 1 Credit Standards; ANCC Accreditation Standards

description: The official title of the CME (Continuing Medical Education) activity as designated by the accrediting organization. Applicable only to CME activity credential types. Used for CME compliance tracking and licensure renewal documentation.
reference: ACCME Standards for Commercial Support; AMA PRA Category 1 Credit Standards

description: The format or delivery modality of the CME activity as classified by the accrediting organization (e.g., live conference, enduring material, performance improvement activity). Applicable only to CME activity credential types.
reference: ACCME Standards for Commercial Support; AMA PRA Category 1 Credit Standards

description: The AMA PRA CME category designation for this activity. Category 1 credits are formally accredited and required for most state licensure renewals; Category 2 credits are self-designated and have more limited acceptance.
reference: AMA PRA Category 1 Credit Standards; State Medical Board CME Requirements

description: The number of CME credit hours awarded upon completion of this CME activity. Used to track cumulative CME hours for state licensure renewal requirements and board recertification MOC compliance.
reference: ACCME Standards; AMA PRA Category 1 Credit Standards; State Medical Board CME Requirements

description: The timestamp when this credential record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements under HIPAA, HITRUST, and ISO 27001 for credentialing data governance.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001; SOC 2

description: The externally-issued identifier for this credential as assigned by the issuing authority (e.g., state license number, DEA registration number, board certification number, CME activity ID). This is the official reference number used in regulatory and payer verification workflows.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; State Medical Practice Acts; ABMS Board Certification Standards; ACCME Standards for Commercial Support

description: Current lifecycle state of the credential as reported by the issuing authority or verified through primary source verification. Active credentials authorize clinical practice; expired or revoked credentials trigger immediate workflow alerts in credentialing and pharmacy order validation systems.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: High-level classification of the credential record. Distinguishes among state medical/nursing licenses, DEA controlled substance registrations, specialty board certifications, and CME (Continuing Medical Education) activity completions. Drives downstream workflow routing in credentialing and privileging systems such as Symplr.
reference: Symplr Credentialing; NCQA Credentialing Standards; URAC Credentialing Standards

description: The number of days remaining until this credential expires, calculated from the current date to the expiration_date. Used for proactive renewal alerting and credentialing dashboard KPIs. Negative values indicate the credential is already expired.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03

business_glossary_term: DEA (Drug Enforcement Administration) Business Activity Type
description: The DEA-designated business activity type associated with the DEA registration. Defines the authorized scope of controlled substance handling (e.g., practitioner prescribing vs. hospital dispensing vs. research). Applicable only to DEA registration credential types.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; DEA Registration Application Form 224

description: Comma-separated list of controlled substance schedule authorizations granted under this DEA registration (e.g., 'II,III,IV,V'). Applicable only to DEA registration credential types. Used by pharmacy order validation systems (Epic Willow, Cerner PharmNet) to authorize controlled substance prescribing.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1308; Controlled Substances Act; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: The date from which this credential record is considered effective and authorizes clinical practice or activity. For state licenses and DEA registrations, this aligns with the issue_date. For board certifications, this is the initial certification effective date. Supports temporal validity queries.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; FHIR Practitioner Resource

description: The date until which this credential record is considered effective. Aligns with expiration_date for most credential types. Null for open-ended or lifetime credentials. Supports SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) and temporal validity queries in the lakehouse.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; FHIR Practitioner Resource

description: The date on which the credential expires and must be renewed or recertified to remain valid. Null for credentials with no expiration (e.g., certain lifetime board certifications). Drives automated renewal alerts in Symplr and Epic credentialing workflows.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; DEA 21 CFR Part 1301

description: The date on which the credential was originally issued or first conferred by the issuing authority. For board certifications, this is the initial certification date. For CME activities, this is the activity completion date.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; ABMS Board Certification Standards; ACCME Standards

description: Full name of the organization or governmental body that issued or confers this credential (e.g., 'Texas Medical Board', 'U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration', 'American Board of Internal Medicine', 'American Medical Association — PRA'). Used for primary source verification routing.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; DEA 21 CFR Part 1301

description: Two-letter US state or territory abbreviation where the credential was issued. Applicable to state medical licenses and DEA state-specific registrations. Null for federal DEA registrations, board certifications, and CME activities that are not state-specific.
reference: State Medical Practice Acts; DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; FSMB Interstate Medical Licensure Compact

description: Current status of the provider's compliance with the Maintenance of Certification (MOC) program requirements for this board certification. MOC compliance is required by many hospitals for privileging and by payers for network participation. Applicable only to board certification credential types.
reference: ABMS MOC Program Requirements; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Free-text field for credentialing staff to document relevant context, exceptions, or special circumstances associated with this credential record (e.g., pending renewal documentation, board action history, conditional license restrictions). Not used for structured data.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03

business_glossary_term: National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) Queried Flag
description: Indicates whether the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) was queried as part of the credentialing verification process for this provider. NPDB queries are required by CMS and The Joint Commission for initial credentialing and re-credentialing.
reference: NPDB Regulations 45 CFR Part 60; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03

business_glossary_term: National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) Query Date
description: The date on which the NPDB was queried for this provider's credentialing record. Required documentation for CMS and Joint Commission credentialing compliance audits.
reference: NPDB Regulations 45 CFR Part 60; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482

business_glossary_term: Office of Inspector General (OIG) Exclusion Check Date
description: The most recent date on which the OIG LEIE (List of Excluded Individuals/Entities) was checked for this provider. CMS recommends monthly screening; documents compliance for RAC audits and OIG investigations.
reference: OIG LEIE; CMS Conditions of Participation; OIG Advisory Opinion 98-14

business_glossary_term: Office of Inspector General (OIG) Exclusion List Checked Flag
description: Indicates whether the OIG (Office of Inspector General) List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE) was checked for this provider as part of credentialing. OIG exclusion screening is required by CMS; employing an excluded provider can result in significant civil monetary penalties.
reference: OIG LEIE; CMS Conditions of Participation; OIG Advisory Opinion 98-14

description: Indicates whether this credential is required for or submitted as part of payer enrollment and network participation applications. True = credential is submitted to payers (e.g., state license, DEA, board certification); False = not required for payer enrollment.
reference: CMS Provider Enrollment 42 CFR Part 424; CAQH ProView Standards; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Indicates whether this credential has been verified directly with the issuing authority (primary source verification). True = verified with issuing authority; False = not yet primary source verified. Required for NCQA and Joint Commission credentialing accreditation.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482

description: Indicates whether this credential is directly relevant to the provider's hospital privileging determination. True = this credential is required or considered in the privileging decision; False = informational only (e.g., a CME activity not tied to a specific privilege).
reference: The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482.12

description: The date on which primary source verification was completed for this credential. NCQA and The Joint Commission require re-verification at defined intervals (typically every 3 years). Drives re-verification scheduling in Symplr.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; URAC Credentialing Standards

description: The method used to conduct primary source verification of this credential (e.g., online query to state medical board database, written confirmation from issuing authority, electronic query via NPDB or OIG). Documents the verification audit trail for accreditation surveys.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; NPDB Data Bank Regulations

description: The date by which the provider must complete recredentialing for this credential to maintain active status. NCQA and The Joint Commission require recredentialing at least every 3 years. Drives workflow queues in Symplr and credentialing management systems.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482

description: The date on which the credential was most recently renewed or recertified by the issuing authority. Tracks the renewal history cycle for compliance reporting and credentialing re-verification workflows.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; State Medical Practice Acts; ABMS MOC Program

description: Description of any restrictions, conditions, or limitations placed on this credential by the issuing authority (e.g., 'Supervised practice only', 'No DEA Schedule II prescribing', 'Probationary license'). Null if no restrictions apply.
reference: State Medical Practice Acts; DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03

business_glossary_term: System for Award Management (SAM) Exclusion Checked Flag
description: Indicates whether the SAM.gov federal exclusions database was checked for this provider. SAM exclusion screening is required for providers participating in federal healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP).
reference: SAM.gov Federal Exclusions; CMS Conditions of Participation; FAR 52.209-6

description: The operational system of record from which this credential record was sourced or last updated (e.g., Symplr Credentialing, Epic, Cerner Millennium, MEDITECH Expanse, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and ETL audit trails in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001

description: The native identifier of this credential record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Symplr credential record ID, Epic provider record ID). Enables traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the source system for reconciliation and audit.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001

description: Granular classification within the credential type. For state licenses: MD, DO, RN, NP, PA, CRNA, PharmD, etc. For DEA registrations: Schedule II, III, IV, V authorization. For board certifications: ABMS, AOA, ABNS, or other recognized board specialty. For CME: Category 1 or Category 2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|RN|NP|PA|CRNA|PharmD|Schedule_II|Schedule_III|Schedule_IV|Schedule_V|ABMS|AOA|ABNS|CME_Cat1|CME_Cat2 — promote to reference product]
reference: AMA CPT; DEA 21 CFR Part 1308; ABMS Board Certification Standards; AMA PRA Category 1

description: The timestamp when this credential record was most recently modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer ETL pipeline and audit trail requirements under HIPAA and HITRUST.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; HITRUST CSF; ISO 27001; SOC 2

description: Indicates the source used to verify this credential. Primary source verification (PSV) is required by NCQA, The Joint Commission, and CMS for credentialing. Secondary source verification uses intermediary databases. Self-reported credentials have not been independently verified.
reference: NCQA Credentialing Standards; The Joint Commission MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each clinical privileging record granted to a clinician at a specific facility. Primary key for this data product.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03 — Medical Staff Privileging Standards

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Clinical privileges are audited for compliance with credentialing standards (TJC MS.06.01.05, CMS 482.12). Compliance teams audit privilege files for documentation of training, case volume, FPPE/OPPE,
Reference to the specific hospital, clinic, or healthcare facility at which the clinical privileges are granted and exercised. Privileges are facility-specific per Joint Commission standards.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Joint Commission and CMS require hospitals to delineate specific CPT-coded procedures each clinician is privileged to perform. Privileging committees use CPT codes to define procedure-level authorizat

Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Surgical and procedural privileges reference ICD-10-PCS procedure codes for delineation of clinical privileges. Medical staff offices use these codes to define procedure-specific authorization scope a

Foreign key linking to radiology.modality. Business justification: Clinical privileges in radiology are modality-specific — a clinician may be privileged for MRI-guided procedures but not fluoroscopy. Linking privileging to modality supports credentialing committees,
Reference to the licensed clinician or healthcare professional to whom clinical privileges are granted. Links to the provider master record.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Reference to the supervising or proctoring physician assigned to observe and evaluate the clinician during the provisional privilege period. Required when is_provisional is True.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.05; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Foreign key linking to facility.service. Business justification: Joint Commission Delineation of Clinical Privileges requires privileges to be tied to specific clinical service lines (e.g., cardiac surgery service). Medical Staff Offices track privilege grants by s

Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Clinical privileges are granted for specific specialties. privileging.specialty_code and specialty_name should reference specialty table as authoritative source.
Calendar date on which the approving medical staff committee formally voted to grant or renew the clinical privileges. Distinct from the effective date, which may be retroactive or future-dated.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; Symplr Credentialing

description: Indicates whether board certification in the relevant specialty is a prerequisite for granting this privilege per the facility's medical staff bylaws. True when board certification is required.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards CR 1; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Actual number of cases or procedures performed by the clinician under this privilege during the current privilege period. Used for ongoing competency assessment and reappointment decisions.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; ACGME Competency Framework

description: Timestamp when this privileging record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA Security Rule audit control requirements.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312(b); ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF

description: Version identifier of the privilege delineation form used to define the scope of this privilege grant. Tracks which version of the approved privilege form was in effect at the time of granting, supporting audit and accreditation review.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; Symplr Credentialing

description: Calendar date on which the clinical privilege grant becomes active and the clinician is authorized to perform the privileged procedures at the facility. Binding start date per medical staff bylaws.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

business_glossary_term: Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act (EMTALA) Coverage Indicator
description: Indicates whether this privilege covers emergency on-call obligations under EMTALA (Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act). True when the clinician is authorized and obligated to respond to emergency department on-call requests under this privilege.
reference: EMTALA 42 CFR §489.24; CMS Conditions of Participation; OIG EMTALA Compliance

description: Calendar date on which the clinical privilege grant expires and must be renewed through the reappointment process. Typically aligned with the medical staff reappointment cycle (commonly every 2 years per TJC standards).
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

business_glossary_term: Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE) Completion Date
description: Calendar date on which the Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE) was completed and findings were submitted to the medical staff committee. Null when FPPE is not required or not yet completed.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.08.01.03 — Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE)

business_glossary_term: Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE) Required Indicator
description: Indicates whether a Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE) is required for this privilege grant. FPPE is mandatory for all new privileges and triggered by performance concerns per TJC standards. True when FPPE is required.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.08.01.03 — Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE)

description: Indicates whether the privilege is granted on a provisional basis, requiring supervision or proctoring during an initial evaluation period before full privileges are confirmed. True when provisional; False when full privileges are granted.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.05; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Indicates whether the clinician's malpractice/professional liability insurance coverage has been verified as current and meeting the facility's minimum coverage requirements at the time of privilege grant. True when verified.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards CR 1; State Departments of Health — Facility Licensing

business_glossary_term: National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) Report Date
description: Calendar date on which the adverse action was reported to the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB). Must occur within 15 days of the adverse action per HCQIA requirements. Null when no NPDB report is required.
reference: Health Care Quality Improvement Act (HCQIA) 42 USC §11101; NPDB Guidebook

business_glossary_term: National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) Report Required Indicator
description: Indicates whether an adverse action report to the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) is required for this privilege action (e.g., suspension >30 days, revocation). True triggers mandatory NPDB reporting workflow.
reference: Health Care Quality Improvement Act (HCQIA) 42 USC §11101; NPDB Guidebook; HHS OIG

business_glossary_term: Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluation (OPPE) Last Review Date
description: Calendar date of the most recent Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluation (OPPE) review for this privilege. OPPE must occur at regular intervals (at least every 12 months) per TJC standards to support reappointment.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.08.01.01 — Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluation (OPPE)

description: Numeric score or rating assigned during the most recent peer review or focused professional practice evaluation (FPPE) or ongoing professional practice evaluation (OPPE) for this privilege. Used to support reappointment decisions. Sensitive business-confidential data.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.08.01.01 (OPPE); MS.08.01.03 (FPPE); NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Broad classification of the privilege type granted. Clinical covers general patient care; surgical covers operative procedures; procedural covers invasive non-surgical acts; diagnostic covers imaging/interpretation; prescriptive covers medication authority; administrative covers leadership roles.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Detailed narrative description of the scope and limitations of the clinical privilege, including any conditions, supervision requirements, or volume thresholds specified by the medical staff committee.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Human-readable name or title of the clinical privilege granted (e.g., 'Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy', 'Cardiac Catheterization', 'Endotracheal Intubation'). Sourced from the facility's approved privilege delineation form.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; AMA CPT Coding Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the medical staff office to uniquely reference this privileging grant. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and credentialing system lookups (e.g., Symplr privilege record number).
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; Symplr Credentialing

description: Current lifecycle state of the clinical privilege grant. Active indicates full authorization; provisional indicates supervised practice during initial period; suspended indicates temporary removal pending investigation; revoked indicates permanent removal; expired indicates lapsed without renewal; pending indicates awaiting committee approval.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22; EMTALA 42 CFR §489.24

description: Specific classification of the privilege grant. Core privileges are standard for the specialty; special privileges require additional training; temporary privileges are time-limited; telemedicine covers remote care delivery; locum tenens covers temporary coverage providers; disaster privileges are emergency-activated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: core|special|temporary|telemedicine|locum_tenens|disaster|provisional — promote to reference product]
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.05; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Calendar date on which the provisional privilege period ends and the clinician transitions to full privileges, contingent on satisfactory proctoring evaluation. Null when privilege is not provisional.
Number of years in the medical staff reappointment cycle for this privilege (typically 2 years per TJC standards). Determines the frequency of privilege renewal and competency review.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.22

description: Calendar date on which the clinician submitted the privilege request or reappointment application to the medical staff office. Used to track processing turnaround time and compliance with credentialing timelines.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards CR 1

description: Minimum number of cases or procedures the clinician must perform within the privilege period to demonstrate competency and maintain the privilege. Set by the medical staff committee based on specialty standards.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; ACGME Competency Framework

description: Calendar date on which the clinical privilege was permanently revoked, if applicable. Null when privilege has not been revoked. Triggers mandatory NPDB adverse action report within 15 days.
reference: Health Care Quality Improvement Act (HCQIA) 42 USC §11101; NPDB Guidebook; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Narrative description of the reason for permanent privilege revocation, such as gross negligence, criminal conviction, license revocation, or sustained peer review findings. Sensitive business-confidential information required for NPDB adverse action reporting.
reference: Health Care Quality Improvement Act (HCQIA) 42 USC §11101; NPDB Guidebook; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; OIG Fraud and Abuse

description: Native identifier of this privileging record in the originating operational system (e.g., Symplr privilege record ID, Epic credentialing record ID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL traceability.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; HITRUST CSF; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this privileging record originated (e.g., SYMPLR for Symplr Credentialing, EPIC for Epic OpTime/Cadence, CERNER for Cerner Millennium, MEDITECH for MEDITECH Expanse, MANUAL for paper-based entry).
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; HITRUST CSF

description: Calendar date on which the clinical privilege was suspended, if applicable. Null when privilege has never been suspended. Required for adverse action reporting to the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB).
reference: Health Care Quality Improvement Act (HCQIA) 42 USC §11101; NPDB Guidebook; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Narrative description of the reason for privilege suspension, such as patient safety concern, peer review finding, license lapse, or investigation pending. Sensitive business-confidential information used for adverse action documentation and NPDB reporting.
reference: Health Care Quality Improvement Act (HCQIA) 42 USC §11101; NPDB Guidebook; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; OIG Fraud and Abuse

description: Indicates whether this privilege extends to telemedicine or telehealth delivery of the authorized services. True when the clinician is authorized to perform the privileged activity via remote/virtual care modalities.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; CMS Telemedicine Conditions of Participation 42 CFR §482.12(a)(8); State Telehealth Laws

description: Indicates whether the clinician has satisfied all required training, education, or competency documentation prerequisites for this privilege as defined on the privilege delineation form. True when all training requirements are verified.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; ACGME Competency Framework; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Timestamp when this privileging record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing for the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312(b); ISO 27001; HITRUST CSF; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

business_glossary_term: Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine Clinical Terms (SNOMED CT) Concept ID
description: The SNOMED CT concept identifier corresponding to this specialty for clinical terminology interoperability and HL7 FHIR resource alignment. Supports HIE data exchange and FHIR PractitionerRole resource population.
reference: SNOMED International SNOMED CT; HL7 FHIR R4 PractitionerRole Resource; ONC Interoperability Standards

business_glossary_term: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) Board Name
description: The specific American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) member board responsible for certifying physicians in this specialty (e.g., 'American Board of Internal Medicine', 'American Board of Pediatrics'). Used in credentialing verification and Symplr primary source verification workflows.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); AOA Bureau of Osteopathic Specialists; Symplr Credentialing

business_glossary_term: Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education (ACGME) Program Code
description: The ACGME program specialty code used to identify accredited residency and fellowship training programs in this specialty. Used for graduate medical education (GME) tracking, resident credentialing, and workforce planning in Workday HCM.
reference: Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education (ACGME); CMS GME Reimbursement Rules 42 CFR Part 413

description: Name of the recognized medical board or certifying organization that issues board certification for this specialty (e.g., 'American Board of Internal Medicine (ABIM)', 'American Board of Surgery (ABS)', 'American Board of Psychiatry and Neurology (ABPN)'). Used in credentialing workflows and provider directory publishing.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); American Osteopathic Association (AOA) Bureau of Osteopathic Specialists; Symplr Credentialing

description: Indicates whether board certification is required for a provider to be credentialed and privileged in this specialty at the organization. When true, credentialing workflows in Symplr enforce board certification verification before granting privileges.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards; Symplr Credentialing

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Enrollment Specialty Type
description: The CMS-defined specialty type label used on provider enrollment applications (CMS-855 series forms) for Medicare and Medicaid enrollment. Distinct from the two-digit CMS specialty code; represents the descriptive label used in enrollment workflows.
reference: CMS Provider Enrollment Form CMS-855I/CMS-855B; 42 CFR Part 424; CMS Provider Enrollment Chain and Ownership System (PECOS)

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Specialty Code
description: The two-digit CMS specialty code used in Medicare and Medicaid claims processing and provider enrollment. Derived from the CMS crosswalk to NUCC taxonomy codes. Required for CMS-1500 and UB-04 claim form submission and CMS provider enrollment taxonomy reporting.
reference: CMS Provider Specialty Codes; CMS-1500 Claim Form; UB-04 Uniform Billing Form; 42 CFR Part 424

description: The timestamp when this specialty record was first created in the enterprise reference catalog. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312(b) Audit Controls; ISO 27001 A.12.4; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

business_glossary_term: Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) Registration Required Flag
description: Indicates whether providers in this specialty are required to hold an active DEA registration number for controlled substance prescribing authority. Relevant for specialties such as pain management, psychiatry, and anesthesiology. Used in payer enrollment and credentialing validation.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act 21 CFR Part 1301; CMS Provider Enrollment; Symplr Credentialing

description: Numeric sequence controlling the display order of specialties in provider directory user interfaces, credentialing dropdown menus, and referral routing selection screens. Lower values appear first in sorted lists.
reference: Enterprise Provider Directory UI Standards; NCQA Provider Directory Requirements

description: The date on which this specialty code and its associated NUCC taxonomy classification became effective and valid for use in provider credentialing, enrollment, and claims submission. Supports temporal validity tracking for regulatory compliance.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set Versioning; CMS Provider Enrollment; 45 CFR Part 162

description: The date on which this specialty code and taxonomy classification was retired, superseded, or became invalid. Null indicates the specialty is currently active. Used to enforce temporal validity in claims submission and provider enrollment workflows.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set Versioning; CMS Provider Enrollment; 45 CFR Part 162

business_glossary_term: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) Practitioner Role Code
description: The HL7 FHIR R4 PractitionerRole.code value corresponding to this specialty for FHIR-compliant API responses and HIE data exchange. Enables alignment with FHIR-based provider directory publishing and ONC interoperability requirements.
reference: HL7 FHIR R4 PractitionerRole Resource; ONC 21st Century Cures Act Interoperability Rule; 45 CFR Part 170

business_glossary_term: Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set (HEDIS) Measure Set
description: Identifies the applicable HEDIS measure set(s) associated with this specialty for quality measurement and NCQA reporting purposes (e.g., 'Preventive Care', 'Chronic Conditions', 'Behavioral Health'). Used in Epic Healthy Planet and population health analytics.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS Star Ratings Program

description: Indicates whether providers in this specialty are subject to hospital privileging and credentialing requirements for inpatient or procedural care delivery. Drives privileging workflow routing in Symplr and The Joint Commission compliance tracking.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.01; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR Part 482.22; Symplr Credentialing

business_glossary_term: Merit-based Incentive Payment System (MIPS) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether providers in this specialty are subject to Merit-based Incentive Payment System (MIPS) reporting requirements under MACRA. Used for quality reporting program enrollment and CMS value-based payment calculations.
reference: CMS MIPS Program; MACRA 42 CFR Part 414 Subpart O; CMS Quality Payment Program

description: Classification of the specialty for CMS and state network adequacy standards, determining minimum provider-to-enrollee ratios and geographic access requirements. Used in network management, payer contracting, and CMS network adequacy reporting.
reference: CMS Network Adequacy Standards 42 CFR Part 438; NCQA Network Adequacy Standards; State Department of Health Network Adequacy Rules

business_glossary_term: National Provider Identifier (NPI) Taxonomy Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this specialty/taxonomy code is eligible to be declared as a taxonomy code on an NPI application or update with the National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES). Ensures only valid NUCC taxonomy codes are submitted during NPI registry alignment.
reference: CMS NPPES NPI Registry; 45 CFR Part 162.408; NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set

description: The second level of the NUCC taxonomy hierarchy representing the provider classification within the provider type (e.g., 'Family Medicine', 'Internal Medicine', 'Psychiatry & Neurology'). Used for payer enrollment specialty validation and network management.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; CMS Provider Enrollment

business_glossary_term: National Uniform Claim Committee (NUCC) Provider Type
description: The broadest level of the NUCC taxonomy hierarchy representing the provider type category (e.g., 'Allopathic & Osteopathic Physicians', 'Nursing Service Providers', 'Behavioral Health & Social Service Providers'). First level of the three-level NUCC classification hierarchy.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; CMS Provider Enrollment Form CMS-855

description: The third and most granular level of the NUCC taxonomy hierarchy representing the specific area of specialization within the classification (e.g., 'Adolescent Medicine', 'Geriatric Medicine', 'Sports Medicine'). May be null for specialties without a defined subspecialization.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; CMS Provider Enrollment

business_glossary_term: National Uniform Claim Committee (NUCC) Taxonomy Code
description: The 10-character alphanumeric NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy code that uniquely classifies the provider type, classification, and specialization. Required for NPI registry alignment, claims submission, and CMS provider enrollment taxonomy reporting. This product is the SSOT for all NUCC taxonomy codes in the provider domain.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set v23.1; CMS NPI Registry; 45 CFR Part 162

description: Indicates whether this specialty is eligible for payer enrollment and credentialing with commercial and government payers. Specialties marked false may be internal classifications not recognized by external payers for enrollment purposes.
reference: CMS Provider Enrollment Form CMS-855; NCQA Credentialing Standards; Symplr Provider Enrollment

business_glossary_term: Provider Enrollment Chain and Ownership System (PECOS) Specialty Code
description: The specialty code used within the CMS Provider Enrollment Chain and Ownership System (PECOS) for Medicare provider enrollment and revalidation. Required for CMS enrollment submissions and RAC audit compliance.
reference: CMS PECOS System; CMS Provider Enrollment Form CMS-855; OIG Exclusion Database

description: Indicates whether providers in this specialty hold prescribing authority for medications under applicable state and federal law. Used in Epic Willow pharmacy integration, MAR workflows, and CPOE order routing to validate prescriber eligibility.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act; State Medical Practice Acts; Epic Willow Pharmacy; CMS Conditions of Participation

description: Indicates whether this specialty qualifies for Primary Care Physician (PCP) designation in managed care networks including HMO, ACO, and PCMH models. Drives PCP assignment logic in Salesforce Health Cloud and Epic Healthy Planet population health workflows.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS ACO Program; PCMH Recognition Program

description: Indicates whether services rendered by providers in this specialty typically require prior authorization from payers before service delivery. Used in Epic order management (CPOE) and revenue cycle pre-authorization workflows.
reference: CMS Prior Authorization Rules; NCQA Utilization Management Standards; Epic Resolute Revenue Cycle

description: Indicates whether a formal referral is required to access providers in this specialty under standard managed care plan rules (HMO, POS). Drives referral authorization workflows in Epic Cadence and Salesforce Health Cloud referral management.
reference: NCQA Utilization Management Standards; CMS Managed Care Rules 42 CFR Part 438

description: The standard physician work Relative Value Unit (RVU) component associated with the specialty, used as a baseline for productivity benchmarking and compensation modeling. Sourced from CMS Physician Fee Schedule. Used in Workday HCM compensation planning and RCM analytics.
reference: CMS Physician Fee Schedule; AMA RVU Update Committee (RUC); MGMA Physician Compensation Survey

description: Abbreviated description of the specialty suitable for display in constrained UI contexts such as mobile provider directories, claim form dropdowns, and scheduling system specialty selectors. Maximum 100 characters.

description: Identifies the authoritative source system from which this specialty record was ingested or maintained (e.g., NUCC taxonomy feed, CMS specialty crosswalk, Epic EHR, Symplr credentialing). description: Internally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the specialty within the enterprise provider directory and credentialing systems. Used as the business key for specialty lookups across Epic, Cerner, and Symplr integrations.

description: Detailed narrative description of the specialty including scope of practice, patient population served, and types of conditions or procedures managed. Used in provider directory publishing, patient-facing portals, and referral routing guidance.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; AMA Specialty Definitions; NCQA Provider Directory Requirements

description: Full human-readable name of the medical or clinical specialty (e.g., 'Internal Medicine', 'Orthopedic Surgery', 'Pediatric Cardiology'). Used in provider directories, credentialing workflows, and payer enrollment forms.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; AMA Specialty Definitions

description: Current lifecycle status of the specialty record in the enterprise reference catalog. Active records are available for provider credentialing, enrollment, and claims submission. Deprecated records are retained for historical reference only.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Standards; NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set Versioning

description: Classification of the specialty as primary care, specialist, subspecialist, hospitalist, advanced practice, or ancillary. Used for referral routing logic, network adequacy analysis, and PCP designation in HMO and ACO network management.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS ACO Program Requirements; NCQA Provider Directory Requirements

description: Indicates whether this specialty is classified as a surgical specialty, requiring operating room (OR) scheduling, surgical credentialing, and OpTime/SurgiNet integration for case scheduling and documentation.
reference: AMA Specialty Definitions; Epic OpTime; Cerner SurgiNet; The Joint Commission Surgical Standards

description: Indicates whether providers in this specialty are eligible to deliver services via telehealth modalities under applicable CMS and state regulations. Used for network directory publishing, referral routing, and telehealth program management.
reference: CMS Telehealth Services; 42 CFR Part 410.78; State Telehealth Parity Laws

description: The timestamp when this specialty record was last modified in the enterprise reference catalog. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312(b) Audit Controls; ISO 27001 A.12.4; Enterprise Data Governance Standards

description: The NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set version number in which this specialty code was introduced or last updated (e.g., '23.1', '22.0'). Enables version-specific taxonomy lookups for historical claims reprocessing and audit purposes.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set Versioning; CMS NPI Registry

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each board certification record in the system. Primary key for the board_certification data product within the provider domain.
Reference to the licensed healthcare professional (physician, nurse practitioner, or allied health professional) who holds this board certification. Links to the provider master record.
reference: Symplr Credentialing; CMS National Provider Identifier (NPI) Registry

business_glossary_term: Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare (CAQH) Provider ID
description: The CAQH ProView universal provider identifier associated with the provider for this certification record. CAQH ProView is the industry-standard credentialing data repository used by payers and health plans for provider enrollment and re-credentialing. Enables direct linkage to the provider's CAQH profile.
reference: CAQH ProView Credentialing Data Standards; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: The unique alphanumeric certificate number issued by the certifying board to the provider upon successful completion of board examination and certification requirements. Used as the externally-known business identifier for this certification record in credentialing and payer enrollment workflows.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); American Osteopathic Association (AOA) Bureau of Osteopathic Specialists

description: Current lifecycle status of the board certification. Active = valid and in good standing; Expired = past expiration date without renewal; Revoked = rescinded by certifying board due to disciplinary action or fraud; Suspended = temporarily inactive; Pending = examination passed but certificate not yet issued; Lapsed = MOC requirements not met within required window.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); The Joint Commission (TJC) Credentialing Standards MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards

description: Indicates whether this certification record represents an initial board certification, a recertification cycle, an added qualification (subspecialty), or a Maintenance of Certification (MOC) milestone. Drives credentialing workflow routing and expiration tracking logic.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) MOC Program; The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03

description: Standardized short code or abbreviation identifying the certifying board (e.g., ABIM for American Board of Internal Medicine, ABS for American Board of Surgery). Used in downstream credentialing systems and payer enrollment data exchanges.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); CAQH ProView Credentialing Data Standards

description: Full name of the recognized specialty certifying board that issued this certification (e.g., American Board of Internal Medicine, American Board of Surgery). Must be an ABMS member board or AOA-recognized certifying board for credentialing acceptance.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); American Osteopathic Association (AOA); The Joint Commission (TJC) Credentialing Standards

description: Classification of the umbrella organization under which the certifying board operates. ABMS = American Board of Medical Specialties (allopathic physicians), AOA = American Osteopathic Association (osteopathic physicians), ANCC = American Nurses Credentialing Center, AANP = American Association of Nurse Practitioners, AAPA = American Academy of Physician Associates.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); American Osteopathic Association (AOA); American Nurses Credentialing Center (ANCC)

description: The date and time when this board certification record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data governance, lineage tracking, and compliance reporting.
reference: HIPAA Administrative Simplification; ISO 27001 Information Security Management; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The date on which the current certification cycle or recertification became effective. For initial certifications this may equal initial_certification_date; for recertifications this reflects the start of the renewed certification period.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); CAQH ProView Credentialing Data Standards

description: The sequential attempt number on which the provider passed the board certification examination. A value of 1 indicates first-attempt pass. Used in quality and credentialing analytics to assess examination performance.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) Examination Policies

description: The date on which the provider successfully passed the board certification examination administered by the certifying board. Distinct from initial_certification_date, which reflects when the certificate was formally issued.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); AOA Bureau of Osteopathic Specialists

description: The date on which the current board certification expires and must be renewed through recertification or Maintenance of Certification (MOC). Null for lifetime certifications issued prior to time-limited certification policies. Critical for credentialing expiration alerts and payer enrollment compliance.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards CR 1

description: The calendar date on which the provider first achieved board certification in this specialty from this certifying board. Represents the effective start of the agreement/credential. Used to calculate years of board certification and tenure in credentialing analytics.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); The Joint Commission (TJC) MS.06.01.03; NCQA Credentialing Standards CR 1

description: Indicates whether active hospital privileges in this specialty are required to be maintained in conjunction with this board certification per the organization's medical staff bylaws or credentialing policies.
reference: The Joint Commission (TJC) Medical Staff Standards MS.06.01.03; CMS Conditions of Participation 42 CFR 482.22

description: Indicates whether this board certification was issued as a lifetime (non-time-limited) certificate, typically granted to physicians who achieved certification prior to the introduction of time-limited recertification requirements by ABMS member boards. When True, expiration_date is expected to be null.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) Time-Limited Certification Policy

description: Indicates whether this board certification represents the provider's primary specialty designation. A provider may hold multiple board certifications; this flag identifies the principal specialty used for provider directory listings, referral routing, and quality reporting.
reference: NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set; CMS Provider Enrollment Chain and Ownership System (PECOS)

description: The deadline by which the provider must complete all required Maintenance of Certification (MOC) activities to maintain certification in good standing. Used for proactive credentialing alerts and compliance tracking.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) MOC Program; The Joint Commission (TJC) Credentialing Standards

description: The date on which the provider enrolled in the Maintenance of Certification (MOC) program associated with this board certification. Used to track MOC program participation timelines and compliance windows.
reference: American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) MOC Program

description: Name of the specific Maintenance of Certification (MOC) program the provider is enrolled in for this certification (e.g., ABIM MOC, ABS Continuous Certification). Different boards operate distinct MOC programs with varying requirements.

description: Indicates whether the DEA registration authorizes the provider to prescribe Schedule III controlled substances (e.g., anabolic steroids, buprenorphine combinations). Schedule III substances have moderate to low physical dependence potential.

description: Indicates whether the DEA registration authorizes the provider to prescribe Schedule IIN non-narcotic controlled substances (e.g., stimulants such as methylphenidate, Adderall). Distinct from Schedule IIN narcotic authorizations for pharmacy dispensing and order validation purposes.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1308.12; Controlled Substances Act

description: Indicates whether the DEA registration authorizes the provider to prescribe Schedule IV controlled substances (e.g., benzodiazepines, tramadol). Schedule IV substances have lower abuse potential relative to Schedule III.

reference: The Joint Commission Survey Types, DNV GL Survey Standards

description: Unique identifier for the exclusion screening record.

Foreign key linking to provider.clinician. Business justification: OIG/SAM exclusion screening is a mandatory monthly credentialing compliance process. Screening records must be directly linked to the clinician entity for credentialing verification, re-credentialing

Foreign key linking to provider.group. Business justification: Provider groups (medical groups, IPAs) are subject to OIG/SAM exclusion screening as billing entities. CMS requires screening of all entities submitting claims. Compliance teams screen provider groups

Foreign key linking to insurance.health_plan. Business justification: Medicare Advantage and Medicaid managed care contracts mandate OIG/SAM exclusion screening as a plan-specific contractual requirement. Screening results must be tracked per health plan to demonstrate

Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: OIG/SAM exclusion screening applies to organizational entities (hospitals, clinics) as well as individuals. CMS requires monthly screening of all providers including org_providers. Compliance teams mu

Reference to the compliance program under which this screening was performed.

Foreign key linking to reference.npi_registry. Business justification: Exclusion screening validates NPIs against the national registry to ensure screened providers are correctly identified (name, address, taxonomy) before checking OIG/SAM exclusion databases for complia

Foreign key linking to scheduling.schedulable_resource. Business justification: Resources screened against OIG exclusion lists before scheduling privileges granted. Federal requirement preventing excluded individuals from providing federally-funded healthcare services.

Foreign key linking to insurance.payer. Business justification: Healthcare organizations must screen payers and health plans for OIG/SAM exclusions to ensure they are not contracting with excluded entities. This is a regulatory requirement for federal program part

Comprehensive audit trail documenting all actions, reviews, and decisions related to this screening record.
Timestamp when the exclusion screening record was first created in the system.
Government agency or authority that imposed the exclusion (e.g., OIG, GSA, State Medicaid).
Date when the exclusion was imposed by the regulatory authority.
Type or category of exclusion found (e.g., mandatory, permissive, conviction-related, license revocation).
Two-letter state code where a waiver from the exclusion may apply, if applicable.
Confidence level of the match found during screening (high, medium, or low) based on data quality and matching criteria.
Detailed information about the exclusion match found, including name matched, identifier matched, and exclusion reason.
Boolean indicator of whether a match was found on any exclusion list during screening.
Timestamp when the exclusion screening record was last modified or updated.
Scheduled date for the next exclusion screening check for this individual or entity.
Date when notification about the screening result was sent to relevant stakeholders.
Boolean indicator of whether notification was sent to relevant stakeholders about the screening result.
Date when the excluded individual or entity is eligible for reinstatement to federal healthcare programs.
Action taken in response to the screening result (e.g., employment terminated, contract cancelled, credentialing denied, further investigation required).
Date when the screening result was resolved and final action was taken.
Free-text notes documenting the resolution process, investigation findings, and rationale for action taken.
Current status of the resolution process for the screening result (pending review, cleared, confirmed exclusion, action taken, or escalated).
Risk level assigned to this screening result based on entity type, role, and match findings.
Type of entity being screened for exclusion status (employee, contractor, vendor, medical staff, volunteer, or board member).
Social Security Number of the individual being screened, used for matching against exclusion lists.
Two-letter state code where the screened individual or business is located or licensed.
Date when the exclusion screening check was performed.
Number of months between required exclusion screening checks for this entity type.
Method used to perform the exclusion screening (automated system, manual search, or third-party vendor service).
Outcome of the exclusion screening check indicating whether the entity is clear, a match was found, result is inconclusive, or an error occurred.
Source database used for the exclusion screening check (OIG LEIE, SAM.gov, state Medicaid exclusion list, or combined search).

Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Compliance training modules focus on specific high-risk or frequently misused diagnosis codes (sepsis, MI, diabetes with complications), requiring direct code references for targeted coder education.

Number of days past the due date after which non-completion triggers an escalation to management. Used for compliance enforcement.
